Title: [bug] runOrphanAttachmentSweep silently skips files with multiple orphaned attachments, deletes R2 object before DB guard
Description:
'<!-- agent-fp: 18063f5fd318 -->

## Trigger Scenario

1. An instructor uploads a video and the system creates `File F` + `Attachment A1` (linked via `fileId`). The attachment is connected to a `CourseLessonAsset`.
2. The instructor removes the lesson asset. `A1` loses all scope relations — it is now an orphan candidate.
3. A second `Attachment A2` is created pointing to the same `File F` (e.g., the same file is attached to a second lesson/module, then that scope is also deleted).
4. Both `A1` and `A2` are now orphaned. Both are older than the 24-hour threshold.
5. The cron fires `POST /api/v1/internal/storage-janitor/orphan-sweep`.
6. `findMany` returns both `A1` and `A2` as orphan candidates.

**Processing A1:**

```
deleteMany WHERE file.id = F.fileId
  AND attachments.every { id = A1.id AND scope = none }
```

`A2` does not satisfy `id = A1.id` → Prisma's `every` returns false → `deleteMany` matches 0 rows → skip.

**Processing A2:** symmetric failure — `A1` is still present, fails `id = A2.id` → skip.

Result: neither attachment is ever cleaned up. The sweep runs every 6 hours forever and never converges.

**Secondary vector (R2 / DB divergence):** `deletePublicAsset` (R2 HTTP DELETE) is called *before* the atomic DB check. When the DB guard fails (as above), the R2 object has already been deleted, but the `File` and `Attachment` rows remain. On subsequent sweeps, R2 deletes a non-existent object (no-op) while the DB rows accumulate — permanent state divergence between R2 and the database.

## Root Cause

`storage-janitor.sweeps.ts` — `runOrphanAttachmentSweep`:

```typescript
attachments: {
  every: {
    id: orphan.id,        // ← incorrect: fails when sibling attachments exist
    courseAssets: {none: {}},
    // ...
  },
},
```

Prisma's `every` translates to SQL `NOT EXISTS (... WHERE NOT (condition))`. With two attachments `A1` and `A2`, processing `A1` means `A2` does not satisfy `id = A1.id`, so the `NOT (...)` is true for `A2`, the `NOT EXISTS` is false, and the file is not deleted. The intent was "re-verify this specific attachment is still orphaned AND no other attachments exist," but the `id:` filter achieves neither correctly in the multi-attachment case.

## Suggested Fix

Replace the `every` clause with two separate conditions using `AND`:

```typescript
// Guard 1: the specific orphan attachment is still unscoped (race protection)
attachments: {
  some: {
    id: orphan.id,
    courseAssets: {none: {}},
    courseModuleAssets: {none: {}},
    courseLessonAssets: {none: {}},
    courseExamQuestionAssets: {none: {}},
    courseQuizQuestionAssets: {none: {}},
  },
},
// Guard 2: no other attachments exist on this file
AND: [
  {
    attachments: {
      none: { id: { not: orphan.id } },
    },
  },
],
```

Also consider moving `deletePublicAsset` to *after* the DB atomic check succeeds to prevent R2/DB state divergence on raced/skipped deletes.

## Related Prior Issues

None (first scan — Linear had no prior bug issues).

## Verified by

* `code-reviewer` agent: confirmed, identified secondary R2/DB divergence vector
* `qa-tester` agent: confirmed trigger scenario with concrete DB state
* `db-engineer` agent: confirmed `every` SQL translation, provided corrected query

*Filed by automated bug-finder routine on 2026-05-20T00:00:00Z.*
Creator: loooogix@gmail.com

Title: [bug] runStorageJanitorSweep unbounded findMany in Next.js app process risks OOM-crashing production server
Description:
'<!-- agent-fp: 197d47660102 -->

## Trigger Scenario

1. A bug in the upload handler (or a client retry storm) causes 100 000+ `File` rows to be created with `status: "pending"` and never transitioned to `ready`. These accumulate over hours or days.
2. PR #337 / commit `9a24959c` migrates the pending-file sweep from an isolated BullMQ worker process into the Next.js app server via `POST /api/v1/internal/storage-janitor/sweep` (cron, every 5 min).
3. The cron fires. `runStorageJanitorSweep` calls:

```typescript
const pendingFiles = await prisma.file.findMany({
  where: { status: "pending", createdAt: { lt: cutoff } },
  select: { id: true, key: true },
  // NO take/limit
});
```

4. Prisma buffers all 100 000 rows in the Rust query engine, then deserializes them into a JS array. At \~400 bytes/row (V8 overhead) + Prisma wire buffer, this is **\~80–160 MB** peak for 100K rows.
5. A 1 GB VPS running Next.js already has \~300–400 MB baseline usage. Adding 160 MB from the sweep (more if a second concurrent sweep fires 5 min later with no lock) pushes the process past the V8 heap limit.
6. Node.js OOM-kills the process. The entire Next.js app crashes, dropping all in-flight user requests. Process restarts, cron fires again in 5 minutes, cycle repeats — the app cannot stay up while the backlog exists.

**Concurrency amplifier:** There is no mutex or DB advisory lock on the sweep. If one sweep takes longer than 5 minutes (serial R2 HTTP + DB delete loop: \~150 ms/file × 10K files ≈ 25 minutes), a second sweep starts with its own full unbounded `findMany`, doubling heap consumption.

**Isolation regression:** Previously this ran in `Dockerfile.worker` (an isolated process — an OOM would crash only the worker, which BullMQ restarts automatically). Moving it into the app process converts a contained failure into a complete service outage.

## Root Cause

`storage-janitor.sweeps.ts` — `runStorageJanitorSweep`: the `findMany` has no `take:` limit. `runOrphanAttachmentSweep` (the new sibling function in the same commit) correctly uses `take: ORPHAN_SWEEP_BATCH_SIZE`, but the existing pending sweep was not updated when it was moved into the app process.

## Suggested Fix

**Minimal (1 line):** Add a `take` limit and `orderBy` for deterministic forward progress:

```typescript
const pendingFiles = await prisma.file.findMany({
  where: { status: "pending", createdAt: { lt: cutoff } },
  select: { id: true, key: true },
  take: Number(process.env.STORAGE_JANITOR_PENDING_BATCH_SIZE ?? 200),
  orderBy: { createdAt: "asc" },   // oldest-first ensures forward progress
});
```

**Hardening:** Add a module-level in-process lock (sufficient for single-instance Docker):

```typescript
let sweepRunning = false;
export async function runStorageJanitorSweep(...) {
  if (sweepRunning) return { cleaned: 0 };
  sweepRunning = true;
  try { /* ... */ } finally { sweepRunning = false; }
}
```

**Optional throughput improvement:** Replace the serial per-file R2 delete loop with a batched S3 `deleteObjects` call (up to 1000 keys per request), reducing 200 HTTP calls to 1 and cutting sweep duration from \~30 s to \~1–2 s per batch.

## Related Prior Issues

None (first scan — Linear had no prior bug issues).

## Verified by

* `code-reviewer` agent: confirmed, identified isolation regression
* `qa-tester` agent: confirmed trigger scenario and memory math
* `performance-engineer` agent: quantified OOM threshold (\~75–150K rows on 1 GB VPS), confirmed no concurrency guard

*Filed by automated bug-finder routine on 2026-05-20T00:00:00Z.*
Creator: loooogix@gmail.com

Title: [bug] JsonLd component: JSON.stringify does not escape </script> — stored XSS on public course pages
Description: ' ## Trigger Scenario 1. **Setup**: A creator creates or edits a course with the following title: ``` Advanced React ``` This payload is persisted in the DB. No server-side HTML-entity escaping occurs on ingestion. 2. **Rendering**: On the public course detail page, the app passes the course's JSON-LD structured data — which includes the title — to ``. The component emits: ```html ..."} ``` 3. **HTML parser closes the script early**: The browser's HTML tokenizer scans `` regardless of `type`. It finds `` mid-JSON and terminates the nonce-bearing script element. The injected `` is parsed as a new, unnonce'd script element. 4. **Execution**: The injected script runs on page load — **for every visitor** — with zero user interaction required. The attack is **stored/persistent**. 5. **CSP nonce bypass**: The original `` regardless of `type`, making any user-controlled string that contains `` an injection vector. ## Suggested Fix Sketch Apply unicode-escape substitution after `JSON.stringify`. This produces valid JSON (parsers accept `<` as `<`) while preventing premature script-tag closure: ```tsx const safeJson = JSON.stringify(data) .replace(//g, '\\u003e') .replace(/&/g, '\\u0026'); // in JSX: dangerouslySetInnerHTML={{ __html: safeJson }} ``` Alternatively, use the `serialize-javascript` library, which applies these escapes by default. Add a unit test that renders `' }} />` and asserts the raw HTML does not contain the literal string ``. ## Related prior issues None. New finding introduced in commit `c00a8bfc074c` (2026-05-20). ## Verified by code-reviewer agent (review of `json-ld.tsx`) + QA agent (full trigger scenario and regression path) *Filed by automated bug-finder routine on 2026-05-20T20:00:00Z.*

Title: [security] Unauthenticated /api/v1/internal/debug/* leak credentials + community post IDOR + stored markdown XSS + Tabby webhook fail-open + CRON_SECRET bypass
Description:
'<!-- agent-fp: eb17b849c258 -->

## Source

`~/brain/raw/reviews/incident#1/security-13052026.md` (date 2026-05-13, status=closed, resolution=fixed)

## Category

data exposure · authorization · XSS · authentication · secrets leakage

## Findings (bundled — same incident file aggregated 4 audit reports)

### 1\. Critical — Unauthenticated credential/token disclosure

* **Location:** `apps/experts-app/app/api/v1/internal/debug/accounts/route.ts`
* **Attack path:** anonymous `GET /api/v1/internal/debug/accounts?id=<userUuid>`; `/api` bypasses proxy auth.
* **Impact:** returns full `User` + `Account` rows with `passwordHash`, `accessToken`, `refreshToken`.
* **Fix:** remove debug route from prod; if retained, admin+secret gate and explicit safe `select`.

### 2\. Critical — Community post IDOR

* **Location:** `apps/experts-app/app/api/v1/community/posts/[id]/route.ts`
* **Attack path:** anonymous `PUT` updates `prisma.post.update({where:{id}})` — no `auth()` or ownership check.
* **Impact:** arbitrary post defacement / hide / pin manipulation.
* **Fix:** require session, Zod validation, owner/admin enforcement, admin-only fields gated.

### 3\. High — Unauthenticated internal debug abuse (mailtrap/redis/notifications/env)

* **Locations:** `app/api/v1/internal/debug/{mailtrap,redis,notifications,env}/route.ts`
* **Impact:** SSRF (mailtrap host param), notification/email/Slack fan-out, Redis URL disclosure + pub/sub writes, env config leakage.
* **Fix:** delete/compile out in production, or admin+service-token gate with IP allowlist; never echo secrets.

### 4\. High — Stored XSS via markdown

* **Location:** `apps/experts-app/src/components/markdown/MarkdownPreview.tsx`
* **Attack path:** authenticated UGC (posts/comments/profile) rendered with `skipHtml={false}`, `rehypeSanitize` disabled, `allowElement` only checks tag-name shape.
* **Fix:** `skipHtml=true` or strict `rehype-sanitize` schema; block script/iframe/event attrs/srcdoc/dangerous URL protocols.

### 5\. High — Tabby webhook fails open when `TABBY_WEBHOOK_SECRET` is unset

* **Location:** `src/notifications/channels/webhook/providers/tabby.provider.ts` → `tabby.webhook.handler.ts`
* **Impact:** forged webhook completes/cancels enrollments + event registrations.
* **Fix:** fail closed in production; require signature verification; alert on missing config.

### 6\. High — Client-controlled session update grants roles

* **Location:** `apps/experts-app/src/lib/auth.ts` (JWT callback writes `token.roles = session.user.roles`)
* **Impact:** privilege escalation; many `/api/v1/*` routes trust `session.user.roles?.includes("admin")` without DB verification.
* **Fix:** never accept roles from session update; rehydrate from DB; use `requireAdmin()` + DB-backed permission checks.

### 7\. Medium/High — CRON_SECRET bypass when env unset

* **Location:** `app/api/v1/admin/refunds/cleanup/route.ts` (and intended in `admin/commissions/approve-pending`)
* **Impact:** `Authorization: Bearer undefined` passes when env is missing.
* **Fix:** require non-empty secret before bearer auth; fix commissions POST signature.

## Status

**Closed / fixed** per source frontmatter. Filed retroactively for ledger.
Creator: loooogix@gmail.com

Title: [security] /api/v1/internal/upload allows executable MIME (text/html, text/javascript) to public R2 under attacker-chosen domain/entityId
Description:
'<!-- agent-fp: 23e901d2126d -->

## Source

`~/brain/raw/reviews/incident#2/security-13052026.md` (2026-05-13, status=closed)

## Category

business logic abuse · XSS (asset origin) · authorization

## Finding — Medium: authenticated arbitrary public upload with executable content

* **Locations:** `app/api/v1/internal/upload/route.ts`, `src/lib/storage/commands/upload-public-asset.command.ts`
* **Attacker:** any authenticated user (incl. student/free-tier).
* **Input:** multipart file, declared MIME, `domain`, `entityId`.
* **Path:** `POST /api/v1/internal/upload` checks `auth()` only — no per-domain ownership; accepts attacker-chosen `domain`/`entityId`; allows `text/html` and `text/javascript` for `type=file`; writes to public R2 with that ContentType.
* **Impact:** trusted-asset-host hosting of HTML/JS under arbitrary entity namespaces — storage/bandwidth abuse + phishing/script-execution on asset origin.

## Fix direction

1. Strict per-domain allowlist + per-domain object ownership/creator authorization before upload.
2. Block executable web MIMEs (or force `application/octet-stream` + `Content-Disposition: attachment` + `X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff`).
3. Quota + rate limits (see incident#4 follow-up work).

## Status

**Closed / fixed.** Hardening continued in incident#12 (per-domain source-code allowlist) and incident#4 (rate-limit).
Creator: loooogix@gmail.com

* `events/[id]/thumbnail/route.ts:43` — `hosts: { some: { userId: session.user.id } }`

Non-owner requests return 404.

Title: [security] Upload routes accept oversized files — re-check buffer.byteLength after arrayBuffer()
Description:
'<!-- agent-fp: 07157ef04386 -->

## Source

`~/brain/raw/reviews/incident#6/security-13052026.md` (2026-05-13, status=closed, verified 2026-05-20)

## Category

DoS · data integrity

## Finding

Upload routes trusted `file.size` from the multipart envelope and did not re-verify after materializing the buffer with `arrayBuffer()`, allowing clients to bypass `MAX_FILE_SIZE`.

## Fix (PR #312, commit e30cde8a, branch `fix/security-incident-6-buffer-bytelength-recheck`, merged 2026-05-14)

All three upload routes now:

* re-check `buffer.byteLength > MAX_FILE_SIZE` after `arrayBuffer()`, return 413
* use `buffer.byteLength` (not `file.size`) when writing the `File` row

Refs: `internal/upload/route.ts:410, 445, 495`; `courses/[id]/thumbnail/route.ts:83, 102`; `events/[id]/thumbnail/route.ts:83, 102`.

## Status

**Closed.**

## Category

XSS · open redirect · host-header injection

## Discovery

Surfaced during the incident#10 investigation pass (security-auditor + qa-tester parallel run on 2026-05-17). qa-tester traced an HTML-injection sink in the three share pages back to `getRequestBaseUrl`. Carved out of #10 because: live vulnerability, small fix (4 files + 1 test), bundling would delay close.

## Severity

**High** (escalated from Medium during triage). Unauthenticated, publicly reachable, broad phishing surface.

## Affected

* `/share/event/[id]`, `/share/course/[id]`, `/share/post/[id]`
* `apps/experts-app/src/lib/share/share-utils.ts` → `getRequestBaseUrl`

## Old code

```ts
const forwardedHost = headers.get("x-forwarded-host") || headers.get("x-forwarded-server") || headers.get("host");
if (!forwardedHost) return FALLBACK_BASE_URL;
const proto = headers.get("x-forwarded-proto") || "https";
return `${proto}://${forwardedHost}`;
```

Attacker-controlled `Host`/`X-Forwarded-*` headers landed unchanged in HTML output on share pages.

## Fix (PR #320)

Validate scheme + allowlist host (canonical origin) before composing the base URL.

## Status

**Closed.**

## Source

`~/brain/raw/reviews/incident#15/security-17052026.md` (2026-05-17, status=in-review)

## Category

business logic · authorization · UX

## Discovery

Surfaced while smoke-testing incident#12 Step 3 (per-domain source-code MIME allowlist). User attempted to upload images through three creator-curriculum panels and reported "nothing happens, no sign of anything, no error, response, request, toast."

## Bugs

1. **Lesson-resource picker excluded images and forced** `type=file` — `lesson-resource-panel.tsx`: hidden `<input type="file" accept=".pdf,.zip,.doc,…">` had no `image/*`; Chrome silently filtered images out of picker; `formData.append("type", "file")` was hardcoded. **Fix:** `accept="image/*,video/*,audio/*,.pdf,.zip,…"`; derive `uploadType` from `file.type`.
2. **All three dropzones rejected images via** `remainingImageSlots={0}` — `lesson-resource-panel.tsx` / `course-assets-panel.tsx` / `module-assets-panel.tsx`. `AssetDropzone` split into `images`/`files`; `remainingImageSlots={0}` sliced images to length 0; the `files` filter excluded `image/*`. Net: image drop produced empty callback that all three panels ignored.
3. **Quiz-question image uploads returned 403** — UI sent client-generated UUIDs for unsaved questions; server-side authz rejected unknown IDs. Fix: save question first or accept transient client UUID with quiz-write authz.
4. **Lesson-resource creation failed with** `Unique constraint failed on (lesson_id, role, position)` — position derived from the wrong table.

## Status

**In Review** — PR pending on `fix/lesson-resource-panel-allow-images-20260517`.

## Note on category

Auth checks themselves are correct; bugs are in how the UI invokes them and how the handler derives a uniqueness key. Not a remediation of a current vulnerability.

Title: [security] Paid-content bypass: forge course completion/certificate without payment + quiz access without enrollment + Noon API credentials logged
Description:
## Source

`~/brain/raw/reviews/incident#16/security-15052026.md` (2026-05-15, status=open)

## Category

authorization · business logic abuse · secrets leakage

## Findings

### 1\. High — Paid course completion/certificate forgery without confirmed payment

* **Locations:** `app/api/v1/courses/[id]/progress/route.ts`, `app/api/v1/courses/[id]/modules/[moduleId]/lessons/[lessonId]/watch-progress/route.ts`, `src/lib/courses/learn/helpers/course-progress.ts`, `app/api/v1/user/certificates/route.ts`, `src/lib/courses/enrollments/handlers/course-enroll.handler.ts`
* **Attack path:** authenticated learner starts paid checkout → pending enrollment created **before** payment. Learner posts forged watch segments/progress; endpoints don't require a *completed* enrollment. `updateCourseEnrollmentProgress` updates any enrollment for that user/course and sets `completedAt` at 100%, while certificate issuance only checks `completedAt`, not enrollment status or payment confirmation.
* **Impact:** unpaid users can mint certificates for supported course structures; assessment-gated courses also weakened.
* **Fix:** require completed/paid enrollment (or privileged instructor/admin) on all learner progress/watch/assessment mutations; certificate issuance requires `enrollment.status === "completed"` + server-derived completion.

### 2\. High — Paid quiz content and attempts reachable without course access

* **Locations:** `app/api/v1/quizzes/[id]/start/route.ts`, `src/lib/courses/quizzes/handlers/start-quiz.handler.ts`, `src/lib/courses/catalog/mappers/course.mapper.ts`, `app/api/v1/courses/[id]/modules/route.ts`
* **Attack path:** course/module responses expose quiz IDs to non-enrolled users; any authenticated user can `POST /api/v1/quizzes/[id]/start`; `startQuiz` only checks `isPublished`, then `buildAttemptResponse` returns question text, options, assets.
* **Fix:** central course-access guard enforced before quiz start/resume/submit; `isPublished` is not sufficient for paid courses.

### 3\. High — Noon API credentials logged during checkout

* **Location:** `src/lib/payments/gateways/noon/noon.client.ts` (via `createNoonPaymentIntent` → `noonPost`)
* **Attack path:** any authenticated Noon checkout triggers `console.log(makeNoonAuthHeader())` which writes the merchant `Authorization` header derived from `NOON_BUSINESS_ID`, `NOON_APP_ID`, `NOON_APP_KEY` to logs.
* **Impact:** anyone with log/observability access can recover live Noon merchant API credentials.
* **Fix:** remove log statement, **rotate Noon credentials**, audit retained logs for prior leakage.

## Status

**Open.** Cross-ref: incident#8 closed for the shallower variant; this is the deeper paid-content-leak.

Title: [security] Production/staging secrets committed to git + role revocation does not invalidate JWT sessions
Description:
'<!-- agent-fp: a0b8f55e493a -->

## Source

`~/brain/raw/reviews/incident#17/security-16052026.md` (2026-05-16, status=open)

## Category

secrets leakage · authentication · privilege escalation

## Findings

### 1\. Critical — Production/staging secrets tracked in git

* **Location:** `apps/experts-app/.env.production`, `apps/experts-app/.env.staging`, `apps/experts-app/.env.e2e`, `apps/experts-app/slack.secret`, with session trust in `apps/experts-app/src/lib/auth.ts` and role-gated routes such as `app/api/v1/courses/[id]/approve/route.ts`.
* **Attack path:** anyone with repo/artifact access reads committed secrets. With the exposed auth secret, an attacker who has or creates a normal account can forge a JWT carrying elevated roles; `auth.ts` copies token roles into `session.user.roles`, and multiple API routes trust those roles directly.
* **Impact:** admin-level API actions reachable via forged sessions; exposed DB / Redis / Slack / OAuth / webhook credentials risk direct data + workspace compromise.
* **Remediation:**
  1. **Immediately rotate/revoke** all secrets present in tracked env files and `slack.secret`.
  2. Remove from git history.
  3. Move runtime config to a secret manager.
  4. Enforce secret scanning + pre-commit blocking.

### 2\. Medium — Role revocation does not invalidate existing JWT sessions

* **Location:** `apps/experts-app/src/lib/auth.ts`, `app/api/v1/courses/[id]/approve/route.ts`, `src/lib/courses/catalog/handlers/course-approve.handler.ts`
* **Attack path:** former admin/instructor controls a still-valid session cookie. JWT callback refreshes DB roles only on sign-in/session-update; normal session reads check user status but preserve existing `token.roles` for up to the configured 30-day JWT session lifetime. Routes then pass `session.user.roles?.includes("admin")` into handlers.
* **Impact:** revoked privileged users continue moderation/mutation actions (e.g., approve/reject courses) until their JWT expires or is otherwise invalidated.
* **Fix:** derive authorization for sensitive mutations from fresh DB permissions (`requireAdmin` / `getUserPermissions`), or add token-version / session-revocation checks that invalidate JWTs when roles change.

## Status

**Open — Urgent.**

Title: [security] Password-reset URL poisoning via Origin/Host + account enumeration on forgot-password + unauthenticated /console/health disclosure
Description:
## R3 tracking

R3 fingerprint: `<!-- agent-fp: e20b15a445a8 -->` — keep this marker in the body verbatim.

## Source

`~/brain/raw/reviews/incident#18/security-17052026.md` (2026-05-17, status=open)

## Category

authentication · data exposure · host-header injection

## Findings

### 1\. High — Password-reset token URL poisoning via attacker-controlled origin

* **Locations:** `apps/experts-app/src/lib/utils.ts`, `app/api/auth/forgot-password/route.ts`, `app/api/v1/auth/native/forgot-password/route.ts`
* **Attack path:** unauthenticated caller controls `Origin`, `Referer`, `Host`, or `x-forwarded-proto` plus victim email. `POST /api/auth/forgot-password` creates a `PASSWORD_RESET` token, calls `getBaseUrl(request)`, then emails `resetUrl` built from those headers.
* **Impact:** victim receives a legitimate reset email whose link points to an attacker domain with the reset token in the query; clicking it gives the attacker the token, who then resets the victim's password via `POST /api/auth/reset-password`.
* **Fix:** build all secret-bearing URLs from a configured canonical origin / allowlisted tenant origin; do not trust request `Origin`/`Referer`/`Host` for reset, verify, checkout, or enrollment callback URLs.

### 2\. Medium — Account enumeration on web forgot-password endpoint

* **Location:** `app/api/auth/forgot-password/route.ts`
* **Attack path:** distinct 404 / 409 / 429 / 200 responses for unknown emails, Google-only accounts, non-password accounts, cooldown state, and valid password accounts.
* **Impact:** map valid users and auth-provider type for targeted phishing / credential stuffing.
* **Fix:** use the native route's safer pattern — always return the same generic success response; keep cooldown/provider decisions internal; add IP/email-hash throttling.

### 3\. Medium — Unauthenticated detailed console health disclosure

* **Location:** `app/api/v1/console/health/route.ts`
* **Attack path:** `GET /api/v1/console/health` runs DB and Redis checks and returns environment, uptime, version/image/sha/release IDs, integration configuration status, subsystem map, and dependency error details — no `auth()` / `requireAdmin()`.
* **Impact:** production fingerprinting + dependency readiness useful for targeted follow-on attacks and timing.
* **Fix:** gate with `requireAdmin()` or a dedicated service-health token; keep only a minimal public liveness endpoint if required.

## Status

**Open.**

Title: [security] EXP-68 follow-ups: schema constraint + body-parse ordering + modules-route test
Description:
## R3 tracking

R3 fingerprint: `<!-- agent-fp: 4e1d47a0007e -->` — keep this marker in the body verbatim; R3's filter greps for the `<!-- agent-fp:` prefix and will skip this issue if it's stripped. Wrapped in inline code so Linear's parser doesn't drop it on UPDATE.

## Source

Residual scope from [EXP-68](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-68/security-paid-content-bypass-forge-course-completioncertificate) (paid-content paywall bypass). Closed via PRs [logi-x/experts#381](https://linear.app/experts/review/fixcourses-gate-enrollment-progress-updatemany-on-statuscompleted-607a51d25963) + [logi-x/experts#382](https://linear.app/experts/review/fixsecurity-exp-68-http-boundary-hardening-paid-content-paywall-55b1f70877f5) for the live exploits; this ticket carries forward defense-in-depth + test-coverage gaps surfaced by the round-2 reviewer fan-out before PR [logi-x/experts#382](https://linear.app/experts/review/fixsecurity-exp-68-http-boundary-hardening-paid-content-paywall-55b1f70877f5) was pushed.

Cross-ref: [EXP-74](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-74/security-urgent-rotate-noon-merchant-credentials-audit-log-retention) owns the Noon credential rotation (ops, not code) and is intentionally NOT scoped here.

## Category

defense-in-depth · test coverage · schema integrity

## Findings (R3 should address each)

### 1\. Add DB-level `isFree`/`price` consistency check

**Locations:** `apps/experts-app/prisma/schema.prisma` Course model + a new Prisma migration under `apps/experts-app/prisma/migrations/`.

**Why:** Schema currently has independent `isFree Boolean @default(true)` and `price Decimal @default(0)` columns with no relationship constraint. A row with `isFree: true, price: 99` (schema-legal today) would bypass enrollment enforcement entirely because `assertPaidCourseEnrollment` short-circuits on `course.isFree`. Today exploiting this requires elevated DB write, but defense-in-depth says it should be impossible at the schema layer.

**Fix:** Prisma migration adding a check constraint:

```sql
ALTER TABLE "Course" ADD CONSTRAINT "course_isfree_price_consistency"
CHECK (NOT (is_free = true AND price > 0));
```

Optionally also `CHECK (NOT (is_free = false AND (price IS NULL OR price = 0)))` if the product invariant is "free courses must have isFree=true AND paid courses must have positive price." Confirm with product before adding the second constraint — it might be too strict if free courses with promo-discount paid variants exist.

**Test:** Add a Prisma integration test (or a SQL-level test if the project has one) asserting that inserting an inconsistent row throws the constraint violation.

### 2\. `watch-progress/route.ts` POST body-parse ordering

**Location:** `apps/experts-app/app/api/v1/courses/[id]/modules/[moduleId]/lessons/[lessonId]/watch-progress/route.ts:68`

**Why:** Pre-existing bug — `const body = await request.json()` runs BEFORE the param validation (line 70) and the auth check (line 78). An unauthenticated request with a malformed body throws uncaught instead of returning 401 or 400. Not a paywall bypass — just bad error hygiene that this PR didn't address.

**Fix:** Move `await request.json()` to after the auth + param validation checks, OR wrap the entire POST body in a top-level try/catch matching the GET pattern. Either approach is fine; the second is more consistent with the file's overall shape.

**Test:** Add a route-level test asserting that an unauthenticated POST with `body: "not-json"` returns 401 (not a 500 / unhandled exception).

### 3\. `modules/route.ts` paid-course preview-path quiz-strip test

**Location:** `apps/experts-app/app/api/v1/courses/[id]/modules/route.ts` (the `buildPreviewModules` quiz-strip branch at line \~65) — and a new test file at `apps/experts-app/app/api/v1/courses/[id]/modules/__tests__/route.test.ts`.

**Why:** PR [logi-x/experts#382](https://linear.app/experts/review/fixsecurity-exp-68-http-boundary-hardening-paid-content-paywall-55b1f70877f5) fixed the `isPaidCourse` null-price logic in this file (line 34: `course.price == null || Number(course.price) > 0`) but there's no end-to-end route test asserting that an authenticated, non-enrolled user on a paid course gets ZERO quiz items in the preview response. The guard's null-price logic is unit-tested centrally in `paid-course-enrollment.guard.test.ts`, but the modules-route consumer of that logic is untested.

**Fix:** Add a test asserting:

* Authenticated user with no `courseEnrollment` row
* Course is `{isFree: false, price: 99, publishingStatus: "published"}`
* GET → 200 response contains zero `kind: "quiz"` items in the modules' items arrays

Use the same mocking patterns as the existing `app/api/v1/courses/[id]/progress/__tests__/route.test.ts` (vi.hoisted + vi.mock for auth/prisma).

Add a SECOND test for the null-price case: `{isFree: false, price: null}` → same assertion (no quiz items). This is the regression guard for the fail-safe behavior that PR [logi-x/experts#382](https://linear.app/experts/review/fixsecurity-exp-68-http-boundary-hardening-paid-content-paywall-55b1f70877f5) introduced.

## Hard constraints for R3

* **Do NOT touch any of the code shipped by PR** [logi-x/experts#381](https://linear.app/experts/review/fixcourses-gate-enrollment-progress-updatemany-on-statuscompleted-607a51d25963) **or PR** [logi-x/experts#382](https://linear.app/experts/review/fixsecurity-exp-68-http-boundary-hardening-paid-content-paywall-55b1f70877f5) — `course-progress.ts`, `paid-course-enrollment.guard.ts`, the HTTP-boundary guard wiring on progress/watch-progress/quiz-start routes, the certificates route, course-detail.handler.ts, course.mapper.ts, modules/route.ts logic (line 34 `isPaidCourse` calc), noon.client.ts, noon.gateway.ts. Those are correct.
* **Do NOT scope-creep into CI configs, .gitignore, or unrelated refactors.** Attempt 1 ([logi-x/experts#371](https://linear.app/experts/review/fixsecurity-remove-noon-credential-log-gate-paid-content-behind-3ac4b2abd22d)) was rejected for that.
* **Tests MUST assert actual SQL/WHERE shape via** `mock.calls[i][0]` — not hand-shaped mock return values. Attempt 2 ([logi-x/experts#373](https://linear.app/experts/review/fixsecurity-close-paid-content-bypass-gate-readwrite-sweep-a9cdfb94e83b)) was rejected for that, and the round-2 reviewers re-flagged it before push of [logi-x/experts#382](https://linear.app/experts/review/fixsecurity-exp-68-http-boundary-hardening-paid-content-paywall-55b1f70877f5).
* **Do NOT touch the Noon files.** Credential rotation is tracked in [EXP-74](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-74/security-urgent-rotate-noon-merchant-credentials-audit-log-retention) as an ops ticket; do not re-add log lines, do not add log audit code, do not modify the gateway client at all.

## Out of scope

* Noon credential rotation → see [EXP-74](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-74/security-urgent-rotate-noon-merchant-credentials-audit-log-retention) (ops, not code)
* The original [EXP-68](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-68/security-paid-content-bypass-forge-course-completioncertificate) paywall bypass → already complete (PRs [logi-x/experts#381](https://linear.app/experts/review/fixcourses-gate-enrollment-progress-updatemany-on-statuscompleted-607a51d25963) + [logi-x/experts#382](https://linear.app/experts/review/fixsecurity-exp-68-http-boundary-hardening-paid-content-paywall-55b1f70877f5))

* Progress route POST body-parse precedence change (informational; security-positive)

Title: [security] URGENT: Rotate Noon merchant credentials + audit log retention (EXP-68 follow-up)
Description:
**Ops ticket — NOT for R3.** No code component. Do not add the `bug` label or `agent-fp` marker; this should never be picked up by the automated fix-bot.

## Background

[EXP-68](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-68/security-paid-content-bypass-forge-course-completioncertificate) Finding 3: three `console.log` calls in `src/lib/payments/gateways/noon/noon.client.ts` and `noon.gateway.ts` wrote the merchant `Authorization` header (derived from `NOON_BUSINESS_ID`, `NOON_APP_ID`, `NOON_APP_KEY`) to application logs on every Noon checkout.

The log lines were removed in PR #382. But the historical leakage cannot be unsent — any retained log store (CloudWatch / Cloudflare / Vercel / in-house logger) contains the merchant credentials in plaintext. **The credentials must be treated as compromised.**

## Required steps

1. **Rotate all three credentials** via the Noon merchant console:
   * `NOON_BUSINESS_ID`
   * `NOON_APP_ID`
   * `NOON_APP_KEY`
2. **Update secret stores** for production and staging (`.env`, deployment secrets, ops vault — whichever applies).
3. **Audit log retention** for the leaked Authorization header pattern:
   * Search application logs from the time the `console.log` lines were introduced through merge of PR #382.
   * Document the exposure window and whether any non-trusted parties had log access during that window.
4. **Notify Noon** if their security/T&C policy requires disclosure of a credential-exposure incident.

## When safe to close

* New credentials in production and verified working
* Old credentials confirmed revoked at Noon
* Log audit report attached (even if "no third-party access detected during the exposure window")

Title: [security/infra] Split user uploads to files.experts.com.sa origin (EXP-64 Step 4)
Description:
**Infra/architecture work — NOT for R3.** No `bug` label, no `agent-fp` marker; this is a coordinated DNS + bucket + URL-mapper change that requires human ownership and a careful deploy. Should never be picked up by the automated fix-bot.

## Background

Carry-over from [EXP-64](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-64/security-lesson-resourcemodule-asset-content-disposition-per-domain) (Lesson-resource / module-asset Content-Disposition + per-domain MIME allowlist + RFC 5987 filenames). Steps 1-3 shipped — the explicit `isInlineRenderableMime` allowlist, RFC 5987 filename encoding, per-domain source-code allowlist, and instructor-only ownership filter together form the first line of defense against stored-XSS via uploaded assets. Step 4 (originally tagged "Optional") was deferred and is now broken out into this ticket as a tracked-but-dormant follow-up.

## Current state — one origin, mixed trust

The existing `app.experts.com.sa` ↔ `cdn.experts.com.sa` split already protects session cookies from CDN requests (cookies are host-only on `app.experts.com.sa`) and keeps `cdn.experts.com.sa` out of the app's `script-src` CSP directive.

But **everything served from** `cdn.experts.com.sa` **shares one origin**, mixing:

| Asset class | Trust level | Examples |
| -- | -- | -- |
| Company-owned / app-generated | trusted | logos, design-system images, generated invoices, signed transactional PDFs |
| Instructor-uploaded | untrusted | course thumbnails, lesson HLS video, module assets (PDFs / source-code zips), quiz/exam question images |
| Learner-uploaded | untrusted | profile avatars, cover images, community post media |

The upload route (`apps/experts-app/app/api/v1/internal/upload/route.ts`) handles 10 asset domains today: `courses`, `course-assets`, `lesson-content`, `lesson-assets`, `module-assets`, `quiz-questions`, `quiz`, `exam-questions`, `community`, `draft-courses` — plus separate handlers for `cover-upload.handler.ts` and `avatar-upload.handler.ts`. All write to the same R2 bucket, all served from `cdn.experts.com.sa`.

## Threats Step 4 closes

If the first line of defense (MIME allowlist + Content-Disposition + RFC 5987) ever fails:

1. **Cross-tenant content read** — Malicious SVG/HTML from one instructor reads another instructor's lesson assets, another learner's avatar, or another company's logo via same-origin `fetch()` from the executed script.
2. **Trusted asset compromise** — Untrusted user uploads share an origin with company-owned signed PDFs, transactional invoices, and design assets. A scripted same-origin read can exfiltrate them.
3. **Subdomain takeover blast radius** — If `cdn.experts.com.sa` is ever taken over (DNS misconfig, R2 binding error, dangling CNAME), the entire mixed-trust corpus is exposed.
4. **CSP rigidity** — Tightening `img-src` / `media-src` requires allowlisting `cdn.experts.com.sa`, which inadvertently trusts user-uploaded image MIMEs alongside company assets.

## Proposed scope — Option A (trust split)

Two CDN hostnames, partitioned by trust:

* `cdn.experts.com.sa` — **company-owned trusted assets only**: logos, design-system images, generated invoices, signed PDFs, marketing assets, anything written by app code on behalf of the platform itself.
* `files.experts.com.sa` — **all user uploads**: every asset domain currently handled by `app/api/v1/internal/upload/route.ts`, plus `cover-upload.handler.ts` and `avatar-upload.handler.ts`.

(Rejected: Option B, per-user subdomain hashes á la GitHub's `githubusercontent.com`. Stricter but far more DNS/cert/CDN complexity than the threat justifies for this codebase today.)

## Implementation plan

### 1\. DNS + TLS

* New `files.experts.com.sa` A/AAAA → R2 binding (or Cloudflare Workers + R2 if there's path-rewriting).
* TLS cert via Cloudflare Universal SSL (free) or Let's Encrypt.
* Verify `files.experts.com.sa` resolves and serves a known test object before any code change.

### 2\. R2 bucket strategy

Two options — confirm with whoever owns infra before picking:

* **2a (simpler):** Single bucket, two CDN hostnames pointing at different key prefixes. `cdn.experts.com.sa` → `bucket/trusted/*`, `files.experts.com.sa` → `bucket/user-uploads/*`. Cheap, no migration of trusted assets needed if their prefix is already separable.
* **2b (cleaner):** Two buckets — `experts-trusted` and `experts-user-uploads`. Stronger isolation at the storage layer (no shared bucket-level signing/keys), but requires migration of existing user-uploaded keys.

Pick 2a if existing keys cleanly partition; pick 2b if there's already mixing or if storage-layer access control matters (e.g., different lifecycle rules, different audit logs).

### 3\. URL mapper change

All upload-site URLs are derived from `R2_PUBLIC_URL` (or equivalent env var) in `apps/experts-app/src/lib/r2.ts` and helpers under `src/lib/storage/helpers/`. Introduce a SECOND env var (`R2_USER_UPLOADS_PUBLIC_URL` or similar) and route user-upload commands through it.

Files to touch (write paths only — never add a dual-read fallback per the storage-migrations rule):

* `src/lib/storage/commands/upload-public-asset.command.ts` (cover/avatar)
* `src/lib/storage/commands/upload-media.command.ts` (if it's used for user-uploaded media)
* `app/api/v1/internal/upload/route.ts` (returns URLs for all 10 instructor/learner domains)
* `src/lib/storage/helpers/signed-url.helper.ts` and `signed-media-url.helper.ts` (if any signed URLs hardcode `cdn.experts.com.sa`)
* `src/lib/storage/attachments/attachment-cdn-url.ts`

Anywhere the CDN hostname is rendered into a DTO (course mapper image URLs, lesson asset URLs, quiz question image URLs, attachment URLs) needs to emit the new origin when the source row is a user upload.

### 4\. CSP

Update `proxy.ts` CSP directives:

* `img-src` and `media-src` add `files.experts.com.sa` (was `cdn.experts.com.sa` only)
* Confirm `script-src` allowlists NEITHER hostname
* Confirm `connect-src` and `frame-src` are tightened consistently

### 5\. Existing data — per the storage-migrations rule

Per project convention (`feedback_storage_migrations_manual.md`): implement only the new write path. Old keys staying on `cdn.experts.com.sa` is fine — they were uploaded under the old origin and any deployed pages referencing those URLs continue to work. No dual-read fallback, no backfill script. If anyone wants existing user uploads physically moved to `files.experts.com.sa`, that's an explicit ops task, out of scope here.

### 6\. Tests

* Update `src/lib/storage/commands/__tests__/upload-public-asset.command.test.ts` to assert the returned URL uses the new origin.
* Update `app/api/v1/internal/upload/__tests__/route.test.ts` to assert each of the 10 domains emits URLs on `files.experts.com.sa`.
* Add a CSP integration test asserting `files.experts.com.sa` is in `img-src` / `media-src` but NOT `script-src`.

## When safe to close

* New hostname resolves + serves a test object
* Bucket strategy decided + (if 2b) new bucket provisioned
* Write-path URL routing through new origin
* CSP updated to allowlist the new hostname for `img-src` / `media-src` only
* Tests asserting the new URL shape
* One end-to-end smoke test: upload a new course thumbnail as an instructor, verify it serves from `files.experts.com.sa` and renders in the course detail page

## Out of scope

* Migrating existing user-uploaded keys (separate ops task if anyone wants it)
* Per-user subdomain hashing (Option B — deferred unless threat model changes)
* Step 1-3 hardening (already shipped in [EXP-64](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-64/security-lesson-resourcemodule-asset-content-disposition-per-domain))

## Cross-refs

* [EXP-64](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-64/security-lesson-resourcemodule-asset-content-disposition-per-domain) (parent, Done) — Steps 1-3 of incident#12
* Storage convention: see `feedback_storage_migrations_manual.md` in agent memory — write path only, no dual-read
Creator: loooogix@gmail.com

Title: [security] JWT role-staleness in event thumbnail upload — revoked admin bypasses host check
Description:
'<!-- agent-fp: 50a0efcf0017 -->

## Severity

**Medium**

## Location

`apps/experts-app/app/api/v1/events/[id]/thumbnail/route.ts` — `POST` handler, introduced/modified in PR #378 (2026-05-22)

## Attack path

**Attacker → Input → Reach → Impact**

* **Attacker:** A user whose admin role has been revoked in the database but who holds a still-valid JWT (window up to 30 days, matching the configured JWT session lifetime).
* **Controlled input:** `session.user.roles` — populated at JWT-mint time, never refreshed from the DB within the request lifecycle.
* **Reachable path:**
  1. Attacker presents a valid but stale JWT with `roles: ["admin"]`.
  2. Line `const isAdmin = Boolean(session.user.roles?.includes("admin"))` evaluates to `true`.
  3. The guard `if (!isAdmin && !hostForUser)` short-circuits — the `hostForUser` DB lookup result is ignored entirely.
  4. The route proceeds to accept any image file and write it to R2 + the `attachment` and `file` DB tables, keyed to any caller-supplied `eventId`.
* **Impact:** Revoked admin can upload and overwrite the thumbnail of any event they do not host, for up to 30 days after role revocation. Thumbnail is publicly visible; mass defacement across all events is possible within the staleness window.

## Vulnerable code

```typescript
// apps/experts-app/app/api/v1/events/[id]/thumbnail/route.ts (PR #378)
const isAdmin = Boolean(session.user.roles?.includes("admin")); // ← JWT-derived, stale

if (!isAdmin && !hostForUser) {
  return NextResponse.json({error: "Forbidden"}, {status: 403});
}
// upload proceeds unchecked for isAdmin=true
```

## Recommended remediation

Replace the JWT-derived check with a fresh DB lookup — the same pattern established by [EXP-69](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-69/security-productionstaging-secrets-committed-to-git-role-revocation)'s fix (commit `efb5bd4`) for approve/reject routes:

```typescript
const [eventRow, hostForUser, dbUser] = await Promise.all([
  prisma.event.findUnique({where: {id: eventId}, select: {id: true}}),
  prisma.eventHost.findFirst({where: {eventId, userId: sessionUserId}, select: {role: true}}),
  prisma.user.findUnique({where: {id: sessionUserId}, select: {isAdmin: true}}),
]);

const isAdmin = Boolean(dbUser?.isAdmin);
```

This parallelises the DB lookup so no extra serial round-trip is added.

## Related prior issues

* [EXP-69](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-69/security-productionstaging-secrets-committed-to-git-role-revocation) (`a0b8f55e493a`) — same vulnerability class (JWT role staleness); fix in PR #375 covered only `approve/route.ts` and `reject/route.ts`. PR #378 introduced this route after [EXP-69](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-69/security-productionstaging-secrets-committed-to-git-role-revocation) was closed, so it was not in scope of that fix. This is a new surface, not a missed remediation.

## Verified by

security-auditor (primary) · architecture-reviewer (verifier)

*Filed by automated security routine on 2026-05-22T06:29:09Z.*
Creator: loooogix@gmail.com
Related to: EXP-85, EXP-84, EXP-88, EXP-89, EXP-90, EXP-86, EXP-69

Title: [bug] Validate lessonId UUID in POST /courses/[id]/progress before Prisma — malformed input returns 500 instead of 400
Description:
'<!-- agent-fp: a619815990b0 -->

## Source

Surfaced by the Gatekeeper independent review on PR #392 ([EXP-76](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-76/bug-course-progress-post-lesson-to-course-ownership-check-skipped-for) attempt 2, merged). Non-blocking note in the PASS verdict:

> `lessonId` is not Zod-validated before reaching Prisma — a malformed UUID surfaces as 500 rather than 400/404. Pre-existing across the file and not introduced by this PR.

## Category

input validation · error hygiene · HTTP surface

## Finding

**Location:** `apps/experts-app/app/api/v1/courses/[id]/progress/route.ts` POST handler.

**Current behavior:** The POST body's `lessonId` field is read directly from `await request.json()` and passed to Prisma queries (`courseLesson.findFirst({where: {id: lessonId, ...}})`, `courseLessonProgress.upsert({where: {userId_lessonId: {userId, lessonId}}})`, `updateCourseEnrollmentProgress({lastLessonId: lessonId})`) WITHOUT any UUID validation.

When `lessonId` is a non-UUID string (e.g. `"foo"`, `""`, or any malformed value), Prisma's query throws on the invalid UUID coercion. The route's catch block at the bottom returns a generic 500 — leaking an internal error message — instead of a 400 (bad input) or 404 (lesson not found).

**Why this matters:**

* HTTP-surface hygiene: malformed input should always be 4xx, never 5xx. 5xx pages routes/clients to retry, alerts the on-call, and triggers observability noise — none of which are warranted for a client-side validation failure.
* Error message exposure: the catch block returns `error.message` directly, which for Prisma UUID errors includes internal type names ("Inconsistent column data: Conversion failed: ...") that hint at the schema.
* Defense in depth: the URL `courseId` IS already validated by `routeParamsWithIdSchema.safeParse({id: courseId})` at the top of both GET and POST. The body's `lessonId` should get equivalent validation.

## Fix

**Option 1 (recommended) — Zod schema on the POST body:**

Add a body schema at the top of the file (or in a sibling `commands/` file matching the file's convention):

```ts
const ProgressPostBodySchema = z.object({
  lessonId: z.string().uuid(),
  completed: z.boolean().optional(),
});
```

In the POST handler, after `await request.json()`:

```ts
const parsed = ProgressPostBodySchema.safeParse(body);
if (!parsed.success) {
  return NextResponse.json(
    {error: "Invalid payload", issues: z.treeifyError(parsed.error)},
    {status: 400},
  );
}
const {lessonId, completed} = parsed.data;
```

This matches the same pattern already used in `watch-progress/route.ts` (`LessonVideoWatchUpsertSchema`) — consistent with the codebase.

**Option 2 (lighter):** Just check `routeParamsWithIdSchema.safeParse({id: lessonId})` against the body's lessonId before any DB call. Less consistent with the file conventions but smaller diff.

Prefer Option 1.

## Tests required

Add to `apps/experts-app/app/api/v1/courses/[id]/progress/__tests__/route.test.ts`:

1. POST with `body: {lessonId: "not-a-uuid", completed: true}` → expect 400 with `error: "Invalid payload"` and `issues.lessonId` set.
2. POST with `body: {lessonId: "", completed: true}` → expect 400.
3. POST with `body: {}` (no lessonId at all) → expect 400.
4. POST with `body: {lessonId: <valid uuid>, completed: "not-a-boolean"}` → expect 400 (or treat as valid `true` if `completed` is intentionally permissive).

The 4 tests MUST assert that Prisma was NOT called (`expect(mocks.prisma.courseLesson.findFirst).not.toHaveBeenCalled()`) — that's what proves validation fires BEFORE the DB layer.

## Hard constraints for R3

* **Single file only:** `app/api/v1/courses/[id]/progress/route.ts` + its test file.
* **Do NOT touch** any other route's validation. If `watch-progress/route.ts` or other handlers have the same gap, file a separate ticket — do not scope-creep here.
* **Do NOT modify** the URL `courseId` validation that's already present at the top of both GET and POST.
* **Do NOT modify** the [EXP-68](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-68/security-paid-content-bypass-forge-course-completioncertificate) paid-enrollment guard (`assertPaidCourseEnrollment` call) or the [EXP-76](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-76/bug-course-progress-post-lesson-to-course-ownership-check-skipped-for) lesson-ownership check (`courseLesson.findFirst` outside the `if (isCompleted)` block). Both are correct and should stay in place. Body validation goes BEFORE both of them.
* **Test must assert that Prisma is not called on malformed input** — not a hand-shaped 400 response. Use `mock.calls` to check call count.

## Out of scope

* Other routes with the same gap (file separately if discovered)
* Refactoring the error catch block to redact `error.message` (separate hygiene ticket)
* Changing the response shape of valid POSTs

## Cross-refs

* Merged PR #392 ([EXP-76](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-76/bug-course-progress-post-lesson-to-course-ownership-check-skipped-for) attempt 2) — the gatekeeper review that surfaced this
* [EXP-68](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-68/security-paid-content-bypass-forge-course-completioncertificate) + [EXP-76](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-76/bug-course-progress-post-lesson-to-course-ownership-check-skipped-for) — paid-content paywall work that touched the same file but did NOT introduce this bug

Title: [security] Race-safe storage usage ledger (UserStorageUsage + reservation flow)
Description:
## R3 tracking

R3 fingerprint: `<!-- agent-fp: c3eb6a48c4db -->` — keep this marker in the body verbatim.

**Status: Backlog.** Ready to flip to Todo only AFTER [EXP-72](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-72/security-per-user-storage-quota-on-apiv1internalupload-cost-control) ships (the preflight gate must exist first). When moved to Todo, R3 will pick this up automatically — labels and marker are already pre-staged.

## Source

Follow-up to [EXP-72](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-72/security-per-user-storage-quota-on-apiv1internalupload-cost-control) (per-user storage quota preflight). [EXP-72](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-72/security-per-user-storage-quota-on-apiv1internalupload-cost-control) ships an `aggregate()`-based gate that has a TOCTOU race: two concurrent uploads can both pass the check, both write to R2, and silently overshoot the quota. This issue closes that race with a reservation ledger.

## Category

cost-control · concurrency hardening · storage abuse

## Problem

After [EXP-72](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-72/security-per-user-storage-quota-on-apiv1internalupload-cost-control) ships, `/api/v1/internal/upload` reads `prisma.file.aggregate({_sum: {size: true}})` then writes to R2. Under concurrent uploads from the same user:

1. T0 — request A reads `usedBytes = 900 MB`, quota is 1 GB, incoming 200 MB → passes (900 + 200 ≤ 1024)
2. T0 — request B reads `usedBytes = 900 MB` (A hasn't written yet) → also passes (900 + 200 ≤ 1024)
3. T1 — A and B both upload to R2, both insert `file` rows
4. Final state: `usedBytes = 1300 MB` (300 MB over quota)

Impact: silent quota bypass under concurrency. Bounded by upload rate-limit but still defeats the purpose of the gate.

## Recommended approach

Replace the read-then-write with an atomic conditional UPDATE against a usage ledger table.

### Schema

Add to `apps/experts-app/prisma/schema.prisma`:

```prisma
model UserStorageUsage {
  userId        String   @id
  usedBytes     BigInt   @default(0)
  reservedBytes BigInt   @default(0)
  updatedAt     DateTime @updatedAt

  user User @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)

  @@map("user_storage_usage")
}
```

Add migration `prisma/migrations/<ts>_add_user_storage_usage/migration.sql`. Backfill `usedBytes` from existing `file` rows where `status = 'ready'`.

### Reservation flow in upload route

Replace the [EXP-72](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-72/security-per-user-storage-quota-on-apiv1internalupload-cost-control) `aggregate()` block in `app/api/v1/internal/upload/route.ts` with:

```ts
// 1. Atomic preflight: conditional UPDATE returns 0 rows if quota would be exceeded
const reserved = await prisma.$executeRaw`
  INSERT INTO user_storage_usage (userId, reservedBytes, updatedAt)
  VALUES (${session.user.id}, ${BigInt(incomingBytes)}, NOW())
  ON CONFLICT (userId) DO UPDATE
    SET reservedBytes = user_storage_usage.reservedBytes + ${BigInt(incomingBytes)},
        updatedAt = NOW()
  WHERE user_storage_usage.usedBytes + user_storage_usage.reservedBytes + ${BigInt(incomingBytes)} <= ${BigInt(quotaBytes)}
`;

if (reserved === 0) {
  // Race-safe: someone else's reservation pushed us over before our UPDATE landed
  return NextResponse.json({error: "Storage quota exceeded", quotaBytes}, {status: 413});
}

let uploadResult;
try {
  uploadResult = await r2Client.put(...);
  // 2. Success: promote reservation to used
  await prisma.userStorageUsage.update({
    where: {userId: session.user.id},
    data: {
      usedBytes: {increment: BigInt(incomingBytes)},
      reservedBytes: {decrement: BigInt(incomingBytes)},
    },
  });
} catch (err) {
  // 3. Failure: release reservation
  await prisma.userStorageUsage.update({
    where: {userId: session.user.id},
    data: {reservedBytes: {decrement: BigInt(incomingBytes)}},
  });
  throw err;
}
```

### Reservation TTL cleanup

Add scheduled job (cron / worker) that releases reservations older than 15 minutes. Stale reservations happen when the upload route crashes between reserve and finalize.

```ts
// apps/experts-app/src/workers/storage-reservation-cleanup.ts
await prisma.$executeRaw`
  UPDATE user_storage_usage
  SET reservedBytes = 0, updatedAt = NOW()
  WHERE reservedBytes > 0 AND updatedAt < NOW() - INTERVAL '15 minutes'
`;
```

Wire into existing cron infrastructure (check `apps/experts-app/src/workers/` for the cron pattern in use).

## Test plan

1. **Concurrent uploads** — two parallel POSTs from same user, each 600MB, quota 1GB. Exactly one succeeds, the other 413s.
2. **Sequential under quota** — 5 sequential 100MB uploads, quota 1GB → all succeed, final `usedBytes = 500MB, reservedBytes = 0`.
3. **R2 failure releases reservation** — mock R2 to throw, assert `reservedBytes` returns to prior value.
4. **Stale reservation cleanup** — manually insert `reservedBytes = 500MB, updatedAt = NOW() - 20min`, run cleanup job, assert `reservedBytes = 0`.
5. **Backfill correctness** — seed `file` rows with mixed `status`, run migration, assert `usedBytes` matches `sum(size where status='ready')`.

Use real Prisma + test DB (concurrency test must hit a real connection pool; mocks can't surface the race).

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `UserStorageUsage` model exists with migration
- [ ] Backfill SQL in migration populates `usedBytes` from existing `file` rows
- [ ] Upload route uses atomic `INSERT ... ON CONFLICT ... WHERE` reservation pattern
- [ ] Reservation released on R2 failure
- [ ] Reservation promoted to `usedBytes` on R2 success
- [ ] Scheduled cleanup job exists and releases reservations >15min old
- [ ] All 5 test cases pass (including concurrency test against real DB)
- [ ] No regression in [EXP-72](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-72/security-per-user-storage-quota-on-apiv1internalupload-cost-control) test suite

## Out of scope

* Per-tier quota values (still uniform 1GB until tiering ships)
* Admin dashboards → [EXP-82](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-82/bug-admin-storage-dashboard-over-quota-alerts) — filed 2026-05-22, Backlog
* R2 orphan cleanup → [EXP-81](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-81/bug-stale-pending-file-row-cleanup-r2-orphan-reaper) — filed 2026-05-22, Backlog

## Related

* [EXP-72](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-72/security-per-user-storage-quota-on-apiv1internalupload-cost-control) — preflight gate (must ship first)
* [EXP-81](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-81/bug-stale-pending-file-row-cleanup-r2-orphan-reaper) — stale pending file row cleanup + R2 orphan reaper (sibling, parallelizable)
* [EXP-82](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-82/bug-admin-storage-dashboard-over-quota-alerts) — admin storage dashboard + over-quota alerts (sibling, depends on this ledger landing first)
* [EXP-103](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-103/spinoff-exp-80-migrate-5-remaining-upload-routes-from) — migrate 5 remaining upload routes to reservation pattern (R3-filed spinoff, post-merge)

## Status

**Backlog.** Move to Todo only after [EXP-72](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-72/security-per-user-storage-quota-on-apiv1internalupload-cost-control) is merged.
Creator: loooogix@gmail.com

Title: [bug] Admin storage dashboard + over-quota alerts
Description:
## R3 tracking

R3 fingerprint: `<!-- agent-fp: 04250008207c -->` — keep this marker in the body verbatim.

**Status: Backlog.** Ready to flip to Todo once [EXP-72](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-72/security-per-user-storage-quota-on-apiv1internalupload-cost-control) (preflight gate) ships. Best done AFTER the race-safe ledger lands so the dashboard reads `usedBytes` from `UserStorageUsage` instead of a slow `aggregate()` per request. Marker and labels pre-staged for R3.

## Source

Follow-up to [EXP-72](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-72/security-per-user-storage-quota-on-apiv1internalupload-cost-control). Once quotas are enforced, ops needs visibility into:

* Total storage across all users
* Per-user storage breakdown
* Top consumers (top 20 by `usedBytes`)
* Users above 80% of quota (early warning before they 413)

Without this, the first signal that quotas matter is the R2 billing surprise or angry user tickets when they hit 413.

## Category

ops · observability · admin UX

## Recommended approach

### Read path

Source data: `UserStorageUsage` table (from the race-safe ledger follow-up). Falls back to `prisma.file.aggregate()` if the ledger hasn't shipped yet — keep the route resilient to both.

### Route

Create `apps/experts-app/app/api/v1/console/storage/metrics/route.ts`:

```ts
import {requireAdmin} from "@/lib/auth/require-admin";

export async function GET(req: NextRequest) {
  const admin = await requireAdmin(req);
  if (!admin) return new Response("Forbidden", {status: 403});

  const [total, top20, overThreshold] = await Promise.all([
    // 1. Total bytes across all users
    prisma.userStorageUsage.aggregate({_sum: {usedBytes: true}}),

    // 2. Top 20 consumers
    prisma.userStorageUsage.findMany({
      orderBy: {usedBytes: "desc"},
      take: 20,
      include: {user: {select: {id: true, email: true, name: true, plan: true}}},
    }),

    // 3. Users at >=80% of their tier quota
    prisma.$queryRaw<Array<{userId: string; usedBytes: bigint; quotaBytes: bigint; pct: number}>>`
      SELECT u."userId", u."usedBytes", q.bytes AS "quotaBytes",
             (u."usedBytes"::float / q.bytes) AS pct
      FROM user_storage_usage u
      JOIN users ON users.id = u."userId"
      JOIN LATERAL (SELECT $1::jsonb -> users.plan ->> 'bytes') AS q(bytes) ON true
      WHERE u."usedBytes"::float / q.bytes >= 0.8
      ORDER BY pct DESC
    `,
  ]);

  return NextResponse.json({total, top20, overThreshold});
}
```

(Adjust the over-threshold query to whatever quota-resolution pattern lands with the tier resolver.)

### UI

Create `apps/experts-app/app/console/storage/page.tsx`:

* Header: total bytes / total user count / avg per user
* Table: top 20 consumers (user, plan, usedBytes, % of quota)
* Table: users >=80% quota (highlighted, with "send warning email" action)
* Sparkline: daily totals over last 30 days (requires snapshot job, file separately if useful)

Apply existing console design tokens. RTL parity required (see [`apps/experts-app/.claude/skills/experts-orbit/SKILL.md`](<apps/experts-app/.claude/skills/experts-orbit/SKILL.md>)).

### Alert hook

When the storage-reservation-cleanup job (from the ledger follow-up) runs, also enqueue a check:

```ts
// If any user crosses 90% for the first time, send notification.
const newlyOverThreshold = await prisma.$queryRaw`
  SELECT userId FROM user_storage_usage
  WHERE usedBytes::float / <resolve quota> >= 0.9
    AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM storage_alert WHERE userId = user_storage_usage.userId AND createdAt > NOW() - INTERVAL '7 days')
`;
// For each: insert storage_alert row + send email/in-app notification
```

Add `StorageAlert` model (one row per user per 7-day window) to prevent alert spam.

## Test plan

1. **Total aggregate** — seed 100 users with random `usedBytes` totaling 50GB, route returns `total = 50GB`.
2. **Top 20** — seed 30 users, route returns exactly 20 ordered by `usedBytes desc`.
3. **Over threshold** — seed users at 70%, 80%, 95%, 100%, 110% → returns the latter 4.
4. **Auth** — non-admin GET → 403.
5. **Alert dedup** — trigger threshold cross, assert one `StorageAlert` row + one notification. Trigger again within 7d, assert no new row/notification.
6. **UI snapshot** — Storybook story for the dashboard with seeded fixture.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `/api/v1/console/storage/metrics` route exists, admin-gated, returns total + top20 + overThreshold
- [ ] `/console/storage/page.tsx` renders the dashboard with proper RTL parity
- [ ] `StorageAlert` model + dedup-aware alert trigger
- [ ] Hook into existing notification system (email + in-app)
- [ ] All test cases pass
- [ ] No regression in [EXP-72](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-72/security-per-user-storage-quota-on-apiv1internalupload-cost-control) / ledger / cleanup tests

## Out of scope

* 30-day sparkline (needs snapshot job → file separately if useful)
* Per-user storage breakdown by file type / age (file separately if useful)
* Bulk admin actions (delete user's files, raise their quota) → file separately

Title: [bug] VALIDATE course_isfree_price_consistency constraint after prod data sweep (EXP-73 follow-up)
Description:
## R3 tracking

R3 fingerprint: `<!-- agent-fp: 2b75b1305d16 -->` — keep this marker in the body verbatim.

**Status: Backlog.** Ready to flip to Todo **only after** a production data sweep confirms no `{is_free=true, price>0}` rows exist in the `courses` table. Until then, attempting `VALIDATE CONSTRAINT` will fail on bad rows and leave the constraint in an inconsistent state.

## Source

Follow-up to [EXP-73](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-73/security-exp-68-follow-ups-schema-constraint-body-parse-ordering) / PR [logi-x/experts#398](https://linear.app/experts/review/fixsecurity-exp-73-follow-ups-schema-check-body-parse-order-modules-ee7203796be8) (schema check + body-parse order + modules test).

PR #398 added a CHECK constraint to the `courses` table to enforce DB-level consistency between `is_free` and `price`:

```sql
ALTER TABLE "public"."courses"
  ADD CONSTRAINT "course_isfree_price_consistency"
  CHECK (
    (is_free = true AND (price IS NULL OR price = 0))
    OR (is_free = false AND price IS NOT NULL AND price > 0)
  )
  NOT VALID;
```

It was added with `NOT VALID` so the migration is safe to deploy without scanning the entire table (cheap, non-blocking). New writes are enforced immediately. Existing rows are **not** checked until someone runs `VALIDATE CONSTRAINT`.

That validation is what this issue tracks.

## Category

data-integrity · DB hygiene · migration follow-up

## Problem

PR #398's `NOT VALID` constraint is a half-measure:

* ✅ New INSERTs / UPDATEs are blocked if they violate the predicate
* ❌ Existing rows that already violate it (e.g. legacy `{is_free=true, price=100}` records from before [EXP-68](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-68/security-paid-content-bypass-forge-course-completioncertificate)'s mapper fix) are not flagged
* ❌ The constraint is technically `NOT VALID` until validated — query planner won't use it for optimization, and tooling that audits "fully-enforced constraints" will skip it

Without finishing the work:

* Pre-existing bad data lingers silently
* Future schema audits may flag the constraint as "incomplete"
* The DB-level guarantee [EXP-73](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-73/security-exp-68-follow-ups-schema-constraint-body-parse-ordering) was supposed to deliver is only half-true

## Procedure

### Step 1 — Sweep production data

Run against the production read replica (or main if no replica):

```sql
SELECT id, slug, is_free, price, "createdAt", "updatedAt"
FROM "public"."courses"
WHERE NOT (
  (is_free = true  AND (price IS NULL OR price = 0))
  OR (is_free = false AND price IS NOT NULL AND price > 0)
)
ORDER BY "updatedAt" DESC;
```

Expected: zero rows. If any rows are returned, **do not proceed to step 2** — file a separate data-fix ticket first to decide each row's correct state (it could be a free course that was accidentally priced, or a paid course incorrectly marked free).

Document the sweep result in this issue as a comment with:

* Date of sweep
* Row count returned
* Query plan note if the table is large enough to need an index hint

### Step 2 — Add a migration that validates the constraint

Create `apps/experts-app/prisma/migrations/<ts>_validate_course_isfree_price_consistency/migration.sql`:

```sql
-- Validate the CHECK constraint added in 20260523000000_course_isfree_price_consistency.
-- Safe to run because the prior data sweep (see Linear EXP-XX) confirmed zero violating rows.
-- This upgrades the constraint from NOT VALID to fully-enforced, enabling planner use and
-- closing the EXP-73 DB-integrity gap.

ALTER TABLE "public"."courses"
  VALIDATE CONSTRAINT "course_isfree_price_consistency";
```

Notes:

* `VALIDATE CONSTRAINT` takes a `SHARE UPDATE EXCLUSIVE` lock on the table — does NOT block reads or writes, but does block concurrent DDL and other VALIDATE operations
* On a course table this size (small), validation completes in milliseconds; no special timing needed
* If the table ever grows large (10M+ rows), schedule during low-traffic window

### Step 3 — Verify after deploy

```sql
SELECT conname, convalidated
FROM pg_constraint
WHERE conrelid = '"public"."courses"'::regclass
  AND conname = 'course_isfree_price_consistency';
```

Expected: `convalidated = true`. Post the verification output in the PR.

## Test plan

1. **Migration is reversible** — write a `down.sql` (or note in the migration body) that sets the constraint back to `NOT VALID` if needed:

   ```sql
   ALTER TABLE "public"."courses"
     DROP CONSTRAINT IF EXISTS "course_isfree_price_consistency";
   -- (re-add NOT VALID version if rollback)
   ```
2. **Smoke test against test DB** — seed 50 valid courses + 0 violating rows, run `pnpm db:migrate`, assert `convalidated = true` via the `pg_constraint` query.
3. **Negative test** — seed 1 violating row, attempt the validation migration, assert it fails with a constraint violation. (This proves the validation step actually checks the data and isn't a no-op.)

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Data sweep query has been run against production and returned zero rows
- [ ] Sweep result documented as a comment on this Linear issue (date + row count)
- [ ] Migration file `<ts>_validate_course_isfree_price_consistency/migration.sql` exists with the `VALIDATE CONSTRAINT` statement and a header comment referencing this issue ID
- [ ] Migration body explains *why* validation is now safe (cross-link to sweep + [EXP-73](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-73/security-exp-68-follow-ups-schema-constraint-body-parse-ordering) + PR #398)
- [ ] Post-deploy verification query confirms `convalidated = true`
- [ ] `pnpm experts:check` + `pnpm experts:test` green from `$REPO_ROOT`
- [ ] No data fixes bundled with this migration — if bad rows are found, ship the fix as a separate prior migration

## Hard rules

* Do NOT run `VALIDATE CONSTRAINT` without first running the sweep in step 1
* Do NOT silently fix bad rows in the same migration as the validation — separate concerns for auditability
* Do NOT bundle other schema changes in this PR; it is a single-statement migration

## Out of scope

* Fixing any bad rows surfaced by the sweep → file as a separate data-fix ticket
* Adding similar constraints to other tables → file separately if/when surfaced
* Backfilling the `Course.isFree` boolean from `price` for older records → already done by the mapper-level fix shipped in [EXP-68](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-68/security-paid-content-bypass-forge-course-completioncertificate) work

## Related

* [EXP-73](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-73/security-exp-68-follow-ups-schema-constraint-body-parse-ordering) — parent issue (schema check shipped via PR #398)
* PR [logi-x/experts#398](https://linear.app/experts/review/fixsecurity-exp-73-follow-ups-schema-check-body-parse-order-modules-ee7203796be8) — added the `NOT VALID` constraint
* [EXP-68](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-68/security-paid-content-bypass-forge-course-completioncertificate) — original paywall bypass that surfaced the is_free / price drift

## Status

**Backlog.** Move to Todo only after the production data sweep confirms zero violating rows.

Title: [security] JWT role-staleness in event list — revoked admin bypasses host check (EXP-78 follow-up)
Description:
## R3 tracking

R3 fingerprint: `<!-- agent-fp: d72831b12e3e -->` — keep this marker in the body verbatim.

**Status: Backlog.** Ready to flip to Todo immediately — no upstream dependency. Filed separately from the sibling [EXP-78](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-78/security-jwt-role-staleness-in-event-thumbnail-upload-revoked-admin) fix (PR [logi-x/experts#399](https://linear.app/experts/review/fixeventsthumbnail-derive-isadmin-from-db-to-close-jwt-staleness-gap-286f8307772c)) so this lands as an independent, focused PR. Marker and labels pre-staged for R3.

## Source

Surfaced by security-auditor during the [EXP-78](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-78/security-jwt-role-staleness-in-event-thumbnail-upload-revoked-admin) fix review (PR [logi-x/experts#399](https://linear.app/experts/review/fixeventsthumbnail-derive-isadmin-from-db-to-close-jwt-staleness-gap-286f8307772c) follow-up note). Same vulnerability class as [EXP-69](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-69/security-productionstaging-secrets-committed-to-git-role-revocation) and [EXP-78](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-78/security-jwt-role-staleness-in-event-thumbnail-upload-revoked-admin), new route surface that the prior fixes didn't touch.

## Severity

**Medium**

## Location

`apps/experts-app/app/api/v1/events/route.ts:73-74` — `isAdmin` is derived from `session.user.roles` (JWT-derived, stale)

## Attack path

**Attacker → Input → Reach → Impact**

* **Attacker:** A user whose admin role has been revoked in the database but who holds a still-valid JWT (window up to 30 days, matching the configured JWT session lifetime).
* **Controlled input:** `session.user.roles` — populated at JWT-mint time, never refreshed from the DB within the request lifecycle.
* **Reachable path:**
  1. Attacker presents a valid but stale JWT with `roles: ["admin"]`.
  2. `const isAdmin = Boolean(session.user.roles?.includes("admin"))` on line 73-74 evaluates to `true`.
  3. The host-scoped filter on the events list query is skipped — the route returns events the caller would not otherwise be authorized to see.
* **Impact:** Revoked admin can list / read events they do not host, for up to 30 days after role revocation. Information disclosure of attendee counts, internal metadata, draft / unpublished events.

## Recommended fix

Apply the exact same pattern that landed in PR #399 for `events/[id]/thumbnail/route.ts` and earlier in [EXP-69](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-69/security-productionstaging-secrets-committed-to-git-role-revocation) for `events/[id]/approve|reject/route.ts`:

```ts
// Replace JWT-derived isAdmin with a fresh DB lookup, in parallel with other queries
const [dbUser, /* ...other parallel queries... */] = await Promise.all([
  prisma.user.findUnique({where: {id: sessionUserId}, select: {isAdmin: true}}),
  // existing parallel queries
]);

const isAdmin = Boolean(dbUser?.isAdmin);
```

Parallelizing with the existing DB lookups avoids adding a serial round-trip. Pattern matches the canonical fix established in commit `efb5bd4` ([EXP-69](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-69/security-productionstaging-secrets-committed-to-git-role-revocation)) and PR #399 ([EXP-78](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-78/security-jwt-role-staleness-in-event-thumbnail-upload-revoked-admin)).

## Test plan

Add to `apps/experts-app/app/api/v1/events/__tests__/route.test.ts` (or sibling):

1. **DB admin sees all events** — `User.isAdmin = true` in DB, JWT also admin → no host filter applied (existing behavior).
2. **Revoked admin (DB false, JWT true)** — `User.isAdmin = false` in DB, but session JWT has `roles: ["admin"]` → host filter IS applied (only their hosted events returned).
3. **Promoted user (DB true, JWT false)** — `User.isAdmin = true` in DB, JWT lacks the admin role → no host filter (fresh DB is authoritative).
4. **Non-admin host** — DB false, JWT false → host filter applied.
5. **No isAdmin shape assertion via mock** — assert `prismaMock.user.findUnique.mock.calls[0][0].select.isAdmin === true` to prevent the mock-shape regression seen in [EXP-68](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-68/security-paid-content-bypass-forge-course-completioncertificate) churn.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `isAdmin` derived from `prisma.user.findUnique({select: {isAdmin: true}})`, not from `session.user.roles`
- [ ] DB lookup parallelized via `Promise.all` with existing queries (no new serial round-trip)
- [ ] All 5 test cases pass against real Prisma + test DB (or full mock-shape assertions)
- [ ] No regression in [EXP-78](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-78/security-jwt-role-staleness-in-event-thumbnail-upload-revoked-admin) / PR #399 tests
- [ ] `pnpm experts:check` + `pnpm experts:test` green from `$REPO_ROOT`

## Out of scope

* The sibling `clone/route.ts` route — tracked separately so each lands as an independent PR (easier review, easier rollback)
* Broader audit of all routes using `session.user.roles` for admin checks → if any more turn up, file them individually; do not batch

## Related

* [EXP-78](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-78/security-jwt-role-staleness-in-event-thumbnail-upload-revoked-admin) — thumbnail upload (sibling, fixed in PR [logi-x/experts#399](https://linear.app/experts/review/fixeventsthumbnail-derive-isadmin-from-db-to-close-jwt-staleness-gap-286f8307772c))
* [EXP-69](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-69/security-productionstaging-secrets-committed-to-git-role-revocation) — approve / reject routes (original vulnerability class, fixed in commit `efb5bd4`)
* Companion follow-up for `events/[id]/clone/route.ts` — separate Linear issue

## Status

**Backlog.** Ready to flip to Todo whenever — no dependencies.
