Description:
## R3 tracking

R3 fingerprint: `<!-- agent-fp: f5c264dbf6b4 -->` — keep this marker in the body verbatim.

**Status: Backlog.** Ready to flip to Todo immediately — no upstream dependency. Filed separately from the sibling [EXP-78](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-78/security-jwt-role-staleness-in-event-thumbnail-upload-revoked-admin) fix (PR [logi-x/experts#399](https://linear.app/experts/review/fixeventsthumbnail-derive-isadmin-from-db-to-close-jwt-staleness-gap-286f8307772c)) so this lands as an independent, focused PR. Marker and labels pre-staged for R3.

## Source

Surfaced by security-auditor during the [EXP-78](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-78/security-jwt-role-staleness-in-event-thumbnail-upload-revoked-admin) fix review (PR [logi-x/experts#399](https://linear.app/experts/review/fixeventsthumbnail-derive-isadmin-from-db-to-close-jwt-staleness-gap-286f8307772c) follow-up note). Same vulnerability class as [EXP-69](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-69/security-productionstaging-secrets-committed-to-git-role-revocation) and [EXP-78](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-78/security-jwt-role-staleness-in-event-thumbnail-upload-revoked-admin), new route surface that the prior fixes didn't touch.

## Severity

**Medium**

## Location

`apps/experts-app/app/api/v1/events/[id]/clone/route.ts:32-33` — `isAdmin` is derived from `session.user.roles` (JWT-derived, stale)

## Attack path

**Attacker → Input → Reach → Impact**

* **Attacker:** A user whose admin role has been revoked in the database but who holds a still-valid JWT (window up to 30 days, matching the configured JWT session lifetime).
* **Controlled input:** `session.user.roles` — populated at JWT-mint time, never refreshed from the DB within the request lifecycle.
* **Reachable path:**
  1. Attacker presents a valid but stale JWT with `roles: ["admin"]`.
  2. `const isAdmin = Boolean(session.user.roles?.includes("admin"))` on line 32-33 evaluates to `true`.
  3. The guard that restricts clone to admins-or-hosts short-circuits — the route accepts any caller-supplied `eventId` and creates a clone.
* **Impact:** Revoked admin can clone any event (including drafts / private events of other hosts) for up to 30 days after role revocation. The cloned event is owned by the cloner, enabling impersonation, deceptive duplicates, or data theft of templated content.

## Recommended fix

Apply the exact same pattern that landed in PR #399 for `events/[id]/thumbnail/route.ts` and earlier in [EXP-69](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-69/security-productionstaging-secrets-committed-to-git-role-revocation) for `events/[id]/approve|reject/route.ts`:

```ts
// Replace JWT-derived isAdmin with a fresh DB lookup, in parallel with other queries
const [eventRow, hostForUser, dbUser] = await Promise.all([
  prisma.event.findUnique({where: {id: eventId}, select: {id: true /* ...existing select... */}}),
  prisma.eventHost.findFirst({where: {eventId, userId: sessionUserId}, select: {role: true}}),
  prisma.user.findUnique({where: {id: sessionUserId}, select: {isAdmin: true}}),
]);

const isAdmin = Boolean(dbUser?.isAdmin);
```

Parallelizing with the existing DB lookups avoids adding a serial round-trip. Pattern matches the canonical fix established in commit `efb5bd4` ([EXP-69](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-69/security-productionstaging-secrets-committed-to-git-role-revocation)) and PR #399 ([EXP-78](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-78/security-jwt-role-staleness-in-event-thumbnail-upload-revoked-admin)).

## Test plan

Add to `apps/experts-app/app/api/v1/events/[id]/clone/__tests__/route.test.ts` (or sibling):

1. **DB admin can clone any event** — `User.isAdmin = true` in DB, JWT also admin → clone succeeds (existing behavior).
2. **Revoked admin (DB false, JWT true)** — `User.isAdmin = false` in DB, JWT has `roles: ["admin"]`, target event not hosted by caller → 403.
3. **Promoted user (DB true, JWT false)** — `User.isAdmin = true` in DB, JWT lacks admin → clone succeeds (fresh DB is authoritative).
4. **Host can clone their own event** — DB false, JWT false, but caller is in `eventHost` → clone succeeds.
5. **Non-admin non-host** — DB false, JWT false, not in `eventHost` → 403.
6. **No isAdmin shape assertion via mock** — assert `prismaMock.user.findUnique.mock.calls[0][0].select.isAdmin === true` to prevent the mock-shape regression seen in [EXP-68](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-68/security-paid-content-bypass-forge-course-completioncertificate) churn.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `isAdmin` derived from `prisma.user.findUnique({select: {isAdmin: true}})`, not from `session.user.roles`
- [ ] DB lookup parallelized via `Promise.all` with existing queries (no new serial round-trip)
- [ ] All 6 test cases pass against real Prisma + test DB (or full mock-shape assertions)
- [ ] No regression in [EXP-78](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-78/security-jwt-role-staleness-in-event-thumbnail-upload-revoked-admin) / PR #399 tests
- [ ] `pnpm experts:check` + `pnpm experts:test` green from `$REPO_ROOT`

## Out of scope

* The sibling `events/route.ts` list endpoint — tracked separately so each lands as an independent PR
* Broader audit of all routes using `session.user.roles` for admin checks → if any more turn up, file them individually; do not batch

Title: [security] Extract enforceStorageQuota guard + apply to all R2-write routes (EXP-72 follow-up)
Description:
## R3 tracking

R3 fingerprint: `<!-- agent-fp: eda762c80f1e -->` — keep this marker in the body verbatim.

**Status: Backlog.** Ready to flip to Todo immediately — depends only on [EXP-72](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-72/security-per-user-storage-quota-on-apiv1internalupload-cost-control) (already merged via PR [logi-x/experts#408](https://linear.app/experts/review/fixupload-per-user-storage-quota-preflight-gate-linear-exp-72-9eff8c24bab2)). Marker and labels pre-staged for R3.

## Source

Surfaced by gatekeeper during the [EXP-72](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-72/security-per-user-storage-quota-on-apiv1internalupload-cost-control) merge review (PR [logi-x/experts#408](https://linear.app/experts/review/fixupload-per-user-storage-quota-preflight-gate-linear-exp-72-9eff8c24bab2) note):

> *Follow-up worth filing: extract the quota gate into a shared enforceStorageQuota guard and apply to certifications/documents/upload, course/event thumbnail routes, and lesson video upload — those write to R2 without the new gate.*

[EXP-72](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-72/security-per-user-storage-quota-on-apiv1internalupload-cost-control) closed the storage-cost gap on `/api/v1/internal/upload` only. Other R2-write paths still allow unbounded uploads from authenticated users, defeating the goal of the quota system.

## Category

cost-control · DoS hardening · refactor

## Problem

PR #408 inlined the quota check directly inside `apps/experts-app/app/api/v1/internal/upload/route.ts`. Other routes that write to R2 (or trigger pending `file` rows) bypass it entirely:

* `apps/experts-app/app/api/v1/certifications/documents/upload/route.ts` — certification docs
* `apps/experts-app/app/api/v1/courses/[id]/thumbnail/route.ts` — course thumbnails
* `apps/experts-app/app/api/v1/events/[id]/thumbnail/route.ts` — event thumbnails (verify path — recent [EXP-78](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-78/security-jwt-role-staleness-in-event-thumbnail-upload-revoked-admin) fix landed here)
* `apps/experts-app/app/api/v1/courses/[id]/modules/[moduleId]/lessons/[lessonId]/video/upload/route.ts` — lesson video upload (verify exact path)
* Any other route that calls `r2Client.put(...)` or inserts a `file` row directly

(Verify the full list with `grep -rn 'r2Client.put\|prisma.file.create' apps/experts-app/app/api/` during research — the list above is the gatekeeper's quick scan, treat as starting point not authoritative.)

## Recommended approach

### Step 1 — Extract the guard

Create `apps/experts-app/src/lib/storage/enforce-storage-quota.ts`:

```ts
import {NextResponse} from "next/server";
import {prisma} from "@/lib/db";
import {getStorageQuotaForTier, type StorageTier} from "./storage-quotas";
import {resolveStorageTier} from "./resolve-storage-tier";

export type QuotaCheckResult =
  | {ok: true; usedBytes: number; quotaBytes: number; remainingBytes: number}
  | {ok: false; response: NextResponse};

export async function enforceStorageQuota(
  userId: string,
  tier: StorageTier,
  incomingBytes: number,
): Promise<QuotaCheckResult> {
  const quotaBytes = getStorageQuotaForTier(tier);

  const used = await prisma.file.aggregate({
    where: {uploadedById: userId, status: "ready"},
    _sum: {size: true},
  });
  const usedBytes = Number(used._sum.size ?? 0);

  if (usedBytes + incomingBytes > quotaBytes) {
    return {
      ok: false,
      response: NextResponse.json(
        {
          error: "Storage quota exceeded",
          usedBytes,
          incomingBytes,
          quotaBytes,
          remainingBytes: Math.max(quotaBytes - usedBytes, 0),
        },
        {status: 413},
      ),
    };
  }

  return {ok: true, usedBytes, quotaBytes, remainingBytes: quotaBytes - usedBytes - incomingBytes};
}
```

### Step 2 — Refactor `/api/v1/internal/upload/route.ts` to use the guard

Replace the inlined block from PR #408 with:

```ts
const tier = resolveStorageTier(session.user);
const check = await enforceStorageQuota(session.user.id, tier, file.size);
if (!check.ok) return check.response;
```

Behavior MUST be byte-identical to PR #408 — existing tests must pass unchanged.

### Step 3 — Apply guard to each remaining R2-write route

For each route in the bullet list above (and any surfaced by the grep), add the same 3-line block after the existing auth check, before any R2 write. The location of "incoming bytes" depends on the route's upload shape (multipart form, base64, signed-url presign body, etc.) — handle each per its existing pattern; do NOT change the upload shape.

For presigned-URL flows (if any), the byte size comes from the client claim. Trust but verify: store the claimed size in the pending `file` row, then re-validate after the actual upload via a finalize endpoint or background reconciliation. (Out of scope here — file separately if exists.)

### Step 4 — Test coverage per route

For each refactored route, add:

1. **Under quota** — succeeds (existing happy path).
2. **Over quota** — 413 with correct payload shape.
3. **Aggregate uses correct predicate** — `prismaMock.file.aggregate.mock.calls[0][0]` includes `where.uploadedById` + `where.status === "ready"` (mock-shape trap protection — see \[\[feedback_dropped_endpoint_lag\]\]).

Plus shared guard tests in `apps/experts-app/src/lib/storage/__tests__/enforce-storage-quota.test.ts` (under/at/over/null-size).

## Test plan

* All [EXP-72](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-72/security-per-user-storage-quota-on-apiv1internalupload-cost-control) tests in `internal/upload/__tests__/route.test.ts` pass unchanged after the refactor.
* New per-route tests added (3 cases each).
* New shared guard test (5 cases: under, at, over, null-incoming, null-used).
* `pnpm experts:check` + `pnpm experts:test` green.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `/api/v1/internal/upload/route.ts` refactored to use the shared guard, [EXP-72](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-72/security-per-user-storage-quota-on-apiv1internalupload-cost-control) tests still green

Title: [security] File.size column is Int32 — overflows at ~2.1GB; migrate to BigInt before any tier exceeds 2GB
Description:
## R3 tracking

R3 fingerprint: `<!-- agent-fp: 2d5b36fd7b73 -->` — keep this marker in the body verbatim.

**Status: Backlog.** Move to Todo BEFORE the tiering project ships any tier whose quota exceeds 2 GB (current uniform quota is 1 GB so this is safe today, but it's a footgun the moment quotas grow).

## Source

Surfaced by gatekeeper during the [EXP-72](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-72/security-per-user-storage-quota-on-apiv1internalupload-cost-control) merge review (PR [logi-x/experts#408](https://linear.app/experts/review/fixupload-per-user-storage-quota-preflight-gate-linear-exp-72-9eff8c24bab2) note):

> *Also* `File.size` *is Int32 (overflows at \~2.1GB) — fine under 1GB cap, must move to BigInt before any tier exceeds 2GB.*

## Category

data-integrity · schema migration · silent overflow risk

## Problem

`File.size` in `apps/experts-app/prisma/schema.prisma` is declared as `Int` which Prisma maps to PostgreSQL `INTEGER` (signed 32-bit). The max value is **2,147,483,647 bytes ≈ 2.147 GB**.

Today's behavior:

* [EXP-72](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-72/security-per-user-storage-quota-on-apiv1internalupload-cost-control) caps users at 1 GB → no individual file or sum can hit the limit
* Single-file uploads larger than 2.147 GB → INSERT fails with `value out of range for type integer`
* Aggregate `_sum(size)` returns `INTEGER` → silently overflows once cumulative uploads cross the boundary; quota check returns garbage

Failure modes once a tier exceeds 2 GB:

1. **File create silently overflows** — Postgres throws on insert, route returns 500
2. **Aggregate sum wraps negative** — `(2.1 GB - 1 byte) + 1 byte = -2.1 GB` after overflow → quota check passes everything, billing surprise compounds
3. **Race with concurrent uploads** — overflow + reservation system from [EXP-80](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-80/security-race-safe-storage-usage-ledger-userstorageusage-reservation) interact unpredictably

Both 1 and 2 are silent unless tests catch them, and once a tier exists at 2.5 GB the system is one upload away from undefined behavior.

## Recommended approach

### Step 1 — Schema migration

Update `apps/experts-app/prisma/schema.prisma`:

```prisma
model File {
  // ...existing fields...
  size BigInt    // was: Int
  // ...
}
```

Create migration `apps/experts-app/prisma/migrations/<ts>_file_size_to_bigint/migration.sql`:

```sql
-- Lock-light migration: ALTER COLUMN TYPE is metadata-only when going Int → BigInt in Postgres
-- (the on-disk representation widens, but for INT → BIGINT Postgres does a rewrite which DOES
-- take an ACCESS EXCLUSIVE lock for the duration of the rewrite).
-- File table is small enough today that the lock window is acceptable. If the table grows large
-- before this ships, prefer the add-new-column-backfill-swap pattern instead.

ALTER TABLE "public"."files"
  ALTER COLUMN "size" TYPE BIGINT USING "size"::BIGINT;

-- Verify with: SELECT pg_typeof(size) FROM files LIMIT 1;
```

Verify the table name (`File` model may map to `files` or `File` depending on `@@map`).

### Step 2 — Update consumers to handle BigInt

Prisma's TypeScript client returns `BigInt` for `BigInt` columns. Audit every read site:

```bash
grep -rn '\.size\b' apps/experts-app/src apps/experts-app/app | grep -v '\.size_t\|fontSize\|test\|\.next'
```

Each consumer needs one of:

* `Number(file.size)` if certain the value fits in `Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER` (9 PB — always safe for filesizes)
* Pass through as `bigint` if doing arithmetic that needs full precision
* Stringify for JSON responses (`JSON.stringify` throws on `BigInt` — use `file.size.toString()` or set up a global replacer)

For `EXP-72`'s preflight gate (`enforce-storage-quota.ts` once extracted, or inline today):

```ts
const used = await prisma.file.aggregate({
  where: {uploadedById: userId, status: "ready"},
  _sum: {size: true},
});
const usedBytes = Number(used._sum.size ?? 0n);  // BigInt → Number (safe under ~9 PB)
```

### Step 3 — JSON response shape

The [EXP-72](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-72/security-per-user-storage-quota-on-apiv1internalupload-cost-control) 413 response includes `usedBytes`, `incomingBytes`, `quotaBytes`, `remainingBytes`. Keep these as plain `Number` for JSON serialization (filesizes stay under JS safe integer). If any response field exposes raw `BigInt`, wrap with `.toString()` or convert to `Number`.

### Step 4 — Verify aggregate overflow gone

Add a test (or run manually against staging) that:

1. Seeds 3 file rows with size = 1 GB each (total 3 GB)
2. Calls aggregate, asserts `_sum.size === 3n * 1024n ** 3n` (BigInt)
3. Converts to Number, asserts `=== 3 * 1024 ** 3`

Without the migration this would either error or return a wrapped value.

## Test plan

1. **Migration up** — run on test DB seeded with `Int`-typed rows, assert column type becomes BIGINT, all existing values preserved
2. **Round-trip** — insert a row with size = 5 GB (greater than Int32 max), assert insert succeeds and read-back equals 5 GB as BigInt
3. **Aggregate** — sum 3 × 1GB rows, assert exact value (no wrap)
4. **JSON serialization** — assert 413 response from [EXP-72](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-72/security-per-user-storage-quota-on-apiv1internalupload-cost-control) path serializes cleanly with the BigInt column (no `TypeError: Do not know how to serialize a BigInt`)
5. **Existing** [EXP-72](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-72/security-per-user-storage-quota-on-apiv1internalupload-cost-control) **tests** — all still pass after the column-type change
6. **Smoke against staging DB** before prod — `ALTER COLUMN TYPE` on production-shaped data validates lock duration

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `prisma/schema.prisma` File.size is `BigInt`
- [ ] Migration ALTER applied cleanly to test DB
- [ ] All read sites either `Number()` cast or carry `bigint` through
- [ ] [EXP-72](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-72/security-per-user-storage-quota-on-apiv1internalupload-cost-control) quota gate handles BigInt aggregate correctly
- [ ] JSON responses serialize without BigInt errors
- [ ] All 6 test cases pass
- [ ] `pnpm experts:check` + `pnpm experts:test` green
- [ ] No regression in [EXP-72](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-72/security-per-user-storage-quota-on-apiv1internalupload-cost-control) / [EXP-80](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-80/security-race-safe-storage-usage-ledger-userstorageusage-reservation) / [EXP-81](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-81/bug-stale-pending-file-row-cleanup-r2-orphan-reaper) / [EXP-82](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-82/bug-admin-storage-dashboard-over-quota-alerts) work
- [ ] Migration includes lock-duration note for future schema reviewers

## Hard rules

* Do NOT ship if the `files` table has grown so large (10M+ rows) that ALTER COLUMN TYPE takes an unacceptable lock — switch to the add-column-backfill-swap pattern instead and re-file
* Do NOT change response field types from Number to BigInt — keep JSON shape stable for clients
* Do NOT bundle this with any other schema change

## Out of scope

* Changing `File.size` units (still bytes)
* Adding compression / dedup / chunking signals — separate concern
* Per-tier quotas — depends on tiering project, blocked

## Related

* [EXP-72](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-72/security-per-user-storage-quota-on-apiv1internalupload-cost-control) — preflight gate (PR #408)
* [EXP-80](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-80/security-race-safe-storage-usage-ledger-userstorageusage-reservation) — race-safe ledger (also uses BigInt for `usedBytes`/`reservedBytes` — alignment matters; ledger schema in [EXP-80](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-80/security-race-safe-storage-usage-ledger-userstorageusage-reservation) already uses BigInt, so they'll be consistent post-migration)
* Tiering project — if any tier targets >2 GB, this issue MUST land first

## Status

**Backlog.** Safe today under 1 GB uniform cap. Become Todo the moment any tier crosses 2 GB.

Title: [security] JWT role-staleness in PUT /api/v1/events/[id] — revoked admin edits any event
Description:
R3 fingerprint: `<!-- agent-fp: 0253a1037e64 -->`.

## Trigger scenario

1. User A has `isAdmin: true` in the DB and holds a valid JWT with `roles: ["admin"]`.
2. An operator revokes A's admin role in the DB — no forced logout, JWT remains live for up to 30 days.
3. A sends `PUT /api/v1/events/<any-non-archived-event-id>` with body `{"publishingStatus": "draft", "isFeatured": true}` for an event they are **not** a host of.
4. The outer host-or-admin gate: `!event.hosts.some(h => h.userId === A.id)` = `true` (not a host), but `!session.user.roles.includes("admin")` = `false` (stale JWT) → `true && false = false` → **gate does not return 401**.
5. `const isAdmin = Boolean(session?.user?.roles?.includes("admin"))` → `true` (stale).
6. The handler proceeds with admin privileges on the non-archived event.

**Confirmed exploitable paths (verified by code-reviewer + qa-tester):**

* **Edit any non-archived event content:** title, description, speakers, agenda, tags — overwriting a host's content without their consent.
* **Set** `isFeatured: true`**:** spread unconditionally from the request body with no separate admin guard — `...(body.isFeatured !== undefined && {isFeatured: body.isFeatured})`. Any caller passing the outer gate can set it.
* **Force-unpublish (**`publishingStatus → "draft"`**):** Guard at line \~163: `if (nextStatus === "draft" && event.publishingStatus === "published" && !isAdmin)` is bypassed when `isAdmin = true` (stale JWT).
* **Archive a draft or published event:** `canArchive(lifecycle, {isAdmin})` returns `true` for any non-archived status when `isAdmin = true`.

**NOT exploitable via this bug (qa-tester verified):**

* Editing **archived** events: `canEditContent` checks `ctx.status === "archived"` *before* the `isAdmin` short-circuit — returns HTTP 409 for archived events regardless of `isAdmin`.
* Bypassing the `pricingLockedUntil` cooldown: the cooldown block has no `&& !isAdmin` escape hatch and fires for all callers.

## Root cause

`apps/experts-app/app/api/v1/events/[id]/route.ts`, `PUT` handler — `session?.user?.roles` is JWT-derived and can be stale for up to 30 days post-revocation. The stale value is used for the outer host-or-admin authorization gate and for `const isAdmin` that gates lifecycle transitions, featured-flag writes, and publishing status changes.

Same class as [EXP-69](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-69/security-productionstaging-secrets-committed-to-git-role-revocation) (approve/reject), [EXP-78](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-78/security-jwt-role-staleness-in-event-thumbnail-upload-revoked-admin) (thumbnail), [EXP-84](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-84/security-jwt-role-staleness-in-event-list-revoked-admin-bypasses-host) (events POST), [EXP-85](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-85/security-jwt-role-staleness-in-event-clone-revoked-admin-bypasses-host) (events clone).

## Suggested fix

Replace JWT-derived `isAdmin` with a fresh DB lookup, parallelised with the existing event fetch:

```typescript
const [event, dbUser] = await Promise.all([
  prisma.event.findUnique({
    where: {id},
    include: {hosts: {select: {userId: true, role: true}}, _count: {select: {registrations: true}}},
  }),
  prisma.user.findUnique({where: {id: session.user.id}, select: {isAdmin: true}}),
]);
if (!dbUser) return NextResponse.json({error: "Unauthorized"}, {status: 401});
const isAdmin = Boolean(dbUser.isAdmin);
```

Apply the same pattern to the outer gate (`!isHost && !isAdmin`) and all downstream `isAdmin` references. Add regression tests for: revoked-admin path, deleted-account path, valid-admin path, and mock-shape assertion on `user.findUnique({select: {isAdmin: true}})`.

## Related prior issues

[EXP-69](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-69/security-productionstaging-secrets-committed-to-git-role-revocation), [EXP-78](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-78/security-jwt-role-staleness-in-event-thumbnail-upload-revoked-admin), [EXP-84](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-84/security-jwt-role-staleness-in-event-list-revoked-admin-bypasses-host), [EXP-85](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-85/security-jwt-role-staleness-in-event-clone-revoked-admin-bypasses-host) (all resolved — same bug class, different route surfaces).

## Verified by

code-reviewer + qa-tester subagents (automated bug-finder scan 2026-05-23).

Title: [security] JWT role-staleness in DELETE /api/v1/events/[id] — revoked admin permanently deletes any event
Description:
R3 fingerprint: `<!-- agent-fp: 5b2fd78b32ae -->`.

## Trigger scenario

1. User A has `isAdmin: true` in the DB and holds a valid JWT with `roles: ["admin"]`.
2. An operator revokes A's admin role in the DB — JWT remains live for up to 30 days.
3. A sends `DELETE /api/v1/events/<any-event-id>` for an event they are **not** a host of.
4. The only authorization gate:

   ```
   const isHost = event.hosts.some(host => host.userId === session.user.id);  // false
   if (!isHost && !session?.user?.roles?.includes("admin")) { ... }           // stale JWT → "admin" still present
   ```

   `!false && !true` = `false` → **gate does not fire**.
5. `prisma.event.delete({where: {id}})` executes. Cascading deletes remove registrations, sessions, schedules, agenda, speakers, occurrences, and price-version history. **This is irreversible without a DB restore.**

## Root cause

`apps/experts-app/app/api/v1/events/[id]/route.ts`, `DELETE` handler — the sole host-or-admin authorization gate uses `session.user.roles` (JWT-derived, stale up to 30 days). A revoked admin who is not a host of an event passes the gate and triggers permanent data destruction.

Same class as [EXP-69](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-69/security-productionstaging-secrets-committed-to-git-role-revocation), [EXP-78](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-78/security-jwt-role-staleness-in-event-thumbnail-upload-revoked-admin), [EXP-84](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-84/security-jwt-role-staleness-in-event-list-revoked-admin-bypasses-host), [EXP-85](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-85/security-jwt-role-staleness-in-event-clone-revoked-admin-bypasses-host).

## Suggested fix

Parallelise a fresh DB lookup with the event fetch (no extra round-trip):

```typescript
const [event, dbUser] = await Promise.all([
  prisma.event.findUnique({
    where: {id},
    include: {hosts: {select: {userId: true, role: true}}, _count: {select: {registrations: true}}},
  }),
  prisma.user.findUnique({where: {id: session.user.id}, select: {isAdmin: true}}),
]);
if (!dbUser) return NextResponse.json({error: "Unauthorized"}, {status: 401});
const isAdmin = Boolean(dbUser.isAdmin);
const isHost = event?.hosts.some(host => host.userId === session.user.id) ?? false;
if (!isHost && !isAdmin) return NextResponse.json({error: "Unauthorized"}, {status: 401});
```

Add regression tests for: revoked-admin-can-not-delete, deleted-account-401, valid-admin-can-delete, and mock-shape assertion on `user.findUnique({select: {isAdmin: true}})`.

## Related prior issues

[EXP-69](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-69/security-productionstaging-secrets-committed-to-git-role-revocation), [EXP-78](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-78/security-jwt-role-staleness-in-event-thumbnail-upload-revoked-admin), [EXP-84](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-84/security-jwt-role-staleness-in-event-list-revoked-admin-bypasses-host), [EXP-85](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-85/security-jwt-role-staleness-in-event-clone-revoked-admin-bypasses-host) (all resolved — same bug class, different route surfaces).

Title: [security] JWT role-staleness in GET /api/v1/events/[id] — revoked admin reads private meeting credentials
Description:
R3 fingerprint: `<!-- agent-fp: c53cfd8af8a6 -->`.

## Trigger scenario

1. User A has `isAdmin: true` in the DB and holds a valid JWT with `roles: ["admin"]`.
2. An operator revokes A's admin role in the DB — JWT remains live for up to 30 days.
3. A sends `GET /api/v1/events/<private-event-id>` for an online or hybrid event they are **not** a host of.
4. In the GET handler:

   ```typescript
   const result = await handleEventDetail({
     eventId: id,
     userId: session?.user?.id,
     isAdmin: Boolean(session?.user?.roles?.includes("admin")),  // stale → true
   });
   ```
5. Inside `handleEventDetail`, `canSeePrivateDetails = true` because `isAdmin` is `true`.
6. The response skips the redaction block that nulls out `meetingUrl` and `locationDetails` for non-admins/non-hosts:

   ```typescript
   if (!canSeePrivateDetails) {
     dto.meetingUrl = null;             // Zoom/Teams link (often includes embedded password)
     dto.locationDetails = null;        // venue access instructions, passcodes
   }
   ```
7. A receives the full private event data — meeting credentials and venue access information — for any event on the platform.

## Root cause

`apps/experts-app/app/api/v1/events/[id]/route.ts`, `GET` handler — `isAdmin` passed to `handleEventDetail` is derived from `session.user.roles` (JWT-derived, stale up to 30 days). This controls the `canSeePrivateDetails` gate that guards `meetingUrl` and `locationDetails`.

Same class as [EXP-69](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-69/security-productionstaging-secrets-committed-to-git-role-revocation), [EXP-78](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-78/security-jwt-role-staleness-in-event-thumbnail-upload-revoked-admin), [EXP-84](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-84/security-jwt-role-staleness-in-event-list-revoked-admin-bypasses-host), [EXP-85](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-85/security-jwt-role-staleness-in-event-clone-revoked-admin-bypasses-host).

## Suggested fix

Add a DB lookup for `isAdmin` before calling `handleEventDetail`:

```typescript
const session = await auth();
const dbUser = session?.user?.id
  ? await prisma.user.findUnique({where: {id: session.user.id}, select: {isAdmin: true}})
  : null;
const isAdmin = Boolean(dbUser?.isAdmin);

const result = await handleEventDetail({eventId: id, userId: session?.user?.id, isAdmin});
```

Add regression tests for: revoked-admin receives null meetingUrl, valid-admin receives meetingUrl, non-host non-admin receives null meetingUrl.

## Related prior issues

[EXP-69](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-69/security-productionstaging-secrets-committed-to-git-role-revocation), [EXP-78](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-78/security-jwt-role-staleness-in-event-thumbnail-upload-revoked-admin), [EXP-84](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-84/security-jwt-role-staleness-in-event-list-revoked-admin-bypasses-host), [EXP-85](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-85/security-jwt-role-staleness-in-event-clone-revoked-admin-bypasses-host) (all resolved — same bug class, different route surfaces).

Title: [security] JWT role-staleness on PUT /api/v1/events/[id] — revoked admin bypasses event edit, publish, and archive guards
Description:
## R3 tracking

R3 fingerprint: `<!-- agent-fp: 0779edb68b5d -->` — keep this marker in the body verbatim; R3's filter greps for the `<!-- agent-fp:` prefix and will skip this issue if it's stripped. Wrapped in inline code so Linear's parser doesn't drop it on UPDATE.

## Severity

**High**

## Location

`apps/experts-app/app/api/v1/events/[id]/route.ts` — `PUT` handler

Two sites consume the JWT-derived `isAdmin` value with no DB revalidation:

1. Authorization gate (line \~120): `if (!isHost && !session?.user?.roles?.includes("admin"))` — primary host/admin bypass.
2. Lifecycle policy variable (line \~130): `const isAdmin = Boolean(session?.user?.roles?.includes("admin"))` — passed to `canEditContent`, `canPublish`, `canArchive`, and the unpublish guard.

## Attack path

**Attacker:** A user whose `isAdmin` flag was revoked in the database but who still holds a valid JWT (window up to the configured JWT session lifetime, \~30 days).

**Controlled input:** Any `PUT /api/v1/events/:id` request body — including `publishingStatus`, `price`, `isFree`, `title`, `description`, `schedule`, `speakers`, `agenda`, `galleryImageUrls`, etc.

**Reach:** Every event in the system that the attacker does not already host. The JWT-derived `isAdmin=true` bypasses the `!isHost` authorization gate unconditionally.

**Concrete impact:**

* Force-publish any draft event owned by any instructor (bypasses `canPublish` draft-only guard with `isAdmin=true`).
* Unpublish any live event (the `!isAdmin` unpublish guard is bypassed).
* Archive any non-archived event (bypasses `canArchive`).
* Overwrite all editable fields: title, description, pricing, schedule, speakers, agenda, gallery — on any event across the platform.
* Bypass the pricing cooldown logic for published events (price change guard runs, but the attacker can still initiate it as if they were the admin).

This is cross-tenant write access with content destruction potential on any event in the system.

## Recommended remediation

Follow the [EXP-69](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-69/security-productionstaging-secrets-committed-to-git-role-revocation) / [EXP-78](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-78/security-jwt-role-staleness-in-event-thumbnail-upload-revoked-admin) / [EXP-84](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-84/security-jwt-role-staleness-in-event-list-revoked-admin-bypasses-host) / [EXP-85](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-85/security-jwt-role-staleness-in-event-clone-revoked-admin-bypasses-host) canonical fix pattern:

1. At the top of the `PUT` handler, after `auth()`, run a parallelised DB lookup:

   ```ts
   const [body, dbUser] = await Promise.all([
     request.json(),
     prisma.user.findUnique({ where: { id: session.user.id }, select: { isAdmin: true } }),
   ]);
   if (!dbUser) return NextResponse.json({ error: "Unauthorized" }, { status: 401 });
   const isAdmin = Boolean(dbUser.isAdmin);
   ```
2. Replace every `session?.user?.roles?.includes("admin")` reference in this handler with the DB-derived `isAdmin`.
3. Add regression tests for: revoked-admin-stale-JWT path (gets 401), deleted-account path (gets 401), DB-admin path (succeeds), and a Prisma call-shape assertion locking in `{where:{id},select:{isAdmin:true}}`.

## Related prior issues

[EXP-69](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-69/security-productionstaging-secrets-committed-to-git-role-revocation) (approve/reject — resolved), [EXP-78](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-78/security-jwt-role-staleness-in-event-thumbnail-upload-revoked-admin) (thumbnail — resolved), [EXP-84](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-84/security-jwt-role-staleness-in-event-list-revoked-admin-bypasses-host) (POST /events — resolved), [EXP-85](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-85/security-jwt-role-staleness-in-event-clone-revoked-admin-bypasses-host) (clone — resolved). Same issue class, this is the next unpatched surface in the events domain.

## Verified by

security-auditor (primary), architecture-reviewer (verifier) — both confirmed High severity and valid attack path.

*Filed by automated security routine on 2026-05-23T00:00:00Z.*

Title: [security] JWT role-staleness on DELETE /api/v1/events/[id] — revoked admin can delete any event
Description:
## R3 tracking

R3 fingerprint: `<!-- agent-fp: 92eee65117fb -->` — keep this marker in the body verbatim; R3's filter greps for the `<!-- agent-fp:` prefix and will skip this issue if it's stripped. Wrapped in inline code so Linear's parser doesn't drop it on UPDATE.

## Severity

**High**

## Location

`apps/experts-app/app/api/v1/events/[id]/route.ts` — `DELETE` handler

Authorization gate (line \~441): `if (!isHost && !session?.user?.roles?.includes("admin"))` — uses JWT-derived roles with no DB revalidation.

## Attack path

**Attacker:** A user whose `isAdmin` flag was revoked in the database but who still holds a valid JWT (window up to \~30 days).

**Controlled input:** `DELETE /api/v1/events/:id` with any valid event UUID.

**Reach:** Every event that the attacker does not already host AND that passes the `canDelete` lifecycle guard (i.e., draft events and published events with zero paid registrations). The JWT-derived `isAdmin=true` bypasses the host-ownership check unconditionally.

**Concrete impact:**

* Mass deletion of draft/unpublished events across the entire platform, including events owned by other instructors.
* Deletion of published events with no paid registrations.
* `canDelete` does guard against events with paid registrations, which limits the blast radius for monetized content — but draft content and zero-revenue events are fully exposed to destructive deletion.

## Recommended remediation

Follow the [EXP-69](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-69/security-productionstaging-secrets-committed-to-git-role-revocation) / [EXP-78](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-78/security-jwt-role-staleness-in-event-thumbnail-upload-revoked-admin) / [EXP-84](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-84/security-jwt-role-staleness-in-event-list-revoked-admin-bypasses-host) / [EXP-85](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-85/security-jwt-role-staleness-in-event-clone-revoked-admin-bypasses-host) canonical fix pattern:

1. At the top of the `DELETE` handler, after `auth()`, add a DB revalidation:

   ```ts
   const dbUser = await prisma.user.findUnique({
     where: { id: session.user.id },
     select: { isAdmin: true },
   });
   if (!dbUser) return NextResponse.json({ error: "Unauthorized" }, { status: 401 });
   const isAdmin = Boolean(dbUser.isAdmin);
   ```
2. Replace `session?.user?.roles?.includes("admin")` in the authorization gate with DB-derived `isAdmin`.
3. Add regression tests for: revoked-admin-stale-JWT path (gets 401), deleted-account path (gets 401), DB-admin path (can delete), and a Prisma call-shape assertion.

## Related prior issues

[EXP-69](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-69/security-productionstaging-secrets-committed-to-git-role-revocation) (approve/reject — resolved), [EXP-78](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-78/security-jwt-role-staleness-in-event-thumbnail-upload-revoked-admin) (thumbnail — resolved), [EXP-84](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-84/security-jwt-role-staleness-in-event-list-revoked-admin-bypasses-host) (POST /events — resolved), [EXP-85](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-85/security-jwt-role-staleness-in-event-clone-revoked-admin-bypasses-host) (clone — resolved). Same issue class, this is the next unpatched surface in the events domain.

Title: [security] JWT role-staleness on GET /api/v1/events/[id] — revoked admin reads meeting credentials and draft event details
Description:
## R3 tracking

R3 fingerprint: `<!-- agent-fp: 79a6ffd4b0c5 -->` — keep this marker in the body verbatim; R3's filter greps for the `<!-- agent-fp:` prefix and will skip this issue if it's stripped. Wrapped in inline code so Linear's parser doesn't drop it on UPDATE.

## Severity

**Medium**

## Location

`apps/experts-app/app/api/v1/events/[id]/route.ts` — `GET` handler, line \~53:

```ts
isAdmin: Boolean(session?.user?.roles?.includes("admin")),
```

This JWT-derived value is passed to `handleEventDetail` which uses it to set `canSeePrivateDetails = Boolean(isHost || userRegistration || isAdmin)`. The `canSeePrivateDetails` flag controls whether `meetingUrl` and `locationDetails` (online meeting join link / password / in-person instructions) are included in the response.

## Attack path

**Attacker:** A user whose `isAdmin` flag was revoked in the database but who holds a still-valid JWT (up to \~30 days).

**Controlled input:** `GET /api/v1/events/:id` for any published event.

**Reach:** Any published event on the platform — the JWT-derived `isAdmin=true` sets `canSeePrivateDetails=true` and bypasses the registration/host check that normally gates meeting credentials.

**Concrete impact:**

* Meeting join links (`meetingUrl`) and location details (passwords, instructions) for **published** online/hybrid events are disclosed to a revoked admin who is not a registrant or host.
* This enables participation in, or disruption of, any live online event on the platform without having registered or paid.

Note: draft/unpublished event visibility is gated separately in `handleEventDetail` (`publishingStatus !== "published" && !isHost`) and does **not** consume `isAdmin`, so draft events are not additionally exposed via this path.

## Recommended remediation

Follow the [EXP-69](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-69/security-productionstaging-secrets-committed-to-git-role-revocation) / [EXP-78](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-78/security-jwt-role-staleness-in-event-thumbnail-upload-revoked-admin) / [EXP-84](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-84/security-jwt-role-staleness-in-event-list-revoked-admin-bypasses-host) / [EXP-85](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-85/security-jwt-role-staleness-in-event-clone-revoked-admin-bypasses-host) canonical fix pattern:

1. In the `GET` handler, after `auth()`, add a DB revalidation:

   ```ts
   const dbIsAdmin = session?.user?.id
     ? (await prisma.user.findUnique({
         where: { id: session.user.id },
         select: { isAdmin: true },
       }))?.isAdmin ?? false
     : false;
   ```
2. Pass `isAdmin: Boolean(dbIsAdmin)` to `handleEventDetail` instead of the JWT-derived value.
3. Add regression tests for: revoked-admin-stale-JWT path (meeting URL absent in response), DB-admin path (meeting URL present), and unauthenticated path (no change).

## Related prior issues

[EXP-69](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-69/security-productionstaging-secrets-committed-to-git-role-revocation) (approve/reject — resolved), [EXP-78](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-78/security-jwt-role-staleness-in-event-thumbnail-upload-revoked-admin) (thumbnail — resolved), [EXP-84](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-84/security-jwt-role-staleness-in-event-list-revoked-admin-bypasses-host) (POST /events — resolved), [EXP-85](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-85/security-jwt-role-staleness-in-event-clone-revoked-admin-bypasses-host) (clone — resolved). Same JWT staleness class, this surface exposes confidential meeting credentials.

## Verified by

security-auditor (primary), architecture-reviewer (verifier) — confirmed Medium severity; information disclosure limited to meeting credentials on published events.

*Filed by automated security routine on 2026-05-23T00:00:00Z.*

Title: [bug] Noon Order.name rejects special characters — checkout 403 on titles with brackets / punctuation
Description:
## R3 tracking

R3 fingerprint: `<!-- agent-fp: dbb4c82c9bf6 -->` — keep this marker in the body verbatim.

## Severity

**High** — blocks checkout for any course/event whose title starts with a non-letter character (brackets, digits, symbols). Live reproduction by user on 2026-05-24.

## Reproduction (production)

**Fails:**

* Title: `[TEST] Bond up the basics in Orthodontics` → Noon 403 `resultCode 5034` `Order.Name has invalid value`

**Succeeds** (confirmed by user probe 2026-05-24):

* Title: `Test [TEST] Bond up the basics in Orthodontics < > \ / | & ; : , * ? " ' ` \[ \] { } ( )\` → checkout succeeds

### Conclusion from the A/B

Noon's `Order.name` validator is **not** rejecting special characters in general. It's rejecting names whose **first character is not a letter**. Special characters elsewhere in the string pass through fine.

Failing request reference (for support trail): `b35494e6-63d0-4f08-9252-e974b8cd97d9` (test env, 2026-05-24).

## Root cause

`apps/experts-app/src/lib/payments/gateways/noon/noon.gateway.ts` derives `order.name` (and `items[].name`) from `input.description` — the raw event/course title. When the title starts with a non-letter character (very common: `[TEST]`, `2025 Workshop`, `#1 ...`, etc.), Noon's validator rejects the order with HTTP 403.

Four sites in `noon.gateway.ts`:

```ts
// line 77 (createNoonPaymentIntent — order.name)
name: input.description.trim().slice(0, 50).trim() || "Experts Order",

// line 82 (items[0].name — NO sanitization at all)
name: input.description,

// lines 193, 206 (subscription paths — same trim+slice pattern as line 77)
name: input.description.trim().slice(0, 50).trim() || "Experts Subscription",
```

Lines 77/193/206 already trim spaces (per the comment at line 76 about a prior bug) but do nothing about leading non-letter chars. Line 82 doesn't even trim.

## Suggested fix

Create `apps/experts-app/src/lib/payments/gateways/noon/noon.sanitize.ts`:

```ts
/**
 * Noon's Order.name validator (resultCode 5034 BusinessViolation) rejects names
 * whose first character is not a letter. Other special characters anywhere in
 * the name are accepted. This sanitizer guarantees the first char is a letter
 * by prepending a brand prefix when the input would otherwise start with
 * digits, brackets, or punctuation.
 *
 * Empirical evidence: user probe 2026-05-24 confirmed that a title containing
 * `[ ] { } ( ) < > \ / | & ; : , * ? " ' \` ` mid-string succeeds; the same
 * title leading with `[` fails. See Linear EXP-94.
 */
export function sanitizeNoonName(input: string | null | undefined, fallback: string): string {
  const trimmed = (input ?? "").normalize("NFC").trim();
  if (!trimmed) return fallback;

  // If first char is already a letter (any script), keep as-is.
  // Otherwise prepend the fallback prefix so the visible name remains useful:
  //   "[TEST] Bond..." → "Experts Order: [TEST] Bond..."
  const startsWithLetter = /^\p{L}/u.test(trimmed);
  const composed = startsWithLetter ? trimmed : `${fallback}: ${trimmed}`;

  return composed.slice(0, 50).trim() || fallback;
}
```

Apply at all 4 sites:

```ts
// createNoonPaymentIntent — line 77
name: sanitizeNoonName(input.description, "Experts Order"),
// items[0].name — line 82
items: [{name: sanitizeNoonName(input.description, "Experts Order"), quantity: 1, unitPrice: amount}],

// createNoonSubscription — line 193
name: sanitizeNoonName(input.description, "Experts Subscription"),
// updateNoonSubscription — line 206
name: sanitizeNoonName(input.description, "Experts Subscription"),
```

### Why prepend, not strip

Stripping `[TEST]` from a title silently loses information the merchant wants. Prepending `"Experts Order: "` keeps the original text intact, makes the brand visible at checkout, and only kicks in when the title would otherwise be rejected. The 50-char slice handles overflow.

Alternative considered: **left-trim leading non-letters** (`replace(/^[^\p{L}]+/u, "")`). Rejected because:

* A title like `[2025] Conference` becomes `Conference` — loses the year, may be ambiguous to the buyer
* A title like `[TEST] X` becomes `X` — drops the test marker that staff/QA rely on

## Test plan

`apps/experts-app/src/lib/payments/gateways/noon/__tests__/noon.sanitize.test.ts` (new):

 1. **Starts with letter, kept as-is** — input `Bond up the basics` → output `Bond up the basics`
 2. **Starts with bracket, fallback prepended** — input `[TEST] Bond up the basics in Orthodontics` → output starts with `Experts Order:` and stays ≤ 50 chars
 3. **Starts with digit, fallback prepended** — input `2025 Workshop` → output starts with `Experts Order: 2025`
 4. **Arabic starting letter preserved** — input `دورة [متقدمة] في تقويم الأسنان` → output equals input (Arabic letters match `\p{L}`)
 5. **All non-letter falls back** — input `[!!!]` → output `Experts Order: [!!!]` sliced to 50
 6. **Empty/null falls back** — input `""` / `null` / `undefined` → output equals fallback (`Experts Order` or `Experts Subscription`)
 7. **Long title slice safety** — input 200-char letter-starting string → output exactly 50 chars, no trailing space
 8. **Slice mid-prefix doesn't break first-letter rule** — input `[X]` with 47-char fallback → composed string sliced at 50 still starts with a letter (the fallback's first char)
 9. **Subscription fallback** — input `[TEST]` with fallback `"Experts Subscription"` → output starts with `Experts Subscription:`
10. **Whitespace-only input** — input `"   "` → output equals fallback (not `Experts Order:    `)

Plus integration assertion in `noon.gateway.test.ts`: calling `createNoonPaymentIntent` with `input.description = "[TEST] foo"` produces a payload where `order.name` and `items[0].name` both start with a letter.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `noon.sanitize.ts` exists with `sanitizeNoonName` and the docblock explaining the "first char must be a letter" rule + evidence
- [ ] All 4 sites in `noon.gateway.ts` route through `sanitizeNoonName`
- [ ] Items\[0\].name uses sanitized value (currently raw — that's the worse bug since it's never trimmed either)
- [ ] All 10 sanitizer test cases pass
- [ ] Integration assertion confirms `noonPost("/order", payload)` receives compliant `order.name` + `items[0].name`
- [ ] Live reproduction title `[TEST] Bond up the basics in Orthodontics` now produces a successful Noon order (validated via mock or real probe)
- [ ] `pnpm experts:check` + `pnpm experts:test` green from `$REPO_ROOT`

## Out of scope

* Probing further Noon validator rules (length, currency-specific edge cases, etc.) — fix is narrowly scoped to the documented "first letter" violation
* Re-sending the failed order with the new sanitization — failed at PSP boundary, no state to repair on our side
* Front-end UX hint about title prefixes appearing at checkout — file separately if useful
* Other PSP gateways (HyperPay, Moyasar, Tap, Tabby/Tamara) — each has its own validator; do NOT batch
* noon.client.ts, noon.webhook.handler.ts changes — fix is gateway-only

## Hard rules

* Do NOT strip characters from the middle/end of the title — user probe confirms those are accepted; stripping would silently lose info
* Do NOT change the 50-char limit — confirmed by existing slice logic and Noon's API contract
* Do NOT touch noon.client.ts or noon.webhook.handler.ts
* Do NOT bundle with any other gateway change

## Related

* [EXP-68](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-68/security-paid-content-bypass-forge-course-completioncertificate) — same file family (Noon gateway); credential rotation tracked in [EXP-74](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-74/security-urgent-rotate-noon-merchant-credentials-audit-log-retention)
* Line 76 comment in `noon.gateway.ts` already acknowledges Noon's strict `order.name` validation (trailing-space case) — this issue extends the defense to the leading-letter rule

## Verified by

Live A/B probe by user (test env, 2026-05-24):

* `[TEST] Bond up the basics in Orthodontics` → **fails** (resultCode 5034)
* `Test [TEST] Bond up the basics in Orthodontics < > \ / | & ; : , * ? " ' ` \[ \] { } ( )\` → **succeeds**

Single-variable difference (leading `[` vs leading `T`) isolates the constraint to "first char must be a letter."
Creator: loooogix@gmail.com

Title: [bug] Course/event title must start with a letter — block invalid titles at form + API validation (EXP-94 sibling)
Description:
## R3 tracking

R3 fingerprint: `<!-- agent-fp: 8eb4a2677146 -->` — keep this marker in the body verbatim.

**Status: Backlog.** Ready to flip to Todo any time after [EXP-94](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-94/bug-noon-ordername-rejects-special-characters-checkout-403-on-titles) ships (the gateway sanitization safety net should exist first; this is the UX-layer hardening on top).

## Source

Defense-in-depth pairing for [EXP-94](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-94/bug-noon-ordername-rejects-special-characters-checkout-403-on-titles). [EXP-94](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-94/bug-noon-ordername-rejects-special-characters-checkout-403-on-titles) fixes the Noon `Order.name` constraint at the gateway layer — if a title starts with a non-letter, the gateway prepends a brand prefix so checkout succeeds. That's the safety net.

This issue catches the bad title at the **input** layer, before it persists to DB: reject titles whose first non-whitespace character is not a letter, with a clear inline form error so the creator picks a usable title instead of discovering the issue at checkout time (where the sanitized name is what buyers see — `"Experts Order: [TEST] Foo"` is functional but ugly).

## Severity

**Medium** — not a bug-fix blocker ([EXP-94](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-94/bug-noon-ordername-rejects-special-characters-checkout-403-on-titles) already prevents the failure mode), but the right long-term shape:

* Better UX: error message at title-entry time, not after checkout fails
* Cleaner Noon checkout pages: real titles instead of fallback-prefixed compounds
* Reduces edge cases for the gateway sanitizer (less load on the safety net)

## Scope

Add a Zod refinement to every command schema that accepts a title destined for downstream PSP serialization. Verified files (audit again during research — the list below was a quick grep):

**Courses:**

* `apps/experts-app/src/lib/courses/catalog/commands/course-create.schema.ts`
* `apps/experts-app/src/lib/courses/catalog/commands/course-update.schema.ts`
* `apps/experts-app/src/lib/courses/catalog/forms/course-create.schema.ts` (frontend form schema if separate)

**Events:**

* `apps/experts-app/src/lib/events/commands/event-create.schema.ts`
* `apps/experts-app/src/lib/events/commands/event-update.schema.ts` (verify path)

**NOT in scope** (do not modify):

* Module/lesson schemas (titles there don't feed PSP [order.name](<http://order.name>); checkout uses the course/event title)
* Asset/attachment schemas (no PSP linkage)
* Any payments/PSP-side validators (handled by [EXP-94](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-94/bug-noon-ordername-rejects-special-characters-checkout-403-on-titles))

## Suggested fix

### Step 1 — Shared validator

Create `apps/experts-app/src/lib/shared/validators/title-starts-with-letter.ts`:

```ts
import {z} from "zod";

/**
 * Course and event titles flow into PSP order names (see Noon Order.name rule,
 * Linear EXP-94). Noon rejects names whose first non-whitespace char is not
 * a letter. Enforcing the constraint at the schema layer keeps titles human
 * and avoids the gateway-sanitizer fallback prefix appearing in checkout.
 *
 * `\p{L}` matches any Unicode letter (Latin, Arabic, CJK, etc.) — same rule
 * the gateway sanitizer uses.
 */
export function titleStartsWithLetter(message = "Title must start with a letter") {
  return z.string().refine(
    (val) => /^\s*\p{L}/u.test(val),
    {message},
  );
}
```

### Step 2 — Apply to course schemas

In `course-create.schema.ts` and `course-update.schema.ts`:

```ts
// Before:
title: z.string().min(1).max(100),

// After:
title: titleStartsWithLetter("Course title must start with a letter").pipe(z.string().min(1).max(100)),
```

Adjust according to existing schema composition (the example assumes simple `.pipe` chaining; match the file's actual style).

### Step 3 — Apply to event schemas

Same pattern for `event-create.schema.ts` and `event-update.schema.ts` with message `"Event title must start with a letter"`.

### Step 4 — Frontend form messages

The Zod refinement produces the error on form submit. Verify the React Hook Form + form-renderer wiring surfaces the message inline next to the title field. If a separate frontend form schema exists (e.g. `course-create.schema.ts` under `forms/`), apply the same refinement there so the validation fires on blur, not just on submit.

### Step 5 — i18n

If the messages are i18n'd (check existing pattern — likely `next-intl` keys), add Arabic + English variants:

* EN: `"Title must start with a letter"`
* AR: equivalent — work with i18n keys, do NOT hardcode Arabic strings in the validator

If i18n isn't applied to schema messages yet (verify; this is monorepo-specific), keep the English fallback and file a follow-up i18n ticket — do NOT introduce ad-hoc translation infrastructure here.

## Test plan

`apps/experts-app/src/lib/shared/validators/__tests__/title-starts-with-letter.test.ts` (new):

1. **Letter start → pass** — `"Bond up the basics"` → valid
2. **Arabic letter start → pass** — `"دورة"` → valid
3. **CJK letter start → pass** — `"課程"` → valid
4. **Leading whitespace then letter → pass** — `"  Bond"` → valid (whitespace-tolerant, matches gateway sanitizer's trim behavior)
5. **Bracket start → fail** — `"[TEST] Foo"` → error message returned
6. **Digit start → fail** — `"2025 Workshop"` → error
7. **Symbol start → fail** — `"#1 Conference"` → error
8. **Empty string → fail** — `""` → error (covered by existing min(1), but ensure refinement message is consistent)

Plus per-schema test in `course-create.schema.test.ts` and `event-create.schema.test.ts`: invalid title → schema parse fails with the new message; valid title → schema parse succeeds.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Shared validator helper exists with `titleStartsWithLetter` and the [EXP-94](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-94/bug-noon-ordername-rejects-special-characters-checkout-403-on-titles) docblock cross-reference
- [ ] Applied to course-create, course-update, event-create, event-update command schemas
- [ ] Frontend form schemas (if separate from command schemas) have the same refinement so inline errors surface

Title: [bug] cf-ipcountry header spoof bypasses Tabby KSA restriction when origin is reachable directly
Description:
R3 fingerprint: `<!-- agent-fp: 302812779a63 -->`.

## ⚠️ Reviewer notes — read before scoring (added after PR [logi-x/experts#461](https://linear.app/experts/review/fixpayments-add-cf-origin-secret-guard-to-tabby-ksa-eligibility-check-f527de78dc0b) BLOCK)

The previous attempt's security-auditor flagged "`CF_ORIGIN_SECRET` lives in plaintext in `.env.production` / `.env.staging`" as CRITICAL. **That concern is resolved and out of scope for this issue. Do NOT re-BLOCK on it.**

State as of 2026-05-25:

1. `.env.production` and `.env.staging` no longer exist on any developer machine; have always been git-ignored (`.gitignore:39-40`); never in repo history.
2. On VPS `experts-server`, both `docker/{production,staging}/.env` files are mode `600`, owner `logix` (only the container-runtime user can read them).
3. All keys exposed in the prior files have been rotated.
4. **Threat boundary clarified.** `CF_ORIGIN_SECRET` defends against an outside attacker who finds the origin IP and tries to forge a Cloudflare-origin header. It is NOT designed to defend against an attacker with container filesystem read — at that point every secret is already lost regardless. The fix achieves its actual stated goal.

Going beyond mode-600 host-only env files (Docker secrets, Doppler, Vault) is an **org-wide secrets-management decision**, not gated by this PR. If that hardening is wanted, file a separate issue.

**In scope for this fix:** HMAC honest construction (use `createHash("sha256")` + `timingSafeEqual` of equal-length digests, not HMAC-with-public-key), `vi.stubEnv("NODE_ENV", ...)` in tests, un-export or rename `getCloudflareCountryCode`, startup warning when `CF_ORIGIN_SECRET` is absent. EXP-109 (subscription checkout Spanish locale) stays out of scope.

---

## Trigger scenario

**Precondition:** The origin server's IP address is reachable directly (not firewall-restricted to Cloudflare IP ranges). Origin IPs commonly leak via historical DNS records, certificate transparency logs, or email headers from server-sent mail.

1. Attacker (located outside KSA, e.g., Egypt) discovers the origin IP via DNS history or CT log lookup.
2. Attacker sends a request directly to the origin, spoofing the `Host` header and injecting `cf-ipcountry: SA`:

```http
GET https://<origin-ip>/api/v1/payments/eligibility
Host: experts.example.com
cf-ipcountry: SA
```

3. `getCloudflareCountryCode(request.headers)` reads the injected header, validates it against `/^[A-Z]{2}$/`, and returns `"SA"`. `resolveTabbyEligibility()` returns `{available: true, source: "cloudflare"}`.
4. Response: `{"tabby": {"available": true, "source": "cloudflare"}}`. Restriction bypassed at the eligibility endpoint.
5. Attacker proceeds to enroll with Tabby:

```http
POST https://<origin-ip>/api/v1/courses/<id>/enroll
Host: experts.example.com
cf-ipcountry: SA
Content-Type: application/json
Authorization: Bearer <valid-token>

{"provider": "tabby", ...}
```

6. The server-side eligibility check in the enroll route also calls `getCloudflareCountryCode()` with the same spoofed header, returns `available: true`, and proceeds to `handleCourseEnroll()`. The Tabby gateway receives the request.

**Why Cloudflare doesn't protect this:** Cloudflare strips/overwrites user-supplied `cf-ipcountry` headers on traffic that flows through its proxy — but only on traffic that actually flows through Cloudflare. When the request bypasses Cloudflare entirely (direct origin access), no sanitization occurs.

The same bypass applies to `POST /api/v1/events/[id]/register`.

## Root cause

`getCloudflareCountryCode()` in `payment-eligibility.ts` trusts the `cf-ipcountry` header without verifying that the request arrived through Cloudflare. The KSA enforcement in all three routes (`/eligibility`, `/enroll`, `/register`) depends solely on this header check with a billing-address DB fallback. If the origin is directly accessible, the entire enforcement mechanism is bypassable with a one-line HTTP header.

## Fix sketch

**Primary (infrastructure):** Restrict the origin server's inbound connections to [Cloudflare's published IP ranges](<https://www.cloudflare.com/ips/>) via firewall/security group rules. This is the canonical Cloudflare recommendation and eliminates the attack surface completely. Verify this is in place for all environments (staging is often more permissive).

**Secondary (defense in depth):** Add a shared secret header injected by a Cloudflare Transform Rule (e.g., `x-cf-origin-secret: <secret>`) that the application validates before trusting any `cf-*` headers. Requests missing the secret are rejected at the application layer even if they arrive on port 443. This protects against IP-range updates that lag Cloudflare's published list.

**Application-layer note:** Even if the Tabby gateway itself rejects non-KSA payments (as the gateway terms-of-service imply), relying solely on the gateway for enforcement means failed payment attempts are recorded on both sides (wasted gateway calls, potential rate-limit consumption, confusing error states for users). Server-side infrastructure-backed enforcement is the correct boundary.

Related prior issues: [logi-x/experts#438](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-96/bug-restrict-tabby-checkout-to-ksa-traffic-only) (Tabby KSA restriction implementation — same code, infrastructure gap not in scope of that issue).  
Verified by: qa-tester (primary, researched Cloudflare docs), code-reviewer, workflow-orchestrator (parallel scan 2026-05-24).

*Filed by automated bug-finder routine on 2026-05-24T08:00:00Z.*

Title: [security] Production PostgreSQL (5432) and Redis (6379) bound to 0.0.0.0 — database ports publicly reachable, bypassing any OS firewall
Description:
R3 fingerprint: `<!-- agent-fp: a37207aca1c4 -->`.

## Severity

**High**

## Location

`docker/production/docker-compose.yml` — services `experts-prd-postgres` and `experts-prd-redis`, introduced in PR #436 (Cleanup/docker remote branch, merged 2026-05-24).

```yaml
experts-prd-postgres:
  ports:
    - 5432:5432   # binds to 0.0.0.0:5432 — no loopback restriction

experts-prd-redis:
  ports:
    - 6379:6379   # binds to 0.0.0.0:6379 — no loopback restriction
```

## Attack path

**Attacker:** Any external actor who can reach the production server's IP on port 5432 or 6379 — or any co-tenant on a shared cloud host network.

**Controlled input:** TCP connection to host:5432 or host:6379; attacker controls authentication credentials.

**Reachable path:** TCP → host IP:5432/6379 → Docker DNAT → `experts-prd-postgres:5432` / `experts-prd-redis:6379`.

**Critical escalation factor — Docker bypasses UFW/iptables:** Docker inserts its own iptables rules above the `FORWARD` chain used by UFW. A host-level `ufw deny 5432` rule has **no effect** on these ports. The only protection is the database password itself.

**Concrete impact:**

* **PostgreSQL:** Brute-force or credential stuffing against POSTGRES_PASSWORD. Compromise yields full read/write access to all application data: user PII, course/event content, payment records, subscription history, enrollment status, certifications.
* **Redis:** Redis `--requirepass` has no built-in rate limiting or account lockout. Brute force at line speed is feasible. Compromise gives: BullMQ job injection/drain (arbitrary background job execution), session store manipulation if sessions are Redis-backed, pub/sub eavesdropping on realtime workers.

**Why** `experts-shared-network` **does not help:** The `external: true` network setting controls intra-container routing only. Docker's `ports:` directive operates at the host kernel level via iptables, independently of any Docker network configuration.

**Why the ports stanza is unnecessary:** All application services (app, workers, realtime, cron) reach postgres and redis by container name within `experts-shared-network`. No intra-application consumer requires host-published ports. The `ports:` stanzas serve only ops tooling and should be removed or restricted.

## Recommended remediation

**Option A (preferred):** Remove the `ports:` stanzas from `experts-prd-postgres` and `experts-prd-redis` entirely. All consumers communicate via Docker DNS on `experts-shared-network`.

**Option B (if ops tooling requires external access):** Restrict to loopback only and use SSH tunnels for remote DBA access:

```yaml
ports:
  - "127.0.0.1:5432:5432"
  - "127.0.0.1:6379:6379"
```

Apply the same fix to `docker/staging/docker-compose.yml` (staging uses 5433:5432 and 6378:6379, same 0.0.0.0 exposure).

## Related prior issues

* [EXP-69](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-69/security-productionstaging-secrets-committed-to-git-role-revocation): production secrets committed to git (resolved) — same `docker/` path class
* none directly matching port exposure

## Verified by

* security-auditor (primary)
* architecture-reviewer (confirmed HIGH; noted Docker iptables bypass of UFW)
* platform-operator (confirmed HIGH; independently identified same issue)

*Filed by automated security routine on 2026-05-24T06:30:00Z.*

Title: [security] redis:latest unversioned image tag in production — unpinned image allows silent version drift and supply-chain risk
Description:
R3 fingerprint: `<!-- agent-fp: 4e6bbe0deae2 -->`.

## Severity

**High**

## Location

`docker/production/docker-compose.yml` and `docker/staging/docker-compose.yml` — service `experts-prd-redis` / `experts-stg-redis`, introduced in PR #436 (merged 2026-05-24).

```yaml
experts-prd-redis:
  image: redis:latest   # ← no version pin
```

## Attack path

**Attacker:** Supply-chain threat actor who compromises Docker Hub's `redis:latest` tag, or an adversary exploiting an unpatched CVE that lands in the production image transparently.

**Controlled input:** Docker image content at pull time — any `docker pull redis:latest` or container restart may fetch a different image without any review.

**Reachable path:** `docker compose pull` / `docker compose up --pull always` during deployment silently upgrades to whatever `redis:latest` resolves to at that moment. The new image executes as the Redis service with full access to `experts-shared-network`, BullMQ queues, and any session data in Redis.

**Concrete impact:**

1. **Silent major version upgrades:** `latest` currently resolves to Redis 7.x but will silently move to Redis 8.x or beyond on next pull. Breaking changes, deprecated AUTH behaviour, or configuration incompatibilities may surface unexpectedly in production.
2. **CVE exposure without visibility:** Redis 7.x has had notable CVEs including heap buffer overflows (CVE-2022-35977), integer overflow leading to heap corruption (CVE-2023-36824), and use-after-free issues. Without a pinned version, it is impossible to answer "which Redis version is in production?" — this slows CVE response from hours to days.
3. **Supply-chain compromise:** A compromised `redis:latest` tag on Docker Hub (via credential theft, maintainer account takeover, or Docker Hub infrastructure incident) would be pulled silently on next deploy, executing arbitrary code inside the Redis container. This container shares `experts-shared-network` with the app, all workers, and the database, giving an attacker lateral movement capability.

## Recommended remediation

Pin to a specific minor-version tag with an Alpine variant for reduced attack surface:

```yaml
experts-prd-redis:
  image: redis:7.4-alpine   # or pin to specific digest for CI reproducibility
```

For maximum supply-chain hardness, pin to a digest:

```yaml
image: redis:7.4-alpine@sha256:<digest>
```

Document the pinned version in a comment so operators know when to upgrade intentionally.

Apply consistently across production and staging compose files. Add a periodic Dependabot or Renovate rule to keep the pin current.

## Related prior issues

none — first filing for this class

## Verified by

* platform-operator (primary; flagged as HIGH with CVE context)
* architecture-reviewer (confirmed; noted inability to audit specific running version)

*Filed by automated security routine on 2026-05-24T06:30:00Z.*

Five routes still call `enforceStorageQuota` (the old aggregate-based, TOCTOU-prone quota gate) instead of the atomic reservation pattern shipped in [EXP-80](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-80/security-race-safe-storage-usage-ledger-userstorageusage-reservation):

Title: [spinoff: EXP-100] Route-level cf-ipcountry pre-check skips DB billing-address lookup when CF header is spoofed
Description:
'<!-- agent-fp: `35df1c2cf320` -->
<!-- spinoff-parent: [EXP-100](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-100/bug-cf-ipcountry-header-spoof-bypasses-tabby-ksa-restriction-when) -->
<!-- spinoff-origin: R3 -->

## Trigger

Four routes call `getCloudflareCountryCode(request.headers)` before `resolveTabbyEligibility` to decide whether to fetch the billing address from DB:

* `apps/experts-app/app/api/v1/payments/eligibility/route.ts:11-18`
* `apps/experts-app/app/api/v1/commerce/subscriptions/checkout/route.ts:43-50`
* `apps/experts-app/app/api/v1/events/[id]/register/route.ts:102-109`
* `apps/experts-app/app/api/v1/courses/[id]/enroll/route.ts:53-60`

When `CF_ORIGIN_SECRET` is configured ([EXP-100](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-100/bug-cf-ipcountry-header-spoof-bypasses-tabby-ksa-restriction-when) fix) and an attacker spoofs `cf-ipcountry: SA` without a valid `x-cf-origin-secret`, `getCloudflareCountryCode` still returns `"SA"` (it reads the raw header with no trust check). The route then skips the DB billing-address lookup. `resolveTabbyEligibility` is called with `billingCountryCode = null` — the trust guard correctly rejects the spoofed header, but the billing-address fallback is also lost. A legitimate Saudi user who hits the origin directly (ISP bypass, CF outage) gets `country_unknown` instead of `available: true` via their billing address.

## What this fixes

Restructure all four routes to always fetch the billing address first, then pass it to `resolveTabbyEligibility` regardless of raw CF header presence. The trust decision is made inside `resolveTabbyEligibility` — the routes should not pre-empt it by skipping the DB call.

## Why this is agent-fixable

Mechanical change in 4 route files: remove the `!cloudflareCountryCode` guard on the DB lookup, always `await prisma.address.findUnique(...)` when user is authenticated. Add one integration test per route asserting that a spoofed-CF + valid-billing-address SA user still gets `available: true, source: "billing_address"`. One R3 cycle, no schema changes.

## Out of scope for THIS issue

* Changes to `payment-eligibility.ts` (done in [EXP-100](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-100/bug-cf-ipcountry-header-spoof-bypasses-tabby-ksa-restriction-when))
* Adding `isTrustedCloudflareRequest` to the public API (not needed if routes delegate to `resolveTabbyEligibility`)
* Performance optimization of the DB call (always fetching costs one extra query per request when CF header is present; acceptable tradeoff)
Creator: loooogix@gmail.com

Title: [security] checkAndSendStorageAlerts has no cron trigger — 90% quota alert tier is dead code in production
Description:
R3 fingerprint: `<!-- agent-fp: 330727080a5f -->`.

## Severity

**Medium**

## Location

`apps/experts-app/src/lib/storage/storage-alert.ts` — `checkAndSendStorageAlerts()` function, introduced in PR #458 ([EXP-82](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-82/bug-admin-storage-dashboard-over-quota-alerts)).
`docker/production/docker-compose.yml` and `docker/staging/docker-compose.yml` — `experts-prd-cron` / `experts-stg-cron` command block.

## Description

PR #458 added `checkAndSendStorageAlerts()` as a designed compensating control: it finds users at ≥ 90% of their storage quota and inserts a `StorageAlert` dedup row so they (or admins) can be notified before hitting the hard quota wall. The function is correctly implemented and has unit tests.

However, the cron sidecar in both the production and staging docker-compose files has **no entry** that invokes this function. The full set of cron entries as of 2026-05-25:

```
*/2  * * * *  ...ai/embeddings/sync
0    * * * *  ...recommendations/profiles/refresh
15   * * * *  ...ai/knowledge/sync
*/5  * * * *  ...storage-janitor/sweep
0  */6 * * *  ...storage-janitor/orphan-sweep
*/15 * * * *  ...reservation-cleanup
0    3 * * *  ...payments/reconcile/batch
```

No entry calls a route that invokes `checkAndSendStorageAlerts()`. There is also no HTTP route wired to it (the function is only called from within `storage-alert.test.ts` unit tests and the `metrics/route.ts` import of the module, but the route does not call it).

Consequences:

1. **The 90% alert tier never fires.** Users exhaust their quota without any warning signal.
2. **The quota enforcement relies solely on the hard-limit reservation ledger (**[EXP-80](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-80/security-race-safe-storage-usage-ledger-userstorageusage-reservation)**).** The designed early-warning layer is silently absent.
3. **The** `StorageAlert` **dedup table never receives rows in production**, so operators cannot distinguish "user was never close to quota" from "alerts fired and were sent."
4. **The notification delivery layer (spun off as** [EXP-106](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-106/spinoff-exp-82-wire-storage-quota-warning-notifications-into)**) has no surface to trigger.** Until `checkAndSendStorageAlerts()` is invoked, [EXP-106](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-106/spinoff-exp-82-wire-storage-quota-warning-notifications-into)'s implementation is also a no-op.

## Attacker → Input → Reach → Impact

Not a direct attacker-controlled vector; this is a security control gap.

* **Path:** User uploads files → approaches 90% quota → no alert fires → user continues uploading → hits hard quota wall with no warning → upload failures from user perspective, no notification to admin or user.
* **Secondary impact:** If the hard-limit reservation ledger ([EXP-80](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-80/security-race-safe-storage-usage-ledger-userstorageusage-reservation)) has any edge-case failure or the janitor is delayed, there is no out-of-band notification safety net to catch quota drift.

## Recommended Remediation

1. **Create a cron API route** at `app/api/v1/internal/storage-alerts/check/route.ts` using the same CRON_SECRET timing-safe guard pattern as `reservation-cleanup/route.ts`. The route should call `checkAndSendStorageAlerts()` and return the list of newly-alerted user IDs.
2. **Wire the route into the cron sidecar** (both production and staging docker-compose):

```yaml
0 */4 * * * curl -fsS -X POST -H "Authorization: Bearer $${CRON_SECRET}" \
  "$${APP_BASE_URL}/api/v1/internal/storage-alerts/check" \
  >/proc/1/fd/1 2>/proc/1/fd/2
```

3. **Connect** [EXP-106](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-106/spinoff-exp-82-wire-storage-quota-warning-notifications-into) (notification delivery layer) once the route exists.

## Related prior issues

none

## Verified by

security-auditor (primary), architecture-reviewer (verifier)

*Filed by automated security routine on 2026-05-25T10:00:00Z.*
Creator: loooogix@gmail.com

Title: [spinoff: EXP-113] StorageAlert createMany dedup race — no unique constraint or skipDuplicates guard
Description:
'<!-- agent-fp: `49154b8b9114` -->
<!-- spinoff-parent: [EXP-113](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-113/security-checkandsendstoragealerts-has-no-cron-trigger-90percent-quota) -->
<!-- spinoff-origin: R3 -->

## Trigger

`apps/experts-app/src/lib/storage/storage-alert.ts:30-43` — `findMany` dedup check + `createMany` are not wrapped in a transaction and `createMany` has no `skipDuplicates: true`.

## What this fixes

Two concurrent cron executions (possible on redeploy or manual trigger) can both pass the `findMany` dedup check and both insert a `StorageAlert` row for the same user within the same 7-day window. The alert is not re-sent, but the dedup table accumulates duplicate rows and the `(userId, sentAt)` window logic becomes unreliable. There is also no unique DB constraint on `(userId)` within the dedup window.

## Why this is agent-fixable

Add `skipDuplicates: true` to the `createMany` call (or add a unique partial index on `userId` where `sentAt >= NOW() - INTERVAL '7 days'`) plus a test confirming idempotent double-call does not insert duplicate rows. One file change + one migration + one test.

## Out of scope for THIS issue

* Changing the 7-day dedup window duration.
* Notification delivery layer.

Title: [spinoff: EXP-113] Cron route timing-safe comparison leaks secret length via length-check short-circuit (all internal cron routes)
Description:
'<!-- agent-fp: `bcefb2624c8b` -->
<!-- spinoff-parent: [EXP-113](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-113/security-checkandsendstoragealerts-has-no-cron-trigger-90percent-quota) -->
<!-- spinoff-origin: R3 -->

## Trigger

Codebase-wide: `rg "a.length === b.length && crypto.timingSafeEqual" apps/experts-app/app/api/v1/internal/` — matches `reservation-cleanup/route.ts`, `storage-alerts/check/route.ts`, and any other cron routes using this pattern.

## What this fixes

The `a.length === b.length` pre-check before `crypto.timingSafeEqual` short-circuits when lengths differ, creating a timing side-channel that leaks the byte-length of `CRON_SECRET` (an attacker can binary-search the secret length from response-time differences between equal-length and different-length token submissions). The standard mitigation is to pad the attacker-supplied buffer to the expected length before calling `timingSafeEqual`, eliminating the length oracle.

## Why this is agent-fixable

Replace the length short-circuit pattern with a constant-length comparison in all affected cron routes: pad the incoming buffer to `expected.length`, then call `timingSafeEqual` unconditionally. Update auth tests in each route to confirm a length-mismatched token still returns 403. One helper function + apply across all affected routes + tests.

## Out of scope for THIS issue

* Rotating `CRON_SECRET` values.
* Changes to non-cron authenticated routes.
Creator: loooogix@gmail.com
