Title: [bug] POST /community/posts/{id}/comments has no rate limit — authenticated spam exhausts DB, notifications, and BullMQ
Description:
R3 fingerprint: `<!-- agent-fp: af2656e7df89 -->`.

## Trigger scenario

1. Authenticate as any valid user (session cookie / bearer token).
2. In a tight loop, send `POST /api/v1/community/posts/<TARGET_POST_UUID>/comments` with `{"content": "<4999-char string>"}`.
3. Every request returns 201 — there is no rate-limit guard.

**Concrete effect:**

* Each comment writes a `Comment` row (\~5 KB), fires a Redis feed event, runs `activityHelpers.trackCommentAdded`, invokes `createNotification` for the post author, and triggers a mention-scan query.
* After 200 comments the `COMMENT_FETCH_CAP` fills; older comments become permanently invisible while new spam rows continue to accumulate in the DB with no server-side brake.
* The target post author's notification inbox is flooded and the BullMQ worker queue is saturated.

Reproduction rate: **100%** — no timing, no concurrency required.

## Root cause

`POST /api/v1/community/posts/route.ts` (post creation) was given a sliding-window rate limit in the same PR (`enforcePostCreationRateLimit`: 10/min, 100/day per user). The sibling `POST /api/v1/community/posts/[id]/comments/route.ts` (comment creation) received input validation (`createCommentSchema`, content ≤ 5000 chars) but no corresponding rate-limit guard. The shared `enforceSlidingWindowRateLimit` infrastructure and `RateLimitResult` type are already imported in the same package and could be wired in directly.

## Suggested fix

Add `enforceCommentCreationRateLimit` (mirrors `post-rate-limit.guard.ts`) called immediately after the `session.user.id` guard:

```ts
// apps/experts-app/src/lib/community/guards/comment-rate-limit.guard.ts
export async function enforceCommentCreationRateLimit(userId: string) {
  return enforceSlidingWindowRateLimit(
    [
      {key: `comment:create:rl:short:${userId}`, count: 20, windowSeconds: 60},
      {key: `comment:create:rl:long:${userId}`, count: 200, windowSeconds: 86400},
    ],
    {error: "Too many comments. Please slow down.", failOpenLabel: "Comment creation rate limit"},
  );
}
```

Related prior issues: [EXP-290](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-290/security-community-comments-post-no-input-validation-allows-unbounded)

Verified by: code-reviewer (static analysis), qa-tester (trigger scenario confirmed — 100% reproducible)

Title: [bug] GET /posts/{id}/comments silently truncates beyond 200 — no hasMore signal, oldest comments permanently inaccessible
Description:
R3 fingerprint: `<!-- agent-fp: 00ccd3cef33a -->`.

## Trigger scenario

1. A post accumulates 201 or more comments (achievable organically on active posts or via Bug af2656e7df89 rate-limit bypass).
2. Client fetches `GET /api/v1/community/posts/<POST_UUID>/comments`.
3. The handler executes:

   ```ts
   const comments = await prisma.comment.findMany({
     where: {postId},
     orderBy: {createdAt: "desc"},
     take: COMMENT_FETCH_CAP,   // = 200
   });
   ```
4. Response: bare array of exactly 200 items (the 200 newest comments). Comment #1 (the oldest) is absent. The response contains **no** `hasMore`, `totalCount`, `truncated`, or pagination fields — clients cannot detect the truncation.
5. `GET /api/v1/community/posts/<POST_UUID>` returns `commentsCount: 201` (live count), so the discrepancy is detectable by comparing endpoints but is unexplained to the client.

Oldest comments are permanently unreachable — the endpoint has no pagination interface.

Reproduction rate: **100%** given ≥ 201 comments on a post.

## Root cause

`COMMENT_FETCH_CAP = 200` was introduced in this PR as the [EXP-289](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-289/security-community-comments-get-unbounded-findmany-with-no-cache-read) fix (unbounded `findMany` → OOM DoS). The cap correctly bounds memory use, but the response schema was not updated to expose truncation metadata. The `GET /posts/[id]` (post detail) response exposes `commentsCount` from a live `prisma.comment.count` query, making the discrepancy machine-detectable but not surfaced to the caller of the comments endpoint itself.

## Suggested fix

Wrap the response in an envelope (breaking change: coordinate with any mobile clients):

```ts
return NextResponse.json({
  comments: commentsData,
  totalComments: await prisma.comment.count({where: {postId}}),
  hasMore: totalComments > COMMENT_FETCH_CAP,
});
```

Or add a non-breaking `X-Has-More: true` header as a short-term signal while a pagination interface is designed.

Related prior issues: [EXP-298](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-298/security-get-postsidcomments-has-no-post-visibility-check-moderated), [EXP-289](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-289/security-community-comments-get-unbounded-findmany-with-no-cache-read)

Verified by: qa-tester (trigger scenario confirmed — 100% reproducible at ≥ 201 comments)

Title: [bug] exam reset POST non-atomic — concurrent student submission in gap window silently corrupts in-flight exam data
Description:
R3 fingerprint: `<!-- agent-fp: f9ce9ef4c26c -->`.

## Trigger scenario

The exam reset handler at `apps/experts-app/app/api/v1/creator/courses/[id]/exams/[examId]/reset/route.ts` performs two sequential (non-transactional) deletes:

```ts
await prisma.courseExamAnswer.deleteMany({   // ← Delete 1
  where: {attempt: {examId: exam.id, userId: targetUserId}},
});
await prisma.courseExamAttempt.deleteMany({  // ← Delete 2
  where: {examId: exam.id, userId: targetUserId},
});
```

**Race path:**

1. Instructor calls `POST .../exams/<examId>/reset` with `{userId: <studentId>}`.
2. Delete 1 executes — all existing `CourseExamAnswer` rows for the student are removed.
3. **Before Delete 2 executes**, the student concurrently submits a new exam answer: a fresh `CourseExamAnswer` row is inserted referencing the still-live `CourseExamAttempt`.
4. Delete 2 executes — the `CourseExamAttempt` is deleted. The student's newly written `CourseExamAnswer` now has a FK pointing at a deleted attempt.
5. Depending on Prisma cascade settings: the new answer either becomes an orphan (permanent data loss — no FK enforced) or triggers a cascade delete (student's in-flight submission silently wiped with no error returned to them).

The inverse race is also possible: if a new attempt is inserted between the deletes, Delete 2 removes it and a subsequent answer insert fails or orphans.

## Root cause

The two `deleteMany` calls are sequential but not wrapped in `prisma.$transaction([...])`. The gap between them (a round-trip to Postgres) is the race window. Wrapping both in a single transaction eliminates the window entirely and serializes the reset against any concurrent student submission.

## Suggested fix

```ts
await prisma.$transaction([
  prisma.courseExamAnswer.deleteMany({
    where: {attempt: {examId: exam.id, userId: targetUserId}},
  }),
  prisma.courseExamAttempt.deleteMany({
    where: {examId: exam.id, userId: targetUserId},
  }),
]);
```

Note: answers must be deleted before attempts to satisfy FK ordering (same as current order — no change needed).

Related prior issues: [EXP-276](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-276/bug-exam-reset-post-does-not-validate-examid-belongs-to-courseid-bola)

Verified by: code-reviewer (concurrent-request race analysis)

Title: [security] Residual git option injection in migration-immutability — git fetch missing -- separator after EXP-279 fix
Description:
R3 fingerprint: `<!-- agent-fp: e2fb255d8f59 -->`.

**Severity**: Medium

**File**: `.github/workflows/experts-app.yml` — `migration-immutability` job, step "Block edits to already-merged migrations"

**Attacker**: Any GitHub user who can open a pull request against logi-x/experts (i.e., any GitHub user with a fork, no collaborator access required).

**Controlled input**: The PR base branch name, exposed as `$GH_BASE_REF` after the [EXP-279](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-279/security-github-actions-workflow-injection-via-dollar-githubbase-ref) fix.

**Reachable path**:

The [EXP-279](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-279/security-github-actions-workflow-injection-via-dollar-githubbase-ref) fix (commit `f6e9b779`) correctly moved `${{ github.base_ref }}` into a step-level `env:` variable and double-quoted it in the bash script, eliminating shell word-splitting injection. However, the corrected step runs:

```bash
base="$GH_BASE_REF"
git fetch --no-tags origin "$base"
```

without a `--` separator between `origin` and the refspec. `git fetch` processes its own `argv[]` after bash completes. Git's option parser treats any argument beginning with `--` as an option flag, not as a refspec — **double-quoting prevents bash word-splitting but does not affect git's own option parsing**. A PR opened from a branch named `--upload-pack=/bin/sh` (GitHub permits branch names beginning with `--` via the git protocol) passes through to git as:

```
git fetch --no-tags origin --upload-pack=/bin/sh
```

This instructs git to exec `/bin/sh` as the remote pack-protocol handler on the `changes-detector` self-hosted runner, achieving RCE on the runner process.

**Impact**: Remote code execution on the persistent `changes-detector` self-hosted runner. Exploitability is lower than [EXP-279](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-279/security-github-actions-workflow-injection-via-dollar-githubbase-ref) (requires a `--upload-pack`-style branch name rather than simple shell metacharacters, and depends on GitHub permitting such a push), but the impact if exploited is identical to [EXP-279](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-279/security-github-actions-workflow-injection-via-dollar-githubbase-ref): exfiltration of co-located secrets including `EXPERTS_APP_DATABASE_URL` and `DOCKERHUB_TOKEN`.

**Recommended remediation**: Add `--` before the refspec argument:

```yaml
git fetch --no-tags origin -- "$base"
```

The `--` forces git to treat everything following it as non-option arguments (refspecs/remote names), regardless of their content. This is the canonical hardening against git argument injection and closes the remaining vector left by the [EXP-279](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-279/security-github-actions-workflow-injection-via-dollar-githubbase-ref) env-variable fix.

Related prior issues: [EXP-279](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-279/security-github-actions-workflow-injection-via-dollar-githubbase-ref)

Verified by: architecture-reviewer

Title: [payments] Tabby geo-block leaves paid-but-not-enrolled — no refund/void on verify-403 or webhook block
Description:
R3 fingerprint: `<!-- agent-fp: b4961bb859ea -->`

## Severity

Medium — money-without-fulfillment. Surfaced by the payments-specialist + security-auditor review of [EXP-129](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-129/security-tabby-verify-and-webhook-paths-bypass-ksa-geo-restriction) (PR for the Tabby KSA geo re-check at completion).

## Problem

[EXP-129](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-129/security-tabby-verify-and-webhook-paths-bypass-ksa-geo-restriction) added a Tabby KSA geo re-check on the completion paths (`courses/[id]/enroll/verify`, `events/[id]/register/verify`, and the Tabby webhook). When the buyer is non-SA, completion is now blocked:

* **Verify handlers** return **403** before `orchestrateCourseEnrollmentComplete` / `handleEventRegistrationComplete`.
* **Webhook** (`tabby.webhook.handler.ts`) marks the event `*_geo_blocked` and returns `{handled: true, status: "success"}` (HTTP 200, so Tabby does not retry).

By the time either path runs, **Tabby has typically already authorized/captured the payment** (the webhook handler itself calls `captureTabbyPayment` before the geo-block branch). So a blocked buyer is left **paid but not enrolled**, with:

* no automated refund/void,
* no internal ledger event for the (eventual, manual) PSP-side refund,
* recovery only via a human reading the `tabby.webhook.geo_blocked` / `course.payment.tabby_geo_blocked` telemetry.

## Why this was deferred from [EXP-129](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-129/security-tabby-verify-and-webhook-paths-bypass-ksa-geo-restriction)

[EXP-129](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-129/security-tabby-verify-and-webhook-paths-bypass-ksa-geo-restriction)'s scope was to *enforce the business rule* (block completion). The remediation (refund vs. void vs. support-queue, and the corresponding ledger entry) is a **product + ops decision**, not a mechanical code change — so it was tracked here rather than bundled.

## Suggested direction (needs product/ops sign-off)

1. On geo-block, issue a Tabby refund/void (counterpart to `captureTabbyPayment`) and record a ledger reversal, instead of silently leaving the capture.
2. Consider moving the webhook geo-check **before** `captureTabbyPayment` so funds are never captured for an ineligible buyer in the first place.
3. Add an alert on `*.tabby_geo_blocked` events (especially `source === "none"`) so false-rejects and paid-but-not-enrolled cases surface immediately.
4. Confirm operationally that the Cloudflare `x-cf-origin-secret` header survives the Tabby return redirect in production (otherwise SA buyers with no stored address fail closed on verify).

## Related

[EXP-129](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-129/security-tabby-verify-and-webhook-paths-bypass-ksa-geo-restriction) (the geo re-check this gap stems from), [EXP-123](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-123/bug-payment-eligibility-cf-origin-secret-absent-on-non-production-env) (CF_ORIGIN_SECRET absent on non-prod — the checkout bypass precondition), [EXP-100](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-100/bug-cf-ipcountry-header-spoof-bypasses-tabby-ksa-restriction-when) (cf-ipcountry spoof — resolved).

*Filed from the* [EXP-129](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-129/security-tabby-verify-and-webhook-paths-bypass-ksa-geo-restriction) *payments + security review, 2026-06-04.*

Title: [spinoff: EXP-129] Tabby verify routes: no auth() check allows unauthenticated triggering of another user's enrollment completion
Description:
`<!-- agent-fp: f83653187ae89f3f870875e47907e8dfc289addb -->`
<!-- spinoff-parent: [EXP-129](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-129/security-tabby-verify-and-webhook-paths-bypass-ksa-geo-restriction) -->
<!-- spinoff-origin: R5 -->

## Trigger

`apps/experts-app/app/api/v1/courses/[id]/enroll/verify/route.ts` (Tabby branch, no auth() call)
`apps/experts-app/app/api/v1/events/[id]/register/verify/route.ts` (Tabby branch, no auth() call)

Neither verify route calls `auth()`. The Tabby branch looks up the enrollment solely by `providerRef` (the Tabby `payment_id`). An attacker who obtains another user's `payment_id` can call the verify URL without authenticating, triggering enrollment completion side-effects (orchestration, DB state, observability events) for that user's pending payment.

## What this fixes

Pre-existing unauthenticated access to state-mutation endpoints: add `auth()` at the entry of both verify routes, assert that `session.user.id === enrollment.userId` (for courses) and `session.user.id === registration.userId` (for events) before proceeding, and return 401/403 on mismatch.

## Why this is agent-fixable

Pattern already exists in the checkout initiation routes and other protected handlers; adding `auth()` + userId ownership assertion is a one-cycle mechanical addition with test coverage.

## Out of scope for THIS issue

Do not add auth to the Stripe or Noon branches in these routes (separate concern); do not change the webhook handler (webhook is server-to-server, no session auth applies).
Creator: loooogix@gmail.com

Title: [bug] Creator "Create & Build Curriculum" fails with 409 — create→submit collides with create-time auto-approve
Description:
R3 fingerprint: `<!-- agent-fp: 91e0682cf457 -->`.

## Severity

High — primary creator flow is broken in production. Clicking **"Create & Build Curriculum"** on `/creator/courses/create` fails with `Failed to create course. Please try again.` ("Save Draft" works).

## Symptom

```
POST /api/v1/courses/<id>/submit 409  {"error":"Course is already approved"}
[browser] Error: Failed to submit course  (create/page.tsx:102, use-course-form.ts:61)
```

## Root cause — a regression from two correct-in-isolation changes colliding

The create→build UI does **two** calls: it `POST`s `/api/v1/courses` (which **forces** `approvalStatus: "approved"` — [EXP-292](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-292/product-no-moderation-gate-at-course-create-time-instructor-courses) product decision, no moderation gate at create), then immediately `POST`s `/api/v1/courses/{id}/submit`. The submit handler now rejects any course that is already `approved`:

* `course-create.handler.ts:106` / `app/api/v1/courses/route.ts:158` → new courses are **born** `approved` ([EXP-292](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-292/product-no-moderation-gate-at-course-create-time-instructor-courses), commit `8b0692c1`).
* `course-submit.handler.ts:31-33` → `if (current.approvalStatus === "approved") return 409` ([EXP-234](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-234/instructor-can-re-submit-an-approved-course-silently-resetting-admin), **PR #732**, commit `1bb553e4`, 2026-06-02) — added to stop instructors re-submitting an *admin-approved* course and silently wiping the approval.

The [EXP-234](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-234/instructor-can-re-submit-an-approved-course-silently-resetting-admin) guard conflates **create-time auto-approved** (never reviewed) with **admin-approved** (reviewed). The system has no field distinguishing them, so the guard also blocks the legitimate brand-new create→submit. The explicit `/submit` call is vestigial — it dates from when a real review queue existed, which [EXP-292](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-292/product-no-moderation-gate-at-course-create-time-instructor-courses) retired.

## Why it shipped green

Tests are per-handler and isolated. `course-workflow.handlers.test.ts:116` asserts the 409 *as correct*. No test exercises **create→submit as one flow**, and the create page's only test (`create/__tests__/page.test.tsx`) uses `renderToStaticMarkup` + a mocked `useCourseForm`, so `handleCreate` is never invoked. The broken flow was invisible to CI.

## Fix (Option A — frontend, zero risk to the [EXP-234](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-234/instructor-can-re-submit-an-approved-course-silently-resetting-admin) invariant)

In `app/(i18n)/_shared/creator/courses/create/page.tsx`:

1. Build the create payload with `publishingStatus: "draft"` for **both** buttons (the course is born a draft; the route still auto-approves it per [EXP-292](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-292/product-no-moderation-gate-at-course-create-time-instructor-courses)).
2. **Remove the vestigial** `/submit` **call** (lines 99–104). "Create & Build Curriculum" creates the course and navigates straight to the curriculum builder; publishing happens later via the existing `course-publish` path.

Backend (the [EXP-234](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-234/instructor-can-re-submit-an-approved-course-silently-resetting-admin) guard) is **untouched** — cannot reopen [EXP-234](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-234/instructor-can-re-submit-an-approved-course-silently-resetting-admin). The course `/submit` route/handler remain (valid API, still tested); the create page was its only frontend caller.

## Tests

* Add a self-documenting **regression-guard** test in `course-workflow.handlers.test.ts`: a course in the exact create-time state (`publishingStatus: "draft"`, `approvalStatus: "approved"`) is rejected 409 by submit — documenting *why* the create→build UI must not call `/submit`.
* Keep the [EXP-234](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-234/instructor-can-re-submit-an-approved-course-silently-resetting-admin) unit test (admin-approved → re-submit → 409) intact so the fix can't silently regress it.

## Related

[EXP-292](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-292/product-no-moderation-gate-at-course-create-time-instructor-courses) (create-time auto-approve product decision — the root tension), [EXP-234](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-234/instructor-can-re-submit-an-approved-course-silently-resetting-admin) (the submit guard, PR #732), [EXP-214](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-214/spinoff-exp-199-course-submit-handler-hardcodes-approvalstatus) (submit→pending). Longer-term: resolving [EXP-292](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-292/product-no-moderation-gate-at-course-create-time-instructor-courses) (retire the submit/pending machinery, or build a real review queue) removes the tension entirely.

*Surfaced via operator-reported functional break + create→submit flow trace, 2026-06-04.*
Creator: loooogix@gmail.com

Title: [ai] No cost controls on OpenAI usage — per-write embeddings + uncached Ask-AI query embeds + cron/worker double-embed → 429 quota burn
Description:
R3 fingerprint: `<!-- agent-fp: f59dc34b0b04 -->`.

## Severity

High — production is hitting OpenAI `429 You exceeded your current quota` on embedding jobs (`embed:post:<uuid>` → `ai.result.job_failed`). There are **no application-level cost controls**; quota burn is unbounded by design.

## Who/what invokes external AI, and why

**Embeddings (**`text-embedding-3-small`**) — fired per content write:**

* `enqueueEmbedOnPublish()` on **every** post create (`community/posts/route.ts:204`), post update (embed-fields touched), course publish/update, event create/update. Job id `embed:<type>:<id>`, BullMQ `attempts: 3` (429 → 2 retries, mild amplification).
* Cron `POST /api/v1/internal/ai/embeddings/sync` (every \~2 min) drains a `embeddingSync` pending table as fallback. `batch.empty` = nothing pending (benign).
* **Ask-AI knowledge retrieval embeds the user's question on every query, uncached** (`ai/knowledge/retrieval.ts`) — likely the single largest waste (same questions re-embedded forever).

**Chat (**`gpt-4o-mini`**):** Ask-AI tutor stream, AI suggest (instructor content gen) — both rate-limited per-user.

## Why the 429 (most → least likely)

1. **Uncached Ask-AI query embeddings** — 1 embed per question, no dedup/cache.
2. **Per-write content embeddings** amplified by the community spam surface ([EXP-299](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-299/bug-post-communitypostsidcomments-has-no-rate-limit-authenticated-spam) no comment rate-limit, [EXP-281](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-281/security-community-post-creation-post-has-no-rate-limit-authenticated) post spam) — each post = 1 embed job.
3. **Cron/worker race double-embed** — the cron reads `embeddingSync` rows as `pending` before the worker flips them to `synced`, so the same entity can be embedded twice in parallel.
4. **No kill-switch** — there is no `EMBEDDINGS_ENABLED` / global AI gate to stop the bleed in an incident.

## Recommended controls (priority order — each can be its own PR)

- [X] **Kill-switch:** `EMBEDDINGS_ENABLED` env gating the 5 `enqueueEmbedOnPublish()` sites (incident lever).
- [X] **Cache Ask-AI question embeddings** (hash(question+locale) → vector, \~1h TTL). Biggest per-query saving.
- [X] **Close the cron/worker race:** mark `embeddingSync` row `synced`/in-flight optimistically before processing (not after), so cron can't double-pick.
- [X] **Quota/backoff hygiene:** on `429`, stop retrying within the same window (don't burn the remaining quota); raise the OpenAI billing limit.
- [X] **Cost observability:** alert on `ai.result.job_failed:embed:*` with `failedReason` containing `429`/`quota`.

These are cost/ops hardening, not a code-correctness regression — sequence behind [EXP-318](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-318/bug-creator-create-and-build-curriculum-fails-with-409-createsubmit). Confirm desired kill-switch default before implementing.

*Surfaced via prod 429 logs + AI invocation trace, 2026-06-04.*
Creator: loooogix@gmail.com

Title: [process] Routine hardening — Functional-Impact gate + filing convergence (stop the flood)
Description:
R3 fingerprint: `<!-- agent-fp: d9d8b35e4ae4 -->`.

Two pillars, both about how the autonomous fix/file routines behave. Source of truth for the filing rules is `.claude/routines/_dedup-protocol.md`; routine prompts are `.claude/routines/0N-*.prompt.md`. **These take effect on the next cloud run only after a** `RemoteTrigger` **push** (the live CCR routines run an inlined copy of each prompt) — editing the `.prompt.md` / protocol is necessary but not sufficient.

---

## Pillar 1 — Functional-Impact / Regression gate (scanner fixes break happy-paths)

Scanner-driven fixes have broken working functionality (e.g. [logi-x/experts#834](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-318/bug-creator-create-and-build-curriculum-fails-with-409-createsubmit): an [logi-x/experts#698](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-234/instructor-can-re-submit-an-approved-course-silently-resetting-admin) guard added in isolation broke the entire creator course-create flow). Root pattern: **scanners only model the attacker's path; they never model the legitimate happy-path that also flows through the code being guarded.** [logi-x/experts#698](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-234/instructor-can-re-submit-an-approved-course-silently-resetting-admin) asked "can an attacker reset approval?" and never "what *else* legitimately submits?" — and the answer (every newly-created course) was invisible because the happy-path lived in a different file and had no end-to-end test. It shipped green because tests are adversarial-only and per-handler.

**Gate (run before writing any scanner-driven fix; verdict written on the PR):**

1. Enumerate every entry path into the symbol being changed (`gitnexus_impact`). For each caller: does this change a currently-working outcome?
2. **Guard red-flag rule:** any fix that adds an early-return / new rejection MUST list every state that previously reached the now-rejected code and succeeded, and confirm none is a legitimate flow.
3. **Pair every adversarial fix with a happy-path test.** An isolated per-handler test is NOT flow coverage.
4. **"Functional Impact" PR section** — "Does this regress existing functionality? Which flows exercise this path? What happy-path test proves they still work?" R5 blocks merge if empty/unverified.

Cheapest 80%: rules 2 + 3 alone catch almost every scanner-fix regression.

## Pillar 2 — Filing convergence (the flood)

Operator can't keep up with auto-filed issues + spinoffs (cadence already cut `9/12` → `9/48`). Root cause: the dedup FP is **file-salted** (`sha1(file:symbol:class)`), so the same `finding_class` across N files (37 `auth-before-try` handlers; the `userBasicInclude` email-PII leak across 4 routes) yields N FPs → N issues. The pipeline optimizes for recall with no mechanism to stop re-deriving known **classes**.

**Mechanism (now** `_dedup-protocol.md` **Rules 5 & 6):**

1. **One issue per CLASS, not per instance.** Every filed issue carries an inline `<!-- agent-class: <finding_class> -->` marker; the first is the anchor, every later same-class instance appends to its checklist instead of filing anew.
2. **A class closes only when its preventive fix lands** (lint rule / shared helper) — ticking instance boxes is not enough; without it the scanner re-derives the class. (Enforced in 03/05.)
3. **Per-run net-new cap (≤3)**; overflow → a single rolling digest (`<!-- agent-digest -->`), never silent truncation (Slack names deferrals).
4. **Severity routing:** High/Critical → top-level/anchor; Medium-no-anchor → digest; promote-on-demand.
5. **Spinoff discipline:** own issue only if disjoint-file AND High/Critical AND no class anchor; else fold into the anchor.

* Full repo search for `process.env.R2_BUCKET_BACKUPS` in all `.ts` files: **zero results outside** `.env.example`
* No backup write command exists in `apps/experts-app/src/lib/storage/commands/` — no `upload-backup.command.ts`, no backup cron handler, no rclone wrapper

**Severity justification:** Bucket-class env (`R2_BUCKET_*`) → bumped from Low (`.env.example`-only declaration) to Medium per audit rules.

**Intent gap:** The R2 backup bucket env was declared alongside a companion `R2_BACKUPS_URL`, signalling a planned backup-to-R2 feature, but no code ever reads this bucket name — the configuration half shipped, the implementation half did not.

**Related prior issues:** [EXP-275](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-275/infra-r2-has-no-automated-backupreplication-objectbucket-deletion-is) (open — "R2 has no automated backup/replication; needs rclone copy script + PITR prefixes + restore script + cron service")

**Verified by:** architecture-reviewer subagent — PASS. Rationale: "The env var was declared alongside a companion R2_BACKUPS_URL, which signals someone intended to wire up actual code to read it — a pure future-placeholder wouldn't need a public URL counterpart. Zero production code reads it, there is no backup command in src/lib/storage/commands/, and [EXP-275](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-275/infra-r2-has-no-automated-backupreplication-objectbucket-deletion-is) is open and explicitly calls out the missing implementation. Title: [security] Event admin-unpublish is reversible by owner (EXP-280 class) — add adminLockedAt parity for events
Description:
R3 fingerprint: `<!-- agent-fp: 208fb1fd41a5 -->`.

Filed manually 2026-06-04 (operator-confirmed "file it, build later"). Deferred — see "Why Backlog" below.

## Summary

`Event` is a near 1:1 structural sibling of `Course` (same `publishingStatus` draft/published/archived, `approvalStatus`, `approvedById/approvedAt`, publicly discoverable in the shared catalog + search), but it has **no** `adminLockedAt` moderation-takedown column, while both `Post` ([EXP-280](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-280/security-community-post-put-allows-owner-to-reverse-admin-unpublish)) and `Course` ([EXP-292](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-292/product-course-moderation-model-instant-publish-reactive-takedown-cert) step 4, PR [logi-x/experts#844](https://linear.app/experts/review/featcourses-risk-based-moderation-state-machine-exp-292-3928505e0e1b) / #846) now do.

The event update path already lets an admin unpublish a live event, with **no durable lock** — so the owner can immediately reverse it. This is the same moderation-bypass shape as [EXP-280](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-280/security-community-post-put-allows-owner-to-reverse-admin-unpublish).

## Trigger scenario (latent — same class as [EXP-280](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-280/security-community-post-put-allows-owner-to-reverse-admin-unpublish))

1. Admin unpublishes a violating live event via `PUT /api/v1/events/{id}` with `{ publishingStatus: "draft" }` (the handler's `!isAdmin` carve-out permits admins).
2. Owner immediately calls `PUT /api/v1/events/{id}` with `{ publishingStatus: "published" }` → event is live again. No `adminLockedAt` lock prevents it.
3. Even an admin *archive* takedown is reversible: owner goes `archived → draft → published` (no lock holds the line).

## Root cause

`apps/experts-app/app/api/v1/events/[id]/route.ts` (PUT) — authorization is owner-or-admin with a non-admin unpublish block, but there is no `adminLockedAt` column to fetch/check, and no admin action stamps one. No event moderation guard exists (contrast `course-moderation.guard.ts` / `isCourseAdminLocked`). There is no `/api/v1/admin/events` surface at all today.

## Scope of fix (the meaningful unit — NOT just a column)

Adding only the column would be dead config (a field nothing reads — the half-migration anti-pattern). Build the full slice, mirroring the course takedown (PR [logi-x/experts#846](https://linear.app/experts/review/featcourses-reactive-admin-takedown-via-courseadminlockedat-exp-292-5c8dedba6a5c)):

- [ ] `Event.adminLockedAt DateTime? @map("admin_locked_at") @db.Timestamptz(6)` + idempotent migration (mirror `20260603000000_post_admin_locked_at`).
- [ ] Stamp the lock on admin takedown of a *live* event (published → draft/archived), clear on admin re-publish — and cover BOTH the unpublish and the archive routes (the exact bypass the gatekeeper caught on the course PR: archive is a second takedown path that must also stamp the lock).
- [ ] Enforce on every event write path (update, delete, publish/unpublish, archive/cancel): non-admin → 403 while locked.
- [ ] Tests: takedown set/clear + non-admin-blocked-while-locked on each write path + a happy-path (unlocked behaves as before).

## Generalization opportunity

With Post + Course + Event all carrying `adminLockedAt`, consider extracting ONE shared `isContentAdminLocked(adminLockedAt, isAdmin)` primitive instead of a third per-domain copy. Worth a small refactor when this lands.

## Why Backlog (deferred, not Todo)

* Events are lower-stakes than courses: ephemeral (a scheduled session), opt-in registration, no platform-vouched credential.
* There is **no admin event-moderation surface today** (no `/admin/events` route), so nothing would deliberately *trigger* a takedown even with the lock present.
* Do this when an admin event-moderation surface is actually prioritized. This is a design+security feature slice, not an R3-auto-fixable instance patch — left in Backlog intentionally.

Related: [EXP-280](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-280/security-community-post-put-allows-owner-to-reverse-admin-unpublish) (community post PUT moderation bypass — the canonical shape, resolved), [EXP-295](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-295/bug-owner-can-delete-admin-locked-post-moderation-lock-not-enforced-in) (community DELETE lock enforcement + cluster), [EXP-292](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-292/product-course-moderation-model-instant-publish-reactive-takedown-cert) (course moderation model — the parity source; PR [logi-x/experts#844](https://linear.app/experts/review/featcourses-risk-based-moderation-state-machine-exp-292-3928505e0e1b) merged, #846 for the course takedown).

*Filed manually by operator session on 2026-06-04.*

Title: [spinoff: EXP-292] course-edit.guard.ts: getCourseLifecycleContext skips adminLockedAt — non-admins can edit curriculum after admin publish-takedown
Description:
`<!-- agent-fp: b0227473cf4ba76bc0a4175dbf64e04bbee5985a -->`
`<!-- spinoff-parent: EXP-292 -->`
`<!-- spinoff-origin: R5 -->`

## Trigger

`apps/experts-app/src/lib/courses/catalog/guards/course-edit.guard.ts` — `getCourseLifecycleContext` fetches only `publishingStatus` and `_count`, never `adminLockedAt`. All curriculum sub-handlers (lesson/module create/update/delete/reorder/bulk-update) route through `assertCourseContentEditable` / `assertCourseStructureEditable` which call this function.

## What this fixes

After an admin publish-based takedown (`published → draft` via `handleCoursePublish`), the course has `adminLockedAt` set and `publishingStatus = "draft"`. Since `canEditContent("draft")` and `canEditStructure("draft")` return `true` for non-admins, the course owner can still add/edit/delete lessons and modules via curriculum handlers, bypassing the moderation lock entirely.

Note: archive-based takedown is NOT affected — `"archived"` status causes `assertCourseStructureEditable` to hard-reject all curriculum mutations without needing the lock check.

## Why this is agent-fixable

Extend `getCourseLifecycleContext` in `course-edit.guard.ts` to add `adminLockedAt: true` to the Prisma select, then call `isCourseAdminLocked(course.adminLockedAt, isAdmin)` at the top of `assertCourseContentEditable` and `assertCourseStructureEditable` before the `canEditContent`/`canEditStructure` checks. Mirror the pattern from `course-archive.handler.ts` (added in [EXP-292](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-292/product-course-moderation-model-instant-publish-reactive-takedown-cert) step 4). Pass `isAdmin` through from the command to both guard functions.

## Out of scope for THIS issue

Changes to the 5 catalog handlers (publish/update/submit/delete/archive) — those are covered by [EXP-292](https://linear.app/experts/issue/EXP-292/product-course-moderation-model-instant-publish-reactive-takedown-cert) step 4 main PR. `course-approve` and `course-reject` are admin-only endpoints and correctly skip the lock check by design.
